treks.name: Everest Base Camp Trek
treks.permit_details: Sagarmatha National Park Permit (NPR 3000), Khumbu Pasang Lhamu Rural Municipality Permit (NPR 2000)
treks.cultural_significance: Sherpa homeland, Buddhist monasteries, UNESCO Sagarmatha National Park
treks.description: The Everest Base Camp Trek is one of the most iconic trekking routes in the world, taking you to the base of the world's highest mountain at 5,364 meters. The journey offers spectacular views of Everest, Lhotse, Nuptse, and Ama Dablam, while passing through traditional Sherpa villages and ancient Buddhist monasteries including Tengboche. The trek includes an early morning hike to Kala Patthar for the best sunrise views of Everest.
treks.short_description: Iconic trek to the base of Mount Everest through Sherpa villages and Buddhist monasteries.

itinerary.place: Kathmandu to Lukla (2840m) trek to Phakding (2610m)
itinerary.description: 30-minute scenic flight to Lukla. Trek 3-4 hours downhill to Phakding along the Dudh Koshi River.
Trek 5-6 hours through pine forests, crossing several high suspension bridges including the famous Hillary Bridge. First views of Everest.
Rest day with optional hike to Everest View Hotel (3880m) for panoramic mountain views. Explore the local market and museum.
Trek 5-6 hours through rhododendron forests with stunning views of Ama Dablam. Visit Tengboche Monastery for evening prayers.
Trek 5-6 hours descending to Deboche, crossing Imja Khola, and climbing to Dingboche.
Hike to Nagarjun Hill (5100m) for views of Lhotse and Makalu. Rest and acclimatization.
Trek 5-6 hours through Thukla Pass, passing memorials for climbers who died on Everest.

itinerary.place: Lobuche to Gorakshep (5170m) to Everest Base Camp (5364m)
itinerary.description: Trek to Gorakshep, then continue to Everest Base Camp (8 hours round trip). Return to Gorakshep overnight.

itinerary.place: Gorakshep to Kala Patthar (5550m) to Pheriche (4280m)
itinerary.description: Early morning hike to Kala Patthar for sunrise over Everest. Descend to Pheriche (7-8 hours).
Long descent through Tengboche and along Dudh Koshi River to Namche (6-7 hours).
Final trekking day descending through Phakding to Lukla (6-7 hours).

treks.name: Gokyo Lakes Trek
treks.permit_details: Sagarmatha National Park Permit (NPR 3000), Khumbu Pasang Lhamu Rural Municipality Permit (NPR 2000)
treks.cultural_significance: Sherpa culture, sacred lakes
treks.description: The Gokyo Lakes Trek offers a stunning alternative to the classic Everest Base Camp route, featuring a series of pristine turquoise glacial lakes and the breathtaking viewpoint of Gokyo Ri at 5,357m. From this summit, you can see four of the world's highest 8,000-meter peaks: Everest, Lhotse, Makalu, and Cho Oyu. The trek also passes the Ngozumpa Glacier, the largest glacier in Nepal.
treks.short_description: Beautiful trek to turquoise glacial lakes with views of four 8000m peaks.

itinerary.place: Kathmandu to Lukla (2840m) trek to Phakding (2610m)
itinerary.description: 30-minute flight to Lukla. Trek 3-4 hours to Phakding.
Trek 5-6 hours through pine forests to Namche Bazaar.
Rest day with optional hike to Everest View Hotel.
Trek 5-6 hours through rhododendron forests to Dole.
Trek 4-5 hours past summer settlements to Machhermo.
Trek 5-6 hours with first views of the turquoise Gokyo Lakes.

itinerary.place: Gokyo Rest Day - Hike to Gokyo Ri (5357m)
itinerary.description: Early morning hike to Gokyo Ri for panoramic views of four 8000m peaks. Return to Gokyo.
Trek 3-4 hours past the lakes to Thagnag.
Long descent back to Namche Bazaar (6-7 hours).
Final trekking day descending to Lukla (6-7 hours).

treks.name: Gokyo Ri Trek
treks.permit_details: Sagarmatha National Park Permit (NPR 3000), Khumbu Pasang Lhamu Rural Municipality Permit (NPR 2000)
treks.cultural_significance: Sherpa culture, sacred lakes
treks.description: The Gokyo Ri Trek focuses on reaching the summit of Gokyo Ri (5,357m), one of the best viewpoints in the Everest region. From this peak, you witness a breathtaking panorama of four 8,000-meter mountains: Everest, Lhotse, Makalu, and Cho Oyu, with the turquoise Gokyo Lakes spread below. The trek follows the same route as the Gokyo Lakes Trek but emphasizes the summit experience.
treks.short_description: Trek focused on summiting Gokyo Ri for unparalleled views of four 8000m peaks.

itinerary.place: Kathmandu to Lukla (2840m) trek to Phakding (2610m)
itinerary.description: Flight to Lukla. Trek to Phakding.

itinerary.place: Gokyo to Gokyo Ri (5357m) and return to Gokyo
itinerary.description: Early morning ascent to Gokyo Ri for sunrise. Return to Gokyo.

treks.name: Gokyo Chola Pass Trek
treks.permit_details: Sagarmatha National Park Permit (NPR 3000), Khumbu Pasang Lhamu Rural Municipality Permit (NPR 2000)
treks.cultural_significance: Sherpa culture, high-altitude mountaineering heritage
treks.description: The Gokyo Chola Pass Trek combines the stunning Gokyo Lakes with a challenging crossing of the Cho La Pass (5,420m) and a visit to Everest Base Camp. This adventurous route offers breathtaking views of the Himalayas, including Everest, Lhotse, and Cho Oyu, while traversing glacial terrain and high mountain passes. It requires glacier crossing experience and excellent acclimatization.
treks.short_description: Adventurous trek combining Gokyo Lakes, Cho La Pass, and Everest Base Camp.

treks.name: Three High Passes Trek
treks.permit_details: Sagarmatha National Park Permit (NPR 3000), Khumbu Pasang Lhamu Rural Municipality Permit (NPR 2000)
treks.cultural_significance: Sherpa culture, high-altitude mountaineering heritage
treks.description: The Three High Passes Trek is the ultimate Everest region adventure, crossing three of the highest mountain passes in the Khumbu: Renjo La (5,360m), Cho La (5,420m), and Kongma La (5,535m). This challenging circuit combines the classic Everest Base Camp trek with the stunning Gokyo Lakes, offering unparalleled Himalayan views, remote valleys, and a true sense of high-altitude achievement. It requires excellent fitness, proper acclimatization, and experience with glacier terrain.
treks.short_description: The ultimate Everest challenge crossing three high passes with spectacular mountain views.

treks.name: Everest View Trek
treks.permit_details: Sagarmatha National Park Permit (NPR 3000), Khumbu Pasang Lhamu Rural Municipality Permit (NPR 2000)
treks.cultural_significance: Sherpa culture, UNESCO Sagarmatha National Park
treks.description: The Everest View Trek is a short and accessible introduction to the Everest region, perfect for those with limited time or who want to experience the Himalayas without the extreme altitudes of Everest Base Camp. The highlight is a visit to the iconic Hotel Everest View at 3,880m, the world's highest luxury hotel, offering breathtaking panoramic views of Mount Everest, Lhotse, and Ama Dablam. The trek includes visits to Namche Bazaar and traditional Sherpa villages.
treks.short_description: Short trek to the world's highest hotel with stunning Everest panoramas.

itinerary.place: Kathmandu to Lukla (2840m) trek to Phakding (2610m)
itinerary.description: 30-minute flight to Lukla. Trek 3-4 hours to Phakding.

treks.name: Jiri to Everest Base Camp Trek
treks.permit_details: Sagarmatha National Park Permit (NPR 3000), TIMS Card, Local Municipality Permits
treks.cultural_significance: Historic trekking route, Sherpa culture, traditional villages
treks.description: The Jiri to Everest Base Camp Trek is the original, classic approach to Everest taken by the early expeditions, including Sir Edmund Hillary and Tenzing Norgay in 1953. This longer and more challenging route starts in the mid-hills of Jiri, offering deeper cultural immersion through traditional villages of the Solu and Khumbu regions. It provides a more gradual acclimatization and a truly rewarding journey to the foot of the world's highest mountain.
treks.short_description: The classic, original approach to Everest Base Camp from Jiri following Hillary's footsteps.

itinerary.description: Drive from Kathmandu to Jiri (7-8 hours).

treks.name: Pikey Peak Trek
treks.permit_details: TIMS Card, Local Municipality Permits
treks.cultural_significance: Lower Sherpa region, Buddhist heritage, traditional villages
treks.description: The Pikey Peak Trek offers one of the best panoramic views of the Himalayas, including Mount Everest, Lhotse, Makalu, and Kanchenjunga. Sir Edmund Hillary himself reportedly described the Everest view from Pikey Peak as the finest he had ever seen. This relatively easy trek in the lower Solu region is perfect for those wanting spectacular mountain views without the extreme altitude or crowds of the main Everest trails. The trek passes through beautiful rhododendron forests and authentic Sherpa villages.
treks.short_description: Easy trek to Pikey Peak with one of the best sunrise views of Everest and the Himalayas.

itinerary.description: Drive from Kathmandu to Dhap (6-7 hours).

Pikey Peak Base Camp to Pikey Peak Summit (4065m) to Loting (3170m)

treks.name: Numbur Cheese Circuit Trek
treks.permit_details: TIMS Card, Local Municipality Permits
treks.cultural_significance: Named after famous cheese factories, indigenous Thami, Tamang, and Sherpa communities
treks.description: The Numbur Cheese Circuit Trek is a unique cultural trek in the Solu region, named after the traditional cheese factories established with Swiss assistance in the 1950s. The route passes through pristine rhododendron forests, visits the sacred Panch Pokhari lakes, crosses the challenging Gyajo La Pass (4,880m), and offers spectacular views of Numbur Himal and the Everest range. This community-based trek provides authentic experiences in Thami, Tamang, and Sherpa villages.
treks.short_description: Unique cultural trek through traditional cheese-making villages with sacred lakes and mountain views.

itinerary.description: Drive from Kathmandu to Jiri (7-8 hours).

treks.name: Khumbu Circuit Trek
treks.permit_details: Sagarmatha National Park Permit (NPR 3000), Khumbu Pasang Lhamu Rural Municipality Permit (NPR 2000)
treks.cultural_significance: Sherpa culture, Buddhist monasteries, UNESCO Sagarmatha National Park
treks.description: The Khumbu Circuit Trek is a comprehensive exploration of the entire Everest region, combining the classic Everest Base Camp route with the Gokyo Valley and crossing the challenging Cho La Pass. This circuit offers the most complete experience of the Khumbu, visiting all major highlights including Namche Bazaar, Tengboche Monastery, Dingboche, Lobuche, Everest Base Camp, Kala Patthar, and the turquoise Gokyo Lakes. It provides spectacular mountain views and deep cultural immersion.
treks.short_description: Complete circuit of the Khumbu region combining Everest Base Camp and Gokyo Lakes.

treks.description: The Chola Pass EBC Trek is an adventurous route that combines the classic Everest Base Camp trek with the stunning Gokyo Valley, connected by the challenging Cho La Pass (5,420m). This trek offers diverse scenery including glacial lakes, high mountain passes, and the iconic base camp of Everest. It requires good physical condition and proper acclimatization for the high-altitude pass crossing.
treks.short_description: Challenging trek combining Everest Base Camp with Gokyo Lakes via Cho La Pass.

treks.name: Ama Dablam Base Camp Trek
treks.permit_details: Sagarmatha National Park Permit (NPR 3000), Khumbu Pasang Lhamu Rural Municipality Permit (NPR 2000)
treks.cultural_significance: Sherpa culture, sacred mountain, Buddhist monasteries
treks.description: The Ama Dablam Base Camp Trek offers close-up views of one of the world's most beautiful mountains, known as the 'Matterhorn of the Himalayas' for its stunning pyramidal shape. This trek branches off from the classic Everest trail at Pangboche and provides a less crowded alternative with spectacular mountain scenery. The route passes through traditional Sherpa villages, rhododendron forests, and offers incredible views of Ama Dablam, Everest, Lhotse, and Makalu.
treks.short_description: Trek to the base of the stunning Ama Dablam, the 'Matterhorn of the Himalayas'.

treks.name: Renjo La Pass Trek
treks.permit_details: Sagarmatha National Park Permit (NPR 3000), Khumbu Pasang Lhamu Rural Municipality Permit (NPR 2000)
treks.cultural_significance: Sherpa culture, ancient trading route to Tibet
treks.description: The Renjo La Pass Trek is the westernmost of the three high passes in the Everest region and offers arguably the most spectacular views from its summit. Crossing at 5,360m, the pass provides breathtaking panoramic views of Everest, Lhotse, Makalu, and Cho Oyu, with the turquoise Gokyo Lakes visible far below. This less-crowded route passes through the beautiful Thame Valley, the home village of many famous Sherpa climbers, before reaching the pristine Gokyo Lakes.
treks.short_description: High pass trek with spectacular views of Everest and the Gokyo Lakes from Renjo La.

treks.name: Kongma La Pass Trek
treks.permit_details: Sagarmatha National Park Permit (NPR 3000), Khumbu Pasang Lhamu Rural Municipality Permit (NPR 2000)
treks.cultural_significance: Sherpa mountaineering culture, high-altitude wilderness
treks.description: The Kongma La Pass Trek crosses the highest of the three passes in the Everest region at 5,535m. This extremely challenging route offers spectacular views of Makalu, Lhotse, and the surrounding peaks, connecting the Chukhung Valley with the Khumbu Valley. The pass requires excellent physical fitness, proper acclimatization, and experience with high-altitude terrain. It is often combined with Everest Base Camp and the other passes for the complete Three Passes experience.
treks.short_description: The highest pass trek in the Everest region with extreme altitude challenges and spectacular views.

treks.name: Phaplu Pikey Peak Trek
treks.permit_details: TIMS Card, Local Municipality Permits
treks.cultural_significance: Lower Sherpa region, Buddhist heritage, traditional villages
treks.description: The Phaplu Pikey Peak Trek offers the same spectacular sunrise views of Everest and the Himalayas as the classic Pikey Peak Trek, but with the convenience of starting and ending at the Phaplu airstrip. This variation eliminates the long drive from Kathmandu to Dhap, making it ideal for those with limited time who still want to experience one of the finest panoramic views of the Everest region. Sir Edmund Hillary reportedly described the Everest view from Pikey Peak as the finest he had ever seen.
treks.short_description: Convenient trek to Pikey Peak starting from Phaplu with spectacular Everest sunrise views.

treks.name: Sagarmatha Buffer Zone Trek
treks.permit_details: Sagarmatha National Park Permit (NPR 3000), TIMS Card
treks.cultural_significance: Sherpa culture, buffer zone communities, conservation areas
treks.description: The Sagarmatha Buffer Zone Trek explores the beautiful forested buffer zone of Sagarmatha National Park, offering a quieter alternative to the main Everest trails. This trek passes through authentic Sherpa villages, lush forests of rhododendron and pine, and offers glimpses of Himalayan wildlife including the elusive red panda and Himalayan thar. At lower altitudes than the classic EBC route, it provides excellent opportunities for nature observation and cultural immersion without the challenges of high-altitude trekking.
treks.short_description: Lower-altitude trek through the forested buffer zone of Everest National Park with wildlife and Sherpa villages.

treks.name: Thame Valley Trek
treks.permit_details: Sagarmatha National Park Permit (NPR 3000), Khumbu Pasang Lhamu Rural Municipality Permit (NPR 2000)
treks.cultural_significance: Home village of famous Sherpa climbers including Tenzing Norgay's family, ancient trading route to Tibet
treks.description: The Thame Valley Trek explores the beautiful Bhote Kosi Valley west of Namche Bazaar, following an ancient trading route to Tibet. This culturally rich trek leads to Thame, the home village of many famous Sherpa climbers including Tenzing Norgay's family and Apa Sherpa. The valley offers spectacular views of Teng Kangpoche and Kwangde peaks, traditional Sherpa villages, ancient monasteries, and a glimpse into traditional mountain life away from the main Everest crowds. The annual Mani Rimdu festival celebration in Thame is a highlight for those timing their visit.
treks.short_description: Cultural trek through the historic Thame Valley, home of famous Sherpa climbers.

itinerary.description: Flight to Lukla. Trek to Namche Bazaar.

treks.name: Khumjung Valley Trek
treks.permit_details: Sagarmatha National Park Permit (NPR 3000), Khumbu Pasang Lhamu Rural Municipality Permit (NPR 2000)
treks.cultural_significance: Largest Sherpa village, Sir Edmund Hillary's school, Buddhist monastery
treks.description: The Khumjung Valley Trek explores the beautiful valley behind Namche Bazaar, home to the largest Sherpa villages in the Khumbu region. Khumjung and neighboring Khunde offer a fascinating glimpse into traditional Sherpa life, with their ancient monasteries, traditional stone houses, and the famous Hillary School established by Sir Edmund Hillary in 1961. The Khumjung Monastery is renowned for housing what is believed to be a yeti scalp. This easy trek provides spectacular views of Ama Dablam, Thamserku, and Kangtega without the extreme altitudes of the main Everest trails.
treks.short_description: Easy cultural trek through the largest Sherpa villages with monasteries and yeti lore.

treks.name: Hinku Valley Trek
treks.permit_details: Sagarmatha National Park Permit (NPR 3000), Makalu Barun National Park Permit, TIMS Card
treks.cultural_significance: Remote Sherpa settlements, untouched wilderness
treks.description: The Hinku Valley Trek is a remote and challenging adventure through one of the most pristine valleys in the Everest region. Located east of the main Khumbu trails, this valley offers untouched wilderness, dense forests, and spectacular views of Mera Peak and other Himalayan giants. The trek often serves as the approach route for Mera Peak climbing expeditions but can be done as a standalone wilderness trek. It requires full camping arrangements and experience with remote, high-altitude trekking.
treks.short_description: Remote wilderness trek through the pristine Hinku Valley with spectacular mountain views.

treks.name: Hongu Valley Trek
treks.permit_details: Sagarmatha National Park Permit, Makalu Barun National Park Permit, Restricted Area Permit
treks.cultural_significance: Pristine wilderness, no permanent settlements
treks.description: The Hongu Valley Trek is one of the most remote and challenging treks in the Everest region, accessing a pristine glacial valley that sees very few visitors. The trek crosses the technical Amphu Labtsa Pass (5,800m), connecting the Khumbu region with the remote Hongu Valley, which is surrounded by unclimbed peaks and pristine glaciers. This expedition-style trek requires full camping support, technical mountaineering skills for the pass crossing, and extensive high-altitude experience. It offers true wilderness exploration for the most adventurous trekkers.
treks.short_description: Extreme expedition trek through remote Hongu Valley crossing technical Amphu Labtsa Pass.

treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Diverse ethnic groups including Gurung, Manangi, Thakali; Hindu and Buddhist pilgrimage sites; ancient trading route to Tibet
treks.description: The Annapurna Circuit is one of the world's great treks, circling the entire Annapurna massif and crossing the Thorong La Pass at 5,416 meters. This classic journey offers incredible diversity, from subtropical forests and terraced farmlands to high-altitude deserts and dramatic gorges. Trekkers pass through villages of diverse ethnic groups, experience ancient Buddhist and Hindu cultures, and witness panoramic views of some of the world's highest peaks including Annapurna, Dhaulagiri, and Manaslu.
treks.short_description: World-class circuit trek circling the Annapurna massif with diverse landscapes and cultural immersion.

itinerary.place: Thorong Phedi to Muktinath (3800m) via Thorong La Pass (5416m)
itinerary.description: Early start to cross the highest pass. Long descent to Muktinath.
Visit sacred Muktinath Temple. Trek through Kagbeni to Jomsom.
Easy trek to the apple capital of Nepal. Explore the charming village.
Long descent through dramatic gorges to Tatopani. Relax in hot springs.
Drive or continue trekking to Nayapul and then to Pokhara.

treks.name: Annapurna Base Camp Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Gurung and Magar cultural heritage, traditional mountain villages
treks.description: The Annapurna Base Camp Trek takes you into the heart of the Annapurna Sanctuary, a natural amphitheater surrounded by towering peaks including Annapurna I, Machhapuchhre (Fishtail), and Hiunchuli. This classic trek passes through diverse landscapes from rhododendron forests to alpine meadows, with opportunities to experience traditional Gurung and Magar village life. The journey culminates at Annapurna Base Camp at 4,130 meters, offering breathtaking 360-degree mountain views.
treks.short_description: Classic trek into the Annapurna Sanctuary surrounded by towering Himalayan peaks.

itinerary.description: Drive from Pokhara to Nayapul. Trek along the Modi Khola to Tikhedhungga.
Climb Ulleri's 3,300 stone steps. Continue through rhododendron forests to Ghorepani.

itinerary.place: Ghorepani to Tadapani (2630m) via Poon Hill (3210m)
itinerary.description: Early morning hike to Poon Hill for sunrise. Trek through forests to Tadapani.
Descend through forests to the Kimrong Khola, then climb to Chhomrong village.
Descend to the Chhomrong Khola, then climb through bamboo and rhododendron forests.
Trek through the narrowing valley to Deurali.

itinerary.place: Deurali to Annapurna Base Camp (4130m) via Machhapuchhre Base Camp (3700m)
itinerary.description: Enter the Annapurna Sanctuary. Reach ABC surrounded by peaks.
Descend through the sanctuary to Bamboo.
Trek to Jhinu Danda. Relax in natural hot springs by the river.

itinerary.place: Jhinu Danda to Nayapul to Pokhara
itinerary.description: Final trekking day to Nayapul. Drive back to Pokhara.

treks.name: Ghorepani Poon Hill Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Gurung and Magar villages, traditional mountain lifestyle
treks.description: The Ghorepani Poon Hill Trek is Nepal's most popular short trek, famous for its spectacular sunrise view from Poon Hill at 3,210 meters. From this viewpoint, trekkers are rewarded with an unobstructed 360-degree panorama of more than 20 Himalayan peaks, including Dhaulagiri, Annapurna, and Machhapuchhre. The trail passes through beautiful rhododendron forests, traditional Gurung villages, and offers a perfect introduction to Himalayan trekking with moderate daily walks and excellent teahouse accommodations.
treks.short_description: Nepal's most popular short trek with stunning sunrise panorama of the Annapurna and Dhaulagiri ranges.

itinerary.description: Drive to Nayapul. Trek along river to Tikhedhungga.
Climb Ulleri's stone steps. Trek through rhododendron forests.

itinerary.place: Ghorepani to Tadapani (2630m) via Poon Hill (3210m)
itinerary.description: Pre-dawn hike to Poon Hill for sunrise. Trek to Tadapani.
Descend to large Gurung village of Ghandruk. Explore village museum.
Trek to Nayapul. Drive back to Pokhara.

treks.name: Ghorepani Ghandruk Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Traditional Gurung village with cultural museum, Gurkha heritage
treks.description: The Ghorepani Ghandruk Trek is a classic loop combining the famous Poon Hill sunrise viewpoint with an overnight stay in Ghandruk, one of Nepal's largest and most beautiful Gurung villages. This trek offers spectacular mountain panoramas, rhododendron forests, and deep cultural immersion in traditional Gurung communities. The village of Ghandruk features a fascinating cultural museum and provides stunning close-up views of Annapurna South and Machhapuchhre.
treks.short_description: Classic loop trek combining Poon Hill sunrise with cultural exploration of Ghandruk village.

itinerary.description: Drive to Nayapul. Trek to Tikhedhungga.
Climb Ulleri steps. Trek through forests to Ghorepani.

itinerary.place: Ghorepani to Tadapani (2630m) via Poon Hill (3210m)
itinerary.description: Sunrise at Poon Hill. Trek to Tadapani.
Descend to Ghandruk. Explore village and museum.
Rest day. Hike to surrounding viewpoints or relax in village.
Trek to Nayapul. Drive back to Pokhara.

treks.name: Mardi Himal Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Gurung villages, relatively new trekking route opened in 2014
treks.description: The Mardi Himal Trek is a relatively new and increasingly popular route offering spectacular close-up views of Machhapuchhre (Fishtail) and the Annapurna range. The trek features a stunning open ridge walk above the treeline, providing expansive mountain panoramas that rival more crowded routes. Passing through beautiful rhododendron forests and traditional Gurung villages, this trek offers a perfect balance of natural beauty and cultural experience with fewer crowds than the classic Annapurna trails.
treks.short_description: Newer ridge trek with spectacular close-up views of Machhapuchhre and the Annapurna range.

itinerary.description: Drive to Kande. Trek to Pothana through forests.
Continue ascending. First views of Machhapuchhre.
Trek above treeline. Spectacular mountain views.

itinerary.place: High Camp to Mardi Himal Base Camp (4500m) and return
itinerary.description: Early morning trek to base camp. Return to High Camp.
Long descent through forests to Siding Village.

treks.name: Khopra Danda Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Community-based tourism, Gurung and Magar villages
treks.description: The Khopra Danda Trek is a community-based trekking route offering stunning views of the Annapurna and Dhaulagiri ranges from a spectacular ridge at 3,660 meters. This less-crowded alternative to the classic Annapurna trails passes through traditional Gurung and Magar villages and features community-managed lodges that ensure tourism benefits reach local households. An optional side trip to the sacred Khayer Lake at 4,660 meters adds adventure for those seeking higher altitudes.
treks.short_description: Community-based ridge trek with spectacular mountain views and authentic village experiences.

itinerary.description: Drive to Ghandruk. Explore the village.
Trek through rhododendron forests to Tadapani.

itinerary.place: Dobato to Muldai Peak (3780m) to Chistabang (2975m)
itinerary.description: Early morning hike to Muldai Peak for sunrise. Trek to Chistabang.
Trek to Khopra Ridge. Spectacular mountain views.
Rest day. Optional hike to Khayer Lake (4660m) or relax at ridge.
Descend through forests to Swanta village.

treks.name: Khopra Ridge Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Community-based tourism, Gurung villages, sacred Khayer Lake
treks.description: The Khopra Ridge Trek is a community-managed route focusing on the spectacular Khopra Ridge and the sacred Khayer Lake at 4,660 meters. This trek offers stunning panoramic views of the Dhaulagiri and Annapurna ranges, with the added highlight of sunrise from Muldai Peak, which is significantly closer to the mountains than the crowded Poon Hill. The trail supports local communities through its network of community lodges, providing an authentic and sustainable trekking experience.
treks.short_description: Community-based ridge trek featuring Khayer Lake and spectacular Dhaulagiri-Annapurna panoramas.

itinerary.description: Drive to Ghandruk. Explore the Gurung village.

itinerary.place: Dobato to Muldai Peak (3780m) to Chistabang (2975m)
itinerary.description: Sunrise at Muldai Peak. Trek to Chistabang.
Trek to Khopra Ridge. Mountain views.

itinerary.place: Khopra Ridge to Khayer Lake (4660m) and return
itinerary.description: Side trip to sacred Khayer Lake. Return to Khopra.

treks.name: Mohare Danda Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Community-based sustainable tourism, Magar villages
treks.description: The Mohare Danda Trek is an eco-trek that exclusively uses community-owned lodges, ensuring direct benefits reach local Magar villages. From the summit at 3,320 meters, trekkers are rewarded with spectacular 360-degree views of the Annapurna and Dhaulagiri ranges, including Annapurna I, Dhaulagiri, Nilgiri, and Machhapuchhre. This socially responsible trek offers authentic cultural experiences, beautiful rhododendron forests, and a peaceful alternative to the more crowded Annapurna trails.
treks.short_description: Eco-trek with panoramic mountain views and community-owned lodges benefiting local Magar villages.

itinerary.description: Drive to Galeshwor. Trek to Purnagaun.
Trek to ridge top. Spectacular mountain views.

treks.name: Poon Hill Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Gurung and Magar villages
treks.description: The Poon Hill Trek is a short and accessible introduction to Himalayan trekking, focusing on the famous sunrise viewpoint at Poon Hill (3,210m). From this vantage point, trekkers witness one of the most spectacular mountain panoramas in Nepal, with over 20 Himalayan peaks including Dhaulagiri, Annapurna, and Machhapuchhre. The trail features the famous Ulleri stone steps, beautiful rhododendron forests, and traditional Gurung villages, making it perfect for those with limited time.
treks.short_description: Short trek to the famous Poon Hill sunrise viewpoint with spectacular mountain panoramas.

itinerary.description: Drive to Nayapul. Trek to Tikhedhungga.
Climb Ulleri stone steps. Trek through forests to Ghorepani.

itinerary.place: Ghorepani to Poon Hill (3210m) to Ulleri (2070m)
itinerary.description: Pre-dawn hike to Poon Hill for sunrise. Descend to Ulleri.
Trek to Nayapul. Drive back to Pokhara.

treks.name: Jomsom Muktinath Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Sacred Hindu and Buddhist pilgrimage site, ancient trading village of Kagbeni, Thakali culture
treks.description: The Jomsom Muktinath Trek follows the Kali Gandaki River through the world's deepest gorge to the sacred temple of Muktinath, revered by both Hindus and Buddhists. This cultural and pilgrimage trek passes through unique desert-like landscapes, the apple capital of Marpha, and the ancient village of Kagbeni at the entrance to Upper Mustang. The combination of spiritual significance, dramatic scenery, and rich Thakali culture makes this a uniquely rewarding trek.
treks.short_description: Pilgrimage trek through the world's deepest gorge to the sacred Muktinath Temple.

itinerary.description: Morning flight to Jomsom. Explore the town.
Trek through Kali Gandaki gorge to ancient Kagbeni village.
Trek to sacred Muktinath Temple. Visit temple and 108 water taps.
Explore temple complex and surrounding area.
Descend to Marpha, famous for apple orchards and brandy.

treks.name: Upper Mustang Trek
treks.permit_details: Upper Mustang Restricted Area Permit USD 500 for 10 days, Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Ancient Kingdom of Lo, preserved Tibetan Buddhist culture, forbidden kingdom opened in 1992
treks.description: The Upper Mustang Trek takes you to the former forbidden Kingdom of Lo, a restricted area that opened to trekkers only in 1992. This trans-Himalayan region lies in the rain shadow of the Annapurna massif, creating a dramatic desert landscape of eroded canyons, red cliffs, and whitewashed villages that feels more like Tibet than Nepal. The walled capital of Lo Manthang, with its ancient monasteries and royal palace, offers a rare glimpse into a living Tibetan Buddhist culture preserved for centuries.
treks.short_description: Journey to the ancient forbidden Kingdom of Lo with preserved Tibetan culture and dramatic desert landscapes.

itinerary.description: Trek to Kagbeni, entrance to Upper Mustang.
Enter Upper Mustang. Dramatic canyon scenery.
Cross several ridges with mountain views.
Explore city, monasteries, and royal palace.

treks.name: Nar Phu Valley Trek
treks.permit_details: Nar Phu Restricted Area Permit USD 100/week (Sept-Nov), USD 75/week (Dec-Aug), Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Isolated Tibetan Buddhist villages with medieval culture, ancient monasteries, restricted area opened recently
treks.description: The Nar Phu Valley Trek is a journey into one of Nepal's most remote and culturally preserved regions, opened to trekkers only in 2002. The medieval Tibetan villages of Nar and Phu, with their ancient monasteries, chortens, and mani walls, offer a rare glimpse into a way of life unchanged for centuries. This challenging trek follows dramatic canyons, crosses the high Kang La Pass (5,306m), and provides spectacular mountain views before rejoining the Annapurna Circuit.
treks.short_description: Remote restricted area trek to medieval Tibetan villages with ancient monasteries and dramatic canyons.

itinerary.description: Enter Nar Phu restricted area. Trek through forests.
Trek through dramatic canyons to remote Phu village.
Explore Phu village and Tashi Lhakhang Monastery.
Trek to the larger Nar village.
Explore Nar and its monasteries. Acclimatization.

itinerary.place: Nar to Ngawal (3660m) via Kang La Pass (5306m)
itinerary.description: Early start to cross Kang La Pass. Descent to Ngawal.
Trek to Manang, rejoining Annapurna Circuit.

treks.name: Tilicho Lake Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Hindu pilgrimage site mentioned in Ramayana, ancient trade routes
treks.description: The Tilicho Lake Trek takes you to one of the world's highest lakes at 4,919 meters, a stunning turquoise body of water nestled beneath the massive peaks of the Annapurna and Nilgiri ranges. Mentioned in the ancient Hindu epic Ramayana, the lake holds deep spiritual significance. This challenging trek can be done as a side trip from the Annapurna Circuit or as a standalone journey, offering spectacular mountain scenery, remote landscapes, and a true high-altitude adventure.
treks.short_description: Challenging trek to one of the world's highest lakes, sacred in Hindu mythology.

itinerary.place: Tilicho Base Camp to Tilicho Lake (4920m) and return
itinerary.description: Early morning trek to sacred lake. Return to base camp.

itinerary.place: Tilicho Base Camp to Yak Kharka (4050m)
Thorong Phedi to Muktinath (3800m) via Thorong La (5416m)
itinerary.description: Cross Thorong La. Descend to Muktinath.
Trek to Jomsom. Flight to Pokhara.

treks.name: Dhampus Sarangkot Trek
treks.permit_details: No permits required for this short trek
treks.cultural_significance: Gurung villages, traditional mountain lifestyle
treks.description: The Dhampus Sarangkot Trek is a short and easy circuit from Pokhara, perfect for those with limited time who still want to experience Himalayan mountain views and Gurung village culture. The trek includes an overnight stay in Dhampus village and an early morning hike to Sarangkot for spectacular sunrise views over the Annapurna range, with Fewa Lake glistening below. This gentle introduction to Nepali trekking offers cultural experiences and mountain panoramas without the need for extensive preparation.
treks.short_description: Short and easy circuit from Pokhara with sunrise mountain views and Gurung village culture.

itinerary.description: Drive to Phedi. Trek to Dhampus village.

itinerary.place: Sarangkot Sunrise and return to Pokhara
itinerary.description: Early morning sunrise views. Descend to Pokhara.

treks.name: Ghandruk Loop Trek
treks.permit_details: No permits required for this short trek
treks.cultural_significance: Largest Gurung village in Nepal, Gurkha heritage, cultural museum
treks.description: The Ghandruk Loop Trek is a short cultural circuit focusing on Ghandruk, one of Nepal's largest and most beautiful Gurung villages. This easy trek offers spectacular close-up views of Annapurna South and Machhapuchhre, while providing deep immersion in traditional Gurung culture. The village features a fascinating cultural museum, traditional stone houses, and warm homestay hospitality, making it perfect for those wanting authentic cultural experiences without long trekking days.
treks.short_description: Short cultural loop focusing on the beautiful Gurung village of Ghandruk with mountain views.

itinerary.description: Drive to Nayapul. Trek to Ghandruk.
Explore village, visit museum, short hikes.
Descend to Nayapul. Drive to Pokhara.

treks.name: Panchase Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000
treks.cultural_significance: Sacred site, mixed ethnic communities including Gurung and Magar
treks.description: The Panchase Trek is a short and accessible trek from Pokhara, known for its rich biodiversity and stunning mountain views. Designated as an Important Bird Area, the Panchase region is home to numerous bird species and offers beautiful rhododendron forests. From Panchase Hill at 2,500 meters, trekkers enjoy panoramic views of the Annapurna, Dhaulagiri, and Machhapuchhre ranges, with Fewa Lake visible far below. This trek is perfect for nature lovers with limited time.
treks.short_description: Short biodiversity trek from Pokhara with excellent bird watching and mountain panoramas.

itinerary.description: Drive to Bhadaure. Trek to Panchase Bhanjyang.

itinerary.place: Panchase Bhanjyang to Panchase Hill (2500m) to Panchase Village
itinerary.description: Sunrise at Panchase Hill. Descend to village.

treks.name: Sikles Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Largest Gurung village in Nepal, community-based tourism model
treks.description: The Sikles Trek is a short and easy cultural trek visiting one of the largest and most beautiful Gurung villages in Nepal. Sikles is a model of community-based tourism, offering authentic homestay experiences where visitors stay with local families and participate in daily village life. The trek provides stunning views of the Annapurna and Dhaulagiri ranges, passes through terraced farmlands and forests, and offers deep cultural immersion in a traditional mountain community.
treks.short_description: Short cultural trek to one of Nepal's largest Gurung villages with authentic homestay experiences.

itinerary.description: Drive to Kalikasthan. Trek to Sikles.
Explore Sikles village, culture, and surrounding area.
Trek to roadhead. Drive to Pokhara.

treks.name: Royal Trek
treks.permit_details: No permits required
treks.cultural_significance: Named after Prince Charles' trek in 1980s, Gurung culture
treks.description: The Royal Trek earned its name from Prince Charles' trek through this area in the 1980s. This short and easy trek offers beautiful views of the Annapurna and Dhaulagiri ranges while passing through picturesque Gurung villages and terraced farmlands. With gentle walking days and excellent teahouse accommodations, it provides a peaceful introduction to Nepali village life and Himalayan scenery, perfect for those with limited time or seeking a relaxed trekking experience.
treks.short_description: Easy, scenic trek through Gurung villages named after Prince Charles' 1980s visit.

itinerary.description: Drive to Kalikasthan. Trek to settlement.
Trek through villages with mountain views.
Trek to roadhead. Drive to Pokhara.

treks.name: Lwang Ghalel Homestay Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000
treks.cultural_significance: Gurung village with award-winning homestay program, organic coffee production
treks.description: The Lwang Ghalel Homestay Trek is a short cultural trek to a beautiful Gurung village famous for its award-winning community homestay program and organic coffee production. This immersive experience allows trekkers to stay with local families, participate in daily activities, learn about traditional Gurung culture, and taste locally grown organic coffee. With stunning views of the Annapurna range and warm village hospitality, this trek offers authentic cultural immersion without demanding trekking days.
treks.short_description: Cultural homestay trek to a Gurung village famous for organic coffee and community tourism.

itinerary.description: Drive to Lwang. Welcome by host family.
Explore village, coffee farms, cultural activities.
Farewell to host family. Drive to Pokhara.

treks.name: Ghalegaun Homestay Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Model Gurung village with museum, award-winning homestay program
treks.description: Ghalegaun is a model Gurung village in the Annapurna region, renowned for its award-winning community homestay program and cultural museum. This short trek offers deep immersion into traditional Gurung life, with opportunities to learn about local customs, taste traditional cuisine, and participate in village activities. The village museum displays fascinating artifacts of Gurung culture, while clear days reveal stunning views of the Annapurna and Manaslu ranges.
treks.short_description: Cultural homestay trek to a model Gurung village with a museum and mountain views.

itinerary.description: Trek to Ghalegaun. Welcome by host family.
Visit museum, cultural activities, village walks.
Trek to Besisahar. Drive to Pokhara.

treks.name: Kori Danda Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Gurung villages, off-the-beaten-path experiences
treks.description: The Kori Danda Trek is a lesser-known ridge trek in the Lamjung district offering spectacular views of Manaslu, Annapurna II, and Lamjung Himal. This peaceful alternative to more crowded routes passes through traditional Gurung villages and beautiful rhododendron forests, providing authentic cultural experiences and pristine mountain scenery. The summit at 3,500 meters rewards trekkers with panoramic Himalayan views and a true sense of discovery away from the main tourist trails.
treks.short_description: Lesser-known ridge trek with Manaslu and Annapurna views through traditional Gurung villages.

itinerary.description: Trek through forests to Gurung village.

itinerary.place: Pasgaun to Kori Danda (3500m) and return
itinerary.description: Summit for mountain views. Return to Pasgaun.
Trek to Besisahar. Drive to Pokhara.

treks.name: Mardi Himal Base Camp Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Gurung villages, relatively new route opened in 2014
treks.description: The Mardi Himal Base Camp Trek focuses on reaching the base camp of Mardi Himal at 4,500 meters, offering some of the closest views of Machhapuchhre (Fishtail) in the entire Annapurna region. This relatively new route features a spectacular open ridge walk above the treeline, providing expansive mountain panoramas without the crowds of more established treks. The journey passes through beautiful rhododendron forests and offers a perfect balance of adventure and accessibility.
treks.short_description: Shorter route to Mardi Himal Base Camp with spectacular close-up views of Machhapuchhre.

itinerary.description: Drive to Kande. Trek to Pothana.

itinerary.place: High Camp to Mardi Himal Base Camp (4500m) and return
itinerary.description: Early morning trek to base camp. Return to High Camp.

treks.name: Australian Camp Trek
treks.permit_details: No permits required for this short trek
treks.cultural_significance: Named after Australian aid project, Gurung villages
treks.description: The Australian Camp Trek is a short and easy trek from Pokhara, named after an Australian aid project that developed the area. This gentle trek offers stunning sunrise views over the Annapurna range, including Annapurna South, Hiunchuli, and Machhapuchhre, with the beautiful Dhampus village along the way. Perfect for those with limited time or as a warm-up for longer treks, this route provides a taste of Himalayan trekking with minimal effort.
treks.short_description: Short and easy trek from Pokhara with beautiful sunrise views over the Annapurna range.

itinerary.description: Drive to Phedi. Trek to Dhampus.
Trek to Australian Camp via forest trails.
Sunrise views. Descend to roadhead. Drive to Pokhara.

treks.name: Sarangkot Trek
treks.permit_details: No permits required
treks.cultural_significance: Popular viewpoint, Gurung settlements
treks.description: The Sarangkot Trek is a short and easy hike to one of Nepal's most famous viewpoints, offering spectacular sunrise views over the Annapurna and Dhaulagiri ranges with Fewa Lake and Pokhara city spread out below. This popular destination can be reached by a gentle trek or a short drive, making it accessible to all. The viewpoint is also a launching point for paragliding, adding adventure to the stunning scenery.
treks.short_description: Short sunrise trek to Pokhara's famous viewpoint with spectacular Annapurna panoramas.

itinerary.description: Trek or drive to Sarangkot. Sunset views.

itinerary.place: Sarangkot Sunrise and return to Pokhara
itinerary.description: Early morning sunrise. Descend to Pokhara.

treks.name: Banthanti Danda Trek
treks.permit_details: No permits required
treks.cultural_significance: Gurung and Magar communities, traditional agriculture
treks.description: The Banthanti Danda Trek is a short and easy trek from Pokhara offering beautiful sunrise views over the Annapurna range. This emerging route passes through charming villages with opportunities for organic farm visits and cultural interactions with Gurung and Magar communities. With gentle walking days and warm village hospitality, this trek provides a peaceful escape into rural Nepali life with rewarding mountain panoramas.
treks.short_description: Easy weekend trek from Pokhara with sunrise mountain views and village culture.

itinerary.description: Drive to trailhead. Trek to Banthanti.
Descend to roadhead. Drive to Pokhara.

treks.name: Bhulbhule Trek
treks.permit_details: No permits required for this short trek
treks.cultural_significance: Gateway to Annapurna Circuit, Gurung villages
treks.description: The Bhulbhule Trek is a short and gentle walk along the Marsyangdi River, perfect as a warm-up for longer treks or as a peaceful escape into the countryside. This easy route passes through terraced rice fields, traditional villages, and offers beautiful views of the surrounding hills and distant mountains. Bhulbhule village itself is a charming settlement and the traditional starting point for the Annapurna Circuit trek.
treks.short_description: Short and gentle riverside trek ideal as a warm-up for longer Annapurna treks.

itinerary.description: Return trek to Besisahar. Drive to Pokhara.

treks.name: Kande Trek
treks.permit_details: No permits required
treks.cultural_significance: Gurung villages, starting point for Mardi Himal
treks.description: The Kande Trek is a short and easy hike to the hilltop village of Kande, offering beautiful views of the Annapurna range and serving as the gateway to the Mardi Himal Trek. This gentle route passes through forests and traditional Gurung settlements, providing a quick escape into nature with rewarding mountain panoramas. Perfect for those with limited time or as an introductory trek.
treks.short_description: Short trek to Kande village with Annapurna views and access to Mardi Himal route.

itinerary.description: Drive to Phedi. Trek to Kande.

treks.name: Tadapani Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000
treks.cultural_significance: Gurung village, traditional mountain lifestyle
treks.description: The Tadapani Trek focuses on the beautiful hilltop village of Tadapani, surrounded by dense rhododendron and oak forests with stunning views of Annapurna South and Machhapuchhre. This peaceful trek offers spectacular sunrises over the Himalayas and opportunities to explore traditional Gurung culture. Tadapani is also a key junction connecting the Ghorepani and Ghandruk routes, making this trek a quiet alternative to more crowded trails.
treks.short_description: Peaceful trek to the hilltop village of Tadapani with stunning mountain views.

itinerary.description: Sunrise views, short hikes, village exploration.
Descend to roadhead. Drive to Pokhara.

treks.name: Landruk Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000
treks.cultural_significance: Gurung village, traditional mountain lifestyle
treks.description: The Landruk Trek is a short cultural trek to a beautiful Gurung village overlooking the Modi River valley. This peaceful route offers stunning views of Annapurna South and Machhapuchhre, with opportunities to experience traditional Gurung village life and hospitality. Landruk is less crowded than nearby Ghandruk, providing a more authentic and quiet experience of mountain village culture.
treks.short_description: Short cultural trek to a traditional Gurung village with beautiful valley views.

itinerary.description: Drive to Nayapul. Trek to Landruk.
Explore village, culture, and surrounding area.
Descend to Nayapul. Drive to Pokhara.

treks.name: Tolka Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000
treks.cultural_significance: Gurung village, traditional mountain lifestyle
treks.description: The Tolka Trek is a short and quiet alternative to more crowded Annapurna trails, visiting the traditional Gurung village of Tolka. This peaceful route offers beautiful mountain views, forest walks, and authentic cultural experiences away from the main tourist flow. Tolka sits on the route between Dhampus and Landruk, providing a tranquil stop with warm village hospitality.
treks.short_description: Short and quiet trek to a traditional Gurung village with authentic cultural experiences.

itinerary.description: Drive to Phedi. Trek to Dhampus.
Descend to roadhead. Drive to Pokhara.

treks.name: Ghandruk Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000
treks.cultural_significance: Largest Gurung village in Nepal, Gurkha heritage, cultural museum
treks.description: The Ghandruk Trek is a short cultural journey to one of Nepal's largest and most beautiful Gurung villages. Ghandruk offers spectacular close-up views of Annapurna South and Machhapuchhre, with its traditional stone houses, terraced fields, and ancient monasteries creating a picturesque mountain village setting. The village museum provides fascinating insights into Gurung culture and Gurkha military history, making this trek a perfect blend of natural beauty and cultural discovery.
treks.short_description: Short cultural trek to the beautiful Gurung village of Ghandruk with mountain views and museum.

itinerary.description: Drive to Nayapul. Trek to Ghandruk.
Visit museum, explore village, short hikes.
Descend to Nayapul. Drive to Pokhara.

treks.name: Manaslu Tsum Valley Trek
treks.permit_details: Manaslu Restricted Area Permit USD 100/week (Sept-Nov), USD 75/week (Dec-Aug), Tsum Valley Restricted Area Permit USD 40/week (Sept-Nov), USD 30/week (Dec-Aug), Manaslu Conservation Area Permit (MCAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Sacred 'Hidden Valley of Happiness', ancient Tibetan Buddhist culture, Bon heritage, restricted area opened in 2008
treks.description: The Manaslu Tsum Valley Trek combines the classic Manaslu Circuit with a detour into the sacred Tsum Valley, known as the 'Hidden Valley of Happiness.' This restricted region opened to trekkers in 2008 and offers a rare glimpse into ancient Tibetan Buddhist culture, with centuries-old monasteries, mani walls, and chortens. The valley is believed to be a sacred Himalayan enclave where Milarepa, the famous Buddhist saint, meditated. This challenging trek offers pristine landscapes, dramatic gorges, and profound cultural immersion in one of Nepal's most remote regions.
treks.short_description: Combined trek through Manaslu Circuit and sacred Tsum Valley with ancient Buddhist monasteries.

itinerary.description: Trek to largest monastery in Tsum Valley.

itinerary.place: Dharmasala to Bimthang (3720m) via Larkya La (5160m)
itinerary.description: Cross Larkya La Pass. Long descent.

itinerary.place: Bimthang to Dharapani to Besisahar to Kathmandu

treks.name: Tsum Valley Trek
treks.permit_details: Tsum Valley Restricted Area Permit USD 40/week (Sept-Nov), USD 30/week (Dec-Aug), Manaslu Conservation Area Permit (MCAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Sacred 'Hidden Valley of Happiness', Tibetan Buddhist heritage, restricted area
treks.description: The Tsum Valley Trek takes you to a sacred Himalayan enclave known as the 'Hidden Valley of Happiness,' nestled in the trans-Himalayan region of northern Gorkha. This restricted area, opened to trekkers in 2008, offers a pristine Tibetan Buddhist culture that has remained unchanged for centuries. The valley is dotted with ancient monasteries, including Mu Gompa and Rachen Gompa, mani walls, and chortens. According to legend, the great Buddhist saint Milarepa meditated in caves here. This trek offers profound spiritual experiences, stunning mountain scenery, and authentic cultural encounters in one of Nepal's most remote and sacred valleys.
treks.short_description: Sacred hidden valley trek with ancient Tibetan Buddhist monasteries and pristine culture.

itinerary.description: Trek to largest monastery in Tsum Valley.
Final trekking day to Soti Khola.

treks.name: Buddha Himal-Himalchuli Great Lake Circuit
treks.permit_details: Manaslu Conservation Area Permit (MCAP) NPR 3000, Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Gurung culture, traditional mountain lifestyle, newly promoted circuit
treks.description: The Buddha Himal-Himalchuli Great Lake Circuit is a newly promoted trekking route in the Manaslu region, connecting several rural municipalities in Lamjung and Gorkha. This remote circuit encompasses the beautiful peaks of Buddha Himal and the Manasiri range, along with numerous pristine glacial lakes nestled in high alpine valleys. The trek offers a true wilderness experience with spectacular mountain scenery, rich biodiversity, and authentic cultural encounters in traditional Gurung villages. Far from the crowded trails, this circuit is perfect for experienced trekkers seeking pristine landscapes and cultural immersion.
treks.short_description: New circuit trek featuring pristine peaks, glacial lakes, and authentic Gurung mountain culture.

itinerary.description: Trek through terraced fields and villages.

itinerary.place: Thoche to Glacial Lake (4400m) and return
itinerary.description: Hike to pristine glacial lake. Return to Thoche.

itinerary.place: Thoche to Buddha Himal Base Camp (4800m) and return
itinerary.description: Trek to base camp for mountain views.

treks.name: Larkya La Pass Trek
treks.permit_details: Manaslu Restricted Area Permit USD 100/week (Sept-Nov), USD 75/week (Dec-Aug), Manaslu Conservation Area Permit (MCAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Tibetan Buddhist culture, ancient trade route
treks.description: The Larkya La Pass Trek focuses on crossing the spectacular Larkya La at 5,160m, one of Nepal's highest and most challenging trekking passes. This shorter version of the Manaslu Circuit still includes the dramatic Budi Gandaki gorge, Tibetan Buddhist villages, and stunning mountain scenery, with the pass crossing as the centerpiece. From the pass, trekkers are rewarded with breathtaking panoramic views of Himlung Himal, Cheo Himal, Kang Guru, and the Annapurna range. This trek offers all the highlights of the Manaslu Circuit in a slightly condensed itinerary.
treks.short_description: Shorter Manaslu trek focusing on the spectacular Larkya La Pass crossing with mountain panoramas.

itinerary.description: Hike to Manaslu Base Camp or Birendra Tal.

itinerary.place: Dharmasala to Bimthang (3720m) via Larkya La (5160m)
itinerary.description: Early start to cross pass. Long descent.

treks.name: Soti Khola Trek
treks.permit_details: No permits required for this short trek
treks.cultural_significance: Gateway to Manaslu, multicultural villages
treks.description: The Soti Khola Trek is a short exploration of the beautiful Budi Gandaki Valley, starting from the village of Soti Khola, the gateway to the Manaslu region. This gentle trek follows the river upstream through lush forests and traditional villages, offering a peaceful introduction to the landscapes and cultures of the Manaslu region. Perfect for those with limited time or as a warm-up before longer treks, this route includes visits to local hot springs and opportunities to experience rural Nepali life.
treks.short_description: Short riverside trek exploring the gateway to the Manaslu region with hot springs.

treks.name: Chhekampar Homestay Trek
treks.permit_details: Manaslu Conservation Area Permit (MCAP) NPR 3000
treks.cultural_significance: Gurung community-based tourism, award-winning homestay program
treks.description: The Chhekampar Homestay Trek is an award-winning community-based tourism initiative in a traditional Gurung village in the Manaslu region. This immersive cultural experience allows trekkers to stay with local families, participate in daily activities, learn about traditional Gurung culture, and enjoy warm village hospitality. The trek offers stunning views of the Manaslu range, beautiful forest walks, and a genuine connection with mountain life, all while directly supporting the local community through sustainable tourism.
treks.short_description: Award-winning community homestay experience in a traditional Gurung village with mountain views.

itinerary.description: Trek to Chhekampar. Welcome by host family.
Participate in daily activities, cultural programs.

treks.name: Barpak Danda Trek
treks.permit_details: Manaslu Conservation Area Permit (MCAP) NPR 3000
treks.cultural_significance: Largest Gurung village in Nepal, epicenter of 2015 earthquake, model community tourism
treks.description: The Barpak Danda Trek takes you to Barpak, the largest Gurung village in Nepal and the epicenter of the devastating 2015 earthquake that has been beautifully rebuilt. This inspiring trek offers a powerful story of resilience and recovery, with the village now featuring an award-winning homestay program that welcomes visitors to experience authentic Gurung culture. Set against the backdrop of the magnificent Manaslu range, Barpak offers spectacular mountain views, traditional stone houses, ancient temples, and warm hospitality. This trek is a testament to the strength of mountain communities and the power of sustainable tourism.
treks.short_description: Cultural trek to Nepal's largest Gurung village, rebuilt after the 2015 earthquake with award-winning homestays.

itinerary.description: Explore village, visit memorial sites, cultural activities.

treks.name: Surya Peak Trek
treks.permit_details: Manaslu Conservation Area Permit (MCAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Gurung villages, traditional mountain culture
treks.description: The Surya Peak Trek offers stunning sunrise views over the Manaslu and Ganesh Himal ranges from a beautiful ridge top at 3,870m. This less-known trek passes through traditional Gurung villages, dense rhododendron forests, and offers spectacular mountain panoramas without the crowds of more popular routes. The summit provides a 360-degree view of the Himalayas, making the challenging ascent well worth the effort. This peaceful trek is perfect for those seeking solitude and authentic cultural experiences in the Manaslu region.
treks.short_description: Peaceful ridge trek with spectacular Manaslu sunrise views and authentic Gurung village experiences.

itinerary.place: Machha Khola to Surya Peak Base Camp (3000m)
Base Camp to Surya Peak (3870m) and return
itinerary.description: Early morning summit for sunrise. Return to base camp.

treks.name: Ganesh Himal Trek (Ruby Valley)
treks.permit_details: TIMS Card NPR 2000, Local Municipality Permit
treks.cultural_significance: Tamang culture, Tibetan Buddhist heritage, named after local gemstones
treks.description: The Ganesh Himal Trek, also known as the Ruby Valley Trek, explores a remote region between Langtang and Manaslu, named after the semi-precious rubies found in the area. This off-the-beaten-path route offers stunning views of the Ganesh Himal range, rich Tamang culture, natural hot springs, and pristine forests with diverse wildlife. The trek passes through traditional villages where ancient Buddhist traditions are still practiced, providing authentic cultural encounters far from the main tourist trails. The Ruby Valley is also known for its warm hospitality and community-based tourism initiatives.
treks.short_description: Remote trek through Ruby Valley with Ganesh Himal views, hot springs, and authentic Tamang culture.

itinerary.place: Somdang to Pangsang Pass (3800m) to Tipling (1900m)

treks.name: Ruby Valley Trek
treks.permit_details: TIMS Card NPR 2000, Local Municipality Permit
treks.cultural_significance: Named after local gemstones, Tamang and Gurung villages, community-based tourism
treks.description: The Ruby Valley Trek is named after the semi-precious stones found in this beautiful region between Langtang and Manaslu. This community-based trek offers stunning views of the Ganesh Himal range, relaxing hot springs, and authentic cultural experiences in traditional Tamang and Gurung villages. The valley is known for its warm hospitality and the opportunity to see traditional gemstone mining practices. With its moderate altitudes, diverse landscapes, and rich cultural encounters, the Ruby Valley Trek provides a perfect off-the-beaten-path adventure away from the main tourist crowds.
treks.short_description: Community-based trek through the beautiful Ruby Valley with Ganesh Himal views and hot springs.

itinerary.place: Borang to Ganesh Himal Base Camp (3850m) and return
itinerary.description: Hike for mountain views. Return to Borang.

treks.name: Rupina La Pass Trek
treks.permit_details: Manaslu Conservation Area Permit (MCAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Remote Gurung villages, traditional mountain culture
treks.description: The Rupina La Pass Trek is a remote and challenging route crossing the high Rupina La Pass at 4,750m, connecting the Manaslu region with the Annapurna region. This less-traveled pass offers spectacular views of the Ganesh Himal, Manaslu, and Annapurna ranges, with pristine alpine landscapes and traditional Gurung villages along the way. The trek requires good physical condition and experience with high-altitude passes, but rewards with solitude and authentic mountain experiences far from the crowded trails.
treks.short_description: Remote pass trek connecting Manaslu and Annapurna regions with spectacular mountain views.

itinerary.place: Philim to Rupina La Base Camp (3800m)
Base Camp to Rupina La Pass (4750m) to Gho (3500m)

treks.name: Kanchenjunga Base Camp Trek
treks.permit_details: Kanchenjunga Restricted Area Permit USD 100/week (Sept-Nov), USD 75/week (Dec-Aug), Kanchenjunga Conservation Area Permit NPR 3000
treks.cultural_significance: Limbu and Sherpa culture, sacred mountain, restricted area
treks.description: The Kanchenjunga Base Camp Trek is one of Nepal's most remote and challenging treks, leading to the base of the world's third-highest mountain, Mount Kanchenjunga (8,586m). This restricted area trek offers pristine wilderness, diverse ecosystems ranging from subtropical forests to alpine zones, and authentic cultural experiences in Limbu and Sherpa villages. The route visits both the north base camp at Pangpema and the south base camp at Oktang, providing spectacular views of Kanchenjunga's massive peaks and glaciers. With minimal tourist infrastructure, this trek offers a true wilderness adventure for experienced trekkers.
treks.short_description: Remote and challenging trek to both base camps of the world's third-highest mountain, Kanchenjunga.

itinerary.description: Trek to north base camp. Return to Lhonak.
Trek to south base camp viewpoint.

treks.name: Kanchenjunga North Base Camp Trek (Pangpema)
treks.permit_details: Kanchenjunga Restricted Area Permit USD 100/week (Sept-Nov), USD 75/week (Dec-Aug), Kanchenjunga Conservation Area Permit NPR 3000
treks.cultural_significance: Limbu and Sherpa culture, sacred mountain, restricted area
treks.description: The Kanchenjunga North Base Camp Trek leads to the remote Pangpema base camp on the north side of the world's third-highest mountain. This extreme trek follows the Ghunsa Valley through pristine forests and alpine meadows, offering unparalleled views of Kanchenjunga's massive north face and the surrounding glaciers. The route passes through traditional Sherpa and Limbu villages where ancient Buddhist culture is still preserved. With limited teahouses and requiring camping above Ghunsa, this trek offers a true wilderness experience for the most dedicated adventurers.
treks.short_description: Extreme trek to the remote north base camp of Mount Kanchenjunga with spectacular glacier views.

itinerary.description: Trek to north base camp. Return to Lhonak.

treks.name: Kanchenjunga South Base Camp Trek (Oktang)
treks.permit_details: Kanchenjunga Restricted Area Permit USD 100/week (Sept-Nov), USD 75/week (Dec-Aug), Kanchenjunga Conservation Area Permit NPR 3000
treks.cultural_significance: Sherpa and Limbu culture, traditional villages, sacred mountain
treks.description: The Kanchenjunga South Base Camp Trek leads to the spectacular Oktang viewpoint on the south side of the world's third-highest mountain. This challenging trek passes through the beautiful Yalung Valley, with diverse ecosystems ranging from subtropical forests to high alpine zones. The trail offers rich biodiversity, including the chance to see red pandas and Himalayan black bears. The Oktang viewpoint provides breathtaking views of Kanchenjunga's massive south face, the Yalung Glacier, and surrounding peaks. This trek offers authentic cultural encounters in traditional Sherpa and Limbu villages along the way.
treks.short_description: Challenging trek to the south base camp of Mount Kanchenjunga with rich biodiversity and cultural encounters.

itinerary.description: Trek to south base camp viewpoint. Return to Ramche.

treks.name: Kanchenjunga Circuit Trek
treks.permit_details: Kanchenjunga Restricted Area Permit USD 100/week (Sept-Nov), USD 75/week (Dec-Aug), Kanchenjunga Conservation Area Permit NPR 3000
attractions: Both North and South Base Camps
treks.cultural_significance: Limbu and Sherpa culture, sacred mountain, complete circuit of third highest peak
treks.description: The Kanchenjunga Circuit Trek is the ultimate adventure in Nepal's far east, completing a full circuit around the world's third-highest mountain. This expedition-style trek visits both the north base camp at Pangpema and the south base camp at Oktang, crossing the remote Lumba Sumba Pass (5,159m) that connects the two valleys. The trek offers unparalleled wilderness, diverse ecosystems from tropical forests to high-altitude deserts, and authentic cultural experiences in Limbu and Sherpa villages. With minimal infrastructure and requiring camping for much of the route, this is one of Nepal's most challenging and rewarding treks for experienced adventurers.
treks.short_description: Ultimate expedition trek completing a full circuit around Mount Kanchenjunga with both base camps.

itinerary.description: Trek to north base camp. Return to Lhonak.

itinerary.place: Ghunsa to Tseram (3400m) via Lumba Sumba Pass (5159m)
itinerary.description: Early start to cross high pass. Long day.
Trek to south base camp viewpoint.

treks.name: Lumba Sumba Pass Trek
treks.permit_details: Kanchenjunga Restricted Area Permit USD 100/week (Sept-Nov), USD 75/week (Dec-Aug), Makalu Barun National Park Permit
treks.cultural_significance: Limbu and Sherpa culture, extremely remote region
treks.description: The Lumba Sumba Pass Trek is one of Nepal's most remote and challenging expeditions, crossing the high Lumba Sumba Pass (5,159m) that connects the Kanchenjunga and Makalu regions. This technical pass requires glacier crossing experience and full camping support, offering a true wilderness adventure through completely untouched landscapes. The trek provides spectacular views of both Kanchenjunga and Makalu, the world's third and fifth-highest mountains, along with pristine forests, alpine meadows, and traditional villages rarely visited by outsiders. This is an expedition for the most dedicated adventurers seeking the ultimate Himalayan wilderness experience.
treks.short_description: Extreme expedition trek crossing remote Lumba Sumba Pass between Kanchenjunga and Makalu regions.

itinerary.description: Side trip to north base camp.

itinerary.place: Lhonak to Lumba Sumba Base Camp (4900m)
Cross Lumba Sumba Pass (5159m) to Makalu side (4500m)

treks.name: Jaljale Himal Trek
treks.permit_details: Local permits, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Limbu and Sherpa culture, remote mountain communities
treks.description: The Jaljale Himal Trek is a challenging high-altitude trek in eastern Nepal, crossing the remote Jaljale Himal range with spectacular views of Kanchenjunga and Makalu. This pristine trek offers untouched wilderness, dense rhododendron forests, and authentic cultural encounters in traditional Limbu and Sherpa villages. The route follows a beautiful ridge line with panoramic mountain views, providing a true sense of adventure far from the crowded trails. With limited infrastructure, this trek is perfect for experienced trekkers seeking solitude and pristine mountain scenery.
treks.short_description: Challenging high-altitude trek through remote Jaljale Himal with spectacular Kanchenjunga views.

itinerary.place: Jaljale Pass to Jaljale Himal Base Camp (4600m) and return

treks.name: Milke Danda Trek
treks.permit_details: Local permits, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Multi-ethnic communities - Rai, Limbu, Sherpa
treks.description: The Milke Danda Trek follows a spectacular ridge covered with rhododendron forests in eastern Nepal, offering panoramic views from Annapurna to Kanchenjunga. Best experienced during spring (March-April) when the hillsides explode with colorful rhododendron blooms, this trek provides excellent opportunities for nature photography and bird watching. The route passes through traditional Rai, Limbu, and Sherpa villages, offering authentic cultural encounters and warm hospitality. With moderate altitudes and beautiful forest trails, this trek is perfect for those seeking Himalayan views without extreme altitude challenges.
treks.short_description: Ridge trek through spectacular rhododendron forests with panoramic Himalayan views in spring.

treks.name: Tinjure-Milke-Jaljale Rhododendron Trek
treks.permit_details: No permits required
treks.cultural_significance: Rai, Limbu, and Sherpa villages, traditional lifestyle
treks.description: The Tinjure-Milke-Jaljale Rhododendron Trek takes you through the 'Rhododendron Capital of Nepal,' an area boasting over 30 species of rhododendron, including 20 that are endemic to the region. Best experienced in March-April when the hillsides explode in a riot of colors, this trek is a floral wonderland. The route follows beautiful ridges through dense rhododendron forests, offering spectacular views of the eastern Himalayas including Kanchenjunga, Makalu, and Everest on clear days. This is a paradise for nature lovers, photographers, and anyone seeking the beauty of Nepal's springtime landscapes.
treks.short_description: Spring trek through the 'Rhododendron Capital of Nepal' with spectacular blooms and Himalayan views.

treks.name: Singalila Ridge Trek
treks.permit_details: Local permits, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Rai and Limbu culture, tea heritage, Nepal-India border region
treks.description: The Singalila Ridge Trek follows the Nepal-India border along a spectacular ridge offering some of the closest views of Kanchenjunga, the world's third-highest mountain. The trek passes through beautiful rhododendron forests, tea gardens, and traditional Rai and Limbu villages, offering unforgettable sunrise views from Sandakpur with Kanchenjunga, Makalu, and even Everest visible on clear days. This ridge walk provides a unique perspective of the eastern Himalayas and is particularly spectacular in spring when the rhododendrons are in full bloom.
treks.short_description: Spectacular ridge trek along the Nepal-India border with close-up views of Kanchenjunga and Everest.

treks.name: Pathibhara Trek
treks.permit_details: No permits required
treks.cultural_significance: Sacred Hindu pilgrimage site, believed to fulfill wishes
treks.description: The Pathibhara Trek is a pilgrimage journey to the sacred Pathibhara Temple, one of Nepal's most revered Hindu shrines, perched at 3,794m with spectacular views of the Kanchenjunga range. Devotees believe that the goddess Pathibhara fulfills the wishes of those who make the pilgrimage. The trek passes through beautiful forests and traditional Limbu villages, offering both spiritual significance and stunning mountain scenery. The temple is especially crowded during the Dashain festival when thousands of pilgrims come to worship and sacrifice animals.
treks.short_description: Pilgrimage trek to the sacred Pathibhara Temple with spectacular Kanchenjunga views.

treks.name: Salpa Danda Trek
treks.permit_details: Local permits, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Rai indigenous culture, traditional mountain lifestyle
treks.description: The Salpa Danda Trek follows a beautiful ridge in eastern Nepal with panoramic Himalayan views from Makalu to Kanchenjunga. This off-the-beaten-path trek passes through traditional Rai villages, dense rhododendron forests, and offers authentic cultural experiences away from the main tourist trails. The ridge walk provides spectacular sunrise views over the eastern Himalayas, with opportunities to experience traditional Rai culture, including their unique language, customs, and hospitality. This trek is perfect for those seeking authentic cultural encounters combined with beautiful mountain scenery.
treks.short_description: Eastern ridge trek with Himalayan views and authentic Rai village cultural experiences.

treks.name: Pokhari Lagna Pass Trek
treks.permit_details: Kanchenjunga Conservation Area Permit, Local permits
treks.cultural_significance: Limbu and Sherpa culture
treks.description: The Pokhari Lagna Pass Trek is a remote route in the Kanchenjunga region, crossing the scenic Pokhari Lagna Pass through pristine alpine lakes and dense rhododendron forests. This challenging trek offers spectacular views of Kanchenjunga and Makalu, with opportunities to encounter rare wildlife including red pandas and Himalayan black bears. The route passes through traditional Limbu and Sherpa villages, providing authentic cultural encounters in a less-visited area of eastern Nepal. With its combination of high passes, sacred lakes, and rich biodiversity, this trek offers a true wilderness adventure.
treks.short_description: Remote pass trek through alpine lakes in Kanchenjunga region with wildlife and mountain views.

itinerary.place: Pokhari Lakes to Pokhari Lagna Pass (4700m) and descent

treks.name: Kumbhakarna Base Camp Trek
treks.permit_details: Kanchenjunga Restricted Area Permit, Kanchenjunga Conservation Area Permit
treks.cultural_significance: Limbu culture, sacred mountain
treks.description: The Kumbhakarna Base Camp Trek leads to the base of Jannu (also known as Kumbhakarna), one of the most spectacular and technically challenging peaks in the Himalayas at 7,711m. This extremely remote trek in the Kanchenjunga region offers pristine wilderness, untouched landscapes, and close-up views of Jannu's massive north face. The route passes through traditional Limbu villages and dense forests before entering high alpine terrain with spectacular glaciers and mountain scenery. With minimal infrastructure and requiring camping, this trek is for experienced adventurers seeking true wilderness.
treks.short_description: Remote trek to the base of spectacular Jannu Peak in Kanchenjunga with pristine wilderness.

treks.name: Chheskam Homestay Trek
treks.permit_details: Makalu Barun National Park Permit
treks.cultural_significance: Rai indigenous culture, community-based tourism
treks.description: The Chheskam Homestay Trek offers an authentic Rai village experience in the Makalu region, with stunning views of Mount Makalu and the surrounding Himalayas. This community-based trek allows visitors to stay with local Rai families, participate in daily activities, and learn about traditional Rai culture, including their unique language, customs, and rituals. The village of Chheskam is nestled in a beautiful valley surrounded by terraced fields and forests, providing a peaceful setting for cultural immersion. This trek directly benefits the local community through sustainable tourism while offering genuine encounters with indigenous mountain culture.
treks.short_description: Authentic Rai village homestay experience with Makalu views and community-based tourism.

treks.name: Ramaroshan Trek
treks.permit_details: No permits required
treks.cultural_significance: Multi-ethnic communities, apple farming heritage
treks.description: The Ramaroshan Trek explores one of Nepal's most beautiful and least-visited regions, famous for its rolling hills covered with apple orchards, rhododendron forests, and traditional villages. Located in far-western Nepal, Ramaroshan is known as the 'Switzerland of Nepal' for its scenic beauty, with 12 lakes scattered across alpine meadows. The trek offers spectacular views of the Api and Saipal Himalayas, opportunities to taste organic apples and local products, and authentic encounters with multi-ethnic communities. This off-the-beaten-path trek is perfect for those seeking pristine nature and genuine cultural experiences away from all tourist crowds.
treks.short_description: Off-the-beaten-path trek through apple orchards, rhododendron forests, and traditional villages in far-western Nepal.

itinerary.description: Fly to Dhangadhi, drive to Mangalsen.

treks.name: Hile Kharka Rhododendron Trek
treks.permit_details: No permits required
treks.cultural_significance: Rai and Limbu indigenous cultures, traditional villages
