treks.description: The Hile Kharka Rhododendron Trek is best experienced in spring when the hillsides of eastern Nepal explode with colorful rhododendron blooms. This beautiful trek passes through dense rhododendron forests in their full glory, offering stunning views of Kanchenjunga, Makalu, and other eastern Himalayan peaks. The route follows forest trails rich in bird life, with opportunities to spot the elusive red panda and other wildlife. Combined with visits to traditional Rai and Limbu villages, this trek offers a perfect blend of natural beauty and cultural experiences during Nepal's most colorful season.
treks.short_description: Spring trek through spectacular rhododendron forests with Kanchenjunga views and bird watching.

treks.name: Mundum Trail
treks.permit_details: Local permits (available at entry points), TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Kirat people's indigenous Mundum religion and culture, ancient shamanic traditions
treks.description: The Mundum Trail is a recently developed trekking route in eastern Nepal, celebrating the rich culture of the Kirat people, one of Nepal's oldest indigenous groups. The trail passes through traditional Rai and Limbu villages, offering deep insights into their ancient Mundum religion, shamanic rituals, and vibrant festivals including the famous Sakela dance. The route provides spectacular views of four 8,000-meter peaks: Everest, Kanchenjunga, Makalu, and Lhotse, while traversing beautiful rhododendron forests and terraced hillsides. This cultural trek offers a unique opportunity to experience living indigenous traditions while enjoying stunning Himalayan scenery.
treks.short_description: Cultural trek through Kirat heartland exploring ancient Mundum religion with spectacular Himalayan views.

treks.name: Upper Dolpo Trek
treks.permit_details: Upper Dolpo Restricted Area Permit USD 500 for first 10 days, plus USD 50 per day thereafter, Shey Phoksundo National Park Permit NPR 3000
treks.cultural_significance: Ancient Bon religion, Tibetan Buddhist culture, restricted area, setting of 'The Snow Leopard'
treks.description: The Upper Dolpo Trek is one of the most remote and culturally rich treks in Nepal, traversing the ancient Tibetan Buddhist and Bon region made famous by Peter Matthiessen's book 'The Snow Leopard.' This restricted area requires special permits and full camping support, taking trekkers through high mountain passes, arid Tibetan plateau landscapes, and ancient villages where traditional culture remains unchanged for centuries. Highlights include the turquoise waters of Phoksundo Lake, the ancient Shey Gompa, and the sacred Crystal Mountain. This expedition-style trek offers unparalleled cultural immersion and wilderness experience for the most dedicated adventurers.
treks.short_description: Extreme expedition through ancient Tibetan Buddhist and Bon culture in remote Upper Dolpo.

itinerary.description: Fly to Nepalgunj, then to Juphal.

itinerary.place: High Camp to Shey Gompa (4300m) via Kang La Pass (5360m)
itinerary.description: Cross highest pass. Descent to Shey.
Trek to one of highest settlements.

treks.name: Lower Dolpo Trek
treks.permit_details: Lower Dolpo Restricted Area Permit USD 20/week, Shey Phoksundo National Park Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Bon religion, Tibetan-influenced culture, traditional mountain lifestyle
treks.description: The Lower Dolpo Trek is a journey to the stunning turquoise waters of Shey Phoksundo Lake, Nepal's deepest lake, and the culturally rich valleys of Lower Dolpo. This trek offers a fascinating mix of natural beauty, ancient Bon and Buddhist culture, and a true sense of remoteness without the extreme challenges of Upper Dolpo. The route passes through traditional villages where Tibetan-influenced culture is still preserved, ancient monasteries, and dramatic landscapes ranging from pine forests to high-altitude deserts. Made famous by the book and film 'The Snow Leopard,' this trek provides authentic cultural immersion in one of Nepal's most unique regions.
treks.short_description: Classic Dolpo trek to Nepal's deepest turquoise lake with unique Bon culture and stunning landscapes.

itinerary.description: Fly to Nepalgunj, then to Juphal.
Explore lake, Ringmo village, Bon monastery.

itinerary.place: Phoksundo Khola to Kagmara Base Camp (4200m)
Base Camp to Dho Tarap (4000m) via Kagmara Pass (4360m)
itinerary.description: Cross pass. Descent to Dho Tarap.

treks.name: Shey Phoksundo Lake Trek
treks.permit_details: Shey Phoksundo National Park Permit NPR 3000, Lower Dolpo Restricted Area Permit USD 20/week, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Bon religion, Tibetan Buddhist culture, traditional village life
treks.description: The Shey Phoksundo Lake Trek is a journey to Nepal's deepest (145m) and one of its most beautiful turquoise lakes, set against the dramatic backdrop of the Dolpo region. This trek offers a stunning combination of natural beauty, unique Bon culture, and a sense of true wilderness without requiring the extreme challenges of Upper Dolpo. The trail follows the Phoksundo River through pine forests and deep canyons, passing waterfalls and traditional villages before reaching the spectacular lake. The village of Ringmo, perched above the lake, is home to an ancient Bon monastery and offers warm hospitality.
treks.short_description: Classic Dolpo trek to Nepal's deepest turquoise lake with Bon culture and spectacular scenery.

itinerary.description: Fly to Nepalgunj, then to Juphal.
Explore lake, Ringmo village, Bon monastery.

treks.name: Phoksundo Lake Trek
treks.permit_details: Shey Phoksundo National Park Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Bon religion, traditional village life
treks.description: The Phoksundo Lake Trek is a shorter version of the classic Dolpo trek, focusing on the stunning turquoise waters of Shey Phoksundo Lake, Nepal's deepest lake at 145m. This trek offers a perfect introduction to the Dolpo region, with its unique Bon culture, traditional villages, and spectacular mountain scenery without requiring the extended time and challenges of longer Dolpo treks. The trail follows the Phoksundo River through beautiful forests and dramatic canyons, passing waterfalls before reaching the breathtaking lake and the traditional village of Ringmo with its ancient Bon monastery.
treks.short_description: Shorter Dolpo trek focusing on Nepal's deepest turquoise lake with Bon culture and stunning scenery.

itinerary.description: Fly to Nepalgunj, then to Juphal.

treks.name: Saldang Trek
treks.permit_details: Upper Dolpo Restricted Area Permit USD 500/10 days, Shey Phoksundo National Park Permit NPR 3000
treks.cultural_significance: Ancient Bon and Buddhist culture, one of the highest permanent settlements in Nepal
treks.description: The Saldang Trek in Upper Dolpo takes you to one of the highest permanent human settlements in Nepal, the remote village of Saldang at over 4,000m. This extreme expedition explores ancient Bon and Buddhist monasteries, crosses high mountain passes, and offers profound immersion into a timeless Tibetan culture preserved for centuries. The stark, dramatic landscapes of the Tibetan plateau, with its deep gorges and snow-capped peaks, provide a breathtaking backdrop to this cultural journey. With full camping required and extremely limited infrastructure, this trek is for the most dedicated adventurers seeking authentic experiences in one of the world's most remote regions.
treks.short_description: Extreme expedition to one of Nepal's highest settlements in Upper Dolpo with ancient Bon culture.

itinerary.place: High Camp to Shey Gompa (4300m) via Kang La Pass (5360m)
itinerary.description: Cross highest pass. Descent to Shey.
Trek to one of highest settlements.

treks.name: Gompa Samba Trek
treks.permit_details: Upper Dolpo Restricted Area Permit USD 500/10 days, Shey Phoksundo National Park Permit NPR 3000
treks.cultural_significance: Ancient Bon and Buddhist monasteries, remote Tibetan culture
treks.description: The Gompa Samba Trek explores remote monasteries and villages in Upper Dolpo, offering an authentic experience of ancient Tibetan Buddhist and Bon culture. This challenging expedition trek requires full camping support and takes you through pristine valleys and high mountain passes to some of the most isolated monasteries in the Himalayas. The region's stark beauty, combined with its rich spiritual heritage and the warm hospitality of its people, creates an unforgettable journey for those seeking profound cultural immersion in one of Nepal's most remote regions.
treks.short_description: Extreme Dolpo trek to remote ancient monasteries with Tibetan Buddhist and Bon culture.

itinerary.place: Phoksundo Khola to Shey Gompa (4300m) via Kang La Pass (5360m)

treks.name: Crystal Mountain Trek
treks.permit_details: Upper Dolpo Restricted Area Permit USD 500/10 days, Shey Phoksundo National Park Permit NPR 3000
treks.cultural_significance: Sacred Crystal Mountain, ancient Bon and Buddhist pilgrimage site
treks.description: The Crystal Mountain Trek is a spiritual journey to one of the most sacred sites in the Tibetan Buddhist and Bon world - the Crystal Mountain (Shey Gompa) in Upper Dolpo. This remote pilgrimage route was made famous by the book 'The Snow Leopard' and offers a profound spiritual experience in one of the most isolated regions of Nepal. The trek crosses high mountain passes, traverses the starkly beautiful Tibetan plateau, and visits ancient monasteries where traditional culture remains unchanged for centuries. For those seeking both adventure and spiritual depth, this expedition offers an unparalleled journey.
treks.short_description: Sacred pilgrimage trek to Crystal Mountain in Upper Dolpo, made famous by 'The Snow Leopard'.

itinerary.place: High Camp to Shey Gompa (4300m) via Kang La Pass (5360m)
itinerary.description: Cross pass. Arrive at Crystal Mountain.

treks.name: Langtang Valley Trek
treks.permit_details: Langtang National Park Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000 (waived if trekking with registered guide)
treks.cultural_significance: Tamang heritage, Buddhist monasteries, Tibetan-influenced culture
treks.description: The Langtang Valley Trek, known as the 'Valley of Glaciers,' is one of Nepal's most beautiful and accessible treks, located just north of Kathmandu. The trail follows the Langtang River through dense forests of rhododendron, oak, and bamboo, offering stunning views of Langtang Lirung (7,227m) and surrounding peaks. The trek provides rich cultural experiences in traditional Tamang villages and Buddhist monasteries, culminating at Kyanjin Gompa with its famous cheese factory and optional hike to Tserko Ri for panoramic Himalayan views.
treks.short_description: Beautiful valley trek close to Kathmandu with Tamang culture, glacier views, and the famous Kyanjin Gompa.

itinerary.description: Scenic 7-8 hour drive from Kathmandu through Trishuli Bazaar and Dhunche. Overnight in Syabrubesi.
Trek 5-6 hours through dense forest along Langtang River, crossing several suspension bridges. Possible wildlife sightings.
Trek 5-6 hours through rhododendron and bamboo forests. First views of Langtang Lirung. Overnight in Langtang village.
Trek 3-4 hours past mani walls and chortens to Kyanjin Gompa. Visit monastery and cheese factory.
Hike to Tserko Ri (4984m) for panoramic views of Langtang range, or explore Langtang Glacier and surrounding valleys.
Descend 5-6 hours through forests back to Lama Hotel.
Final trekking day descending 4-5 hours to Syabrubesi.

treks.name: Gosaikunda Lake Trek
treks.permit_details: Langtang National Park Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Sacred Hindu and Buddhist pilgrimage site, believed to be created by Lord Shiva
treks.description: The Gosaikunda Lake Trek is a popular pilgrimage and trekking route leading to a series of sacred alpine lakes at 4,380m. According to Hindu mythology, these lakes were created by Lord Shiva when he thrust his trident into the mountain to obtain water to cool his burning throat after consuming poison. The main lake, Gosaikunda, is revered by both Hindus and Buddhists and is surrounded by dramatic mountain scenery. The trek is particularly busy during the Janai Purnima festival in August when thousands of pilgrims visit.
treks.short_description: Pilgrimage trek to sacred alpine lakes at 4,380m with stunning mountain views and Hindu mythology.

itinerary.description: Drive 7-8 hours from Kathmandu to Dhunche, the district headquarters of Rasuwa.
Trek 5-6 hours through forests to Chandanwari. Visit Sing Gompa and cheese factory.
Trek 6-7 hours ascending to Laurebina Pass and descending to Gosaikunda Lake. Challenging day with altitude gain.
Explore the sacred lake and surrounding smaller lakes including Bhairav Kunda, Saraswati Kunda, and Surya Kunda.

treks.name: Tamang Heritage Trail
treks.permit_details: Langtang National Park Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Authentic Tamang culture, traditional lifestyle, Buddhist heritage, indigenous weaving traditions
treks.description: The Tamang Heritage Trail is a culturally rich trek designed to showcase the unique traditions, lifestyle, and hospitality of the Tamang people, one of Nepal's largest indigenous ethnic groups. Trekkers stay in authentic homestays, participate in daily village life, learn about traditional weaving, and visit local monasteries. The trail features natural hot springs at Tatopani, spectacular mountain views from Nagthali, and warm hospitality in traditional villages that were rebuilt after the 2015 earthquake. This community-based tourism initiative directly benefits local families.
treks.short_description: Cultural trek focused on authentic Tamang homestays, hot springs, and traditional village experiences.

itinerary.description: Drive 7-8 hours from Kathmandu to Syabrubesi.
Trek 5-6 hours to beautiful Tamang village of Gatlang.
Trek 4-5 hours to Tatopani. Relax in natural hot springs.
Hike to Nagthali for mountain views. Descend to Thuman village.
Trek 4-5 hours to Timure, near Tibetan border. Visit historic fort.
Trek 5-6 hours through forests to beautiful Briddim village.
Rest day. Explore village, monastery, and local culture.

treks.name: Helambu Trek
treks.permit_details: Shivapuri Nagarjun National Park Permit NPR 1000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Hyolmo (Sherpa) culture, Tibetan Buddhist traditions, ancient trade route
treks.description: The Helambu Trek is a gentle introduction to Himalayan trekking, located just north of Kathmandu Valley. This culturally rich region is home to the Hyolmo people, who share close cultural and linguistic ties with the Sherpa. The trek passes through beautiful rhododendron and oak forests, traditional villages with ancient monasteries, and terraced farmlands famous for apple orchards. With moderate altitudes and excellent teahouse accommodations, Helambu offers a perfect short trek for those wanting cultural experiences and mountain views without the challenges of high-altitude trekking.
treks.short_description: Gentle cultural trek near Kathmandu through Hyolmo villages with monasteries and apple orchards.

itinerary.description: Drive to Sundarijal. Trek 5-6 hours through Shivapuri National Park to Chisapani.
Trek 6-7 hours through forests and villages.
Trek 5-6 hours ascending to Tharepati, the highest point with mountain views.
Descend 5-6 hours to Melamchi Gaon, a beautiful Hyolmo village.
Trek 4-5 hours through forests to Tarke Gyang, the largest village in Helambu.
Trek 5-6 hours along ridge with mountain views to Sermathang.
Long descent 5-6 hours to Melamchi Bazaar.

treks.name: Helambu Circuit Trek
treks.permit_details: Shivapuri Nagarjun National Park Permit NPR 1000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Complete circuit of Helambu region, Hyolmo culture, traditional villages
treks.description: The Helambu Circuit Trek is a complete loop around the Helambu region, offering a more comprehensive experience than the standard Helambu Trek. Starting and ending in Sundarijal, this circuit explores both the eastern and western sides of the valley, crossing Tharepati Pass and providing panoramic views of the Langtang, Ganesh, and Jugal Himal ranges. The trek passes through diverse landscapes from subtropical forests to alpine meadows, and visits numerous traditional Hyolmo villages with ancient Buddhist monasteries and warm homestay hospitality.
treks.short_description: Complete circuit of the Helambu region with diverse landscapes, mountain views, and Hyolmo culture.

itinerary.description: Drive to Sundarijal. Trek 5-6 hours to Chisapani.
Trek 5-6 hours to Tharepati, the highest point.

itinerary.place: Pati Bhanjyang to Sundarijal to Kathmandu
itinerary.description: Descend to Sundarijal. Drive to Kathmandu.

treks.name: Chisapani Nagarkot Trek
treks.permit_details: Shivapuri Nagarjun National Park Permit NPR 1000
treks.cultural_significance: Tamang and Newar villages, traditional mountain lifestyle
treks.description: The Chisapani Nagarkot Trek is a short and easy trek around the rim of Kathmandu Valley, perfect for those with limited time or seeking a gentle introduction to Himalayan trekking. The trail passes through Shivapuri Nagarjun National Park with its lush forests, waterfalls, and diverse bird life. Chisapani offers stunning sunrise views over the Langtang range, while Nagarkot is world-famous for its panoramic Himalayan vistas stretching from Annapurna to Everest on clear days. The trek combines natural beauty, cultural encounters in traditional Tamang and Newar villages, and spectacular mountain views without the challenges of high altitude.
treks.short_description: Easy weekend trek near Kathmandu with Himalayan sunrise views from Chisapani and Nagarkot.

itinerary.description: Drive 1 hour to Sundarijal. Trek 5-6 hours through Shivapuri National Park to Chisapani.
Trek 6-7 hours through forests and villages to Nagarkot. Sunset views.

itinerary.place: Nagarkot Sunrise and trek to Changunarayan (1540m)
itinerary.description: Early morning sunrise views. Trek 4-5 hours to Changunarayan Temple.
Explore ancient temple. Drive 1 hour back to Kathmandu.

treks.name: Ganjala Pass Trek
treks.permit_details: Langtang National Park Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000, Shivapuri National Park Permit (for Helambu section)
treks.cultural_significance: Ancient trans-Himalayan trading route connecting Langtang and Helambu, Tamang and Sherpa culture
treks.description: The Ganjala Pass Trek is one of the most challenging and adventurous routes in the Langtang region, crossing the high Ganjala Pass at 5,130m that connects the Langtang Valley with the Helambu region. This remote trek follows the classic Langtang Valley to Kyanjin Gompa, then diverges into true wilderness, requiring camping equipment and technical skills for the pass crossing. The route offers spectacular mountain scenery, pristine alpine landscapes, and a true sense of adventure far from crowded trails. After crossing the pass, the trek descends through the beautiful Helambu Valley with its rich Hyolmo culture.
treks.short_description: Challenging remote trek crossing Ganjala Pass (5130m) connecting Langtang Valley with Helambu.

itinerary.description: Hike to Tserko Ri (4984m) for acclimatization and views.

itinerary.place: Ngegang to Ganjala Pass (5130m) to Keldang (4300m)
itinerary.description: Early start to cross pass. Technical section. Descend to Keldang. Camping.

treks.name: Langtang Valley via Ganja La Pass Trek
treks.permit_details: Langtang National Park Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000, Shivapuri National Park Permit
treks.cultural_significance: Ancient trans-Himalayan trade route, Tamang and Hyolmo cultures
treks.description: The Langtang Valley via Ganja La Pass Trek combines the best of the Langtang Valley with a challenging high-altitude pass crossing into the Helambu region. This epic journey follows the classic Langtang Valley route to Kyanjin Gompa, then continues into remote wilderness to cross the Ganja La Pass at 5,130m. The pass offers spectacular panoramic views of the Langtang, Ganesh, and Jugal Himal ranges. The trek then descends through the pristine Helambu Valley, visiting traditional Hyolmo villages before ending near Kathmandu. This route requires camping equipment, technical skills for the pass crossing, and excellent physical fitness.
treks.short_description: Epic Langtang to Helambu trek crossing Ganja La Pass with wilderness camping and mountain views.

itinerary.place: Ngegang to Ganja La Pass (5130m) to Keldang (4300m)

treks.name: Pangsang Pass Trek
treks.permit_details: Langtang National Park Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Remote Tamang villages, traditional mountain lifestyle
treks.description: The Pangsang Pass Trek is a remote and less-traveled route in the Langtang region, crossing the scenic Pangsang Pass at 3,850m. This trek offers a wonderful alternative to the more crowded Langtang Valley, passing through pristine forests, traditional Tamang villages, and offering spectacular views of the Langtang, Ganesh, and Manaslu ranges. The route provides authentic cultural experiences in villages rarely visited by tourists, with opportunities for homestays and deep immersion in traditional mountain life.
treks.short_description: Remote pass trek through pristine forests and authentic Tamang villages with mountain views.

itinerary.place: Somdang to Pangsang Pass (3850m) to Tipling (1900m)

treks.name: Dhunche Trek
treks.permit_details: No permits required for this short trek
treks.cultural_significance: District headquarters of Rasuwa, Tamang culture, trading center
treks.description: The Dhunche Trek is a short and easy exploration of the area around Dhunche, the district headquarters of Rasuwa and the gateway to the Langtang region. This gentle trek offers beautiful views of the Langtang range, visits to traditional Tamang villages, and opportunities to experience local market culture. It's perfect for those with limited time or as a warm-up trek before longer adventures in the Langtang Valley.
treks.short_description: Short and easy trek around Dhunche with mountain views and Tamang village culture.

itinerary.description: Short hikes to nearby villages and viewpoints.

treks.name: Syabrubesi Trek
treks.permit_details: No permits required for this short trek
treks.cultural_significance: Gateway to Langtang, multicultural village (Tamang, Tibetan, Gurung)
treks.description: The Syabrubesi Trek is a short exploration of the vibrant village of Syabrubesi, the main gateway to the Langtang Valley and Gosaikunda treks. Located at the confluence of the Langtang and Bhote Koshi rivers, this multicultural village offers fascinating insights into the mix of Tamang, Tibetan, and Gurung cultures. Short hikes from the village lead to viewpoints, nearby hot springs, and traditional settlements, making it perfect for those with limited time or as an acclimatization stop before longer treks.
treks.short_description: Short cultural trek exploring the gateway village of Syabrubesi and surrounding areas.

itinerary.description: Hike to hot springs and nearby villages.

treks.name: Thulo Syafru Homestay Trek
treks.permit_details: Langtang National Park Permit NPR 3000
treks.cultural_significance: Traditional Tamang village with community homestay program
treks.description: The Thulo Syafru Homestay Trek offers an authentic Tamang village experience in the beautiful hills of the Langtang region. This community-based trek focuses on cultural immersion, with visitors staying in traditional homes, participating in daily activities like farming and cooking, and learning about Tamang traditions and Buddhist culture. The warm hospitality of the villagers, combined with beautiful forest walks and mountain views, creates a memorable and meaningful trekking experience that directly benefits the local community.
treks.short_description: Cultural homestay trek in a traditional Tamang village with authentic lifestyle experiences.

itinerary.description: Trek 4-5 hours to village. Welcome by host family.
Participate in daily activities, cultural programs.

treks.name: Mulkharka Village Trek
treks.permit_details: No permits required for this short trek
treks.cultural_significance: Traditional Tamang village with homestay program
treks.description: The Mulkharka Village Trek is a short cultural trek to a beautiful Tamang village in the hills above Melamchi. This easy trek offers authentic homestay experiences, allowing visitors to immerse themselves in traditional village life, learn about Tamang customs and cuisine, and enjoy warm hospitality. Surrounded by terraced fields and forests, Mulkharka provides a peaceful escape from the city and a genuine glimpse into rural Nepali life, all within easy reach of Kathmandu.
treks.short_description: Short cultural homestay trek to a traditional Tamang village near Kathmandu.

itinerary.description: Village walks, cultural activities, short hikes.
Descend to Melamchi. Drive to Kathmandu.

treks.name: Melamchi Valley Trek
treks.permit_details: Langtang National Park Permit NPR 3000
treks.cultural_significance: Tamang and Hyolmo culture, traditional villages
treks.description: The Melamchi Valley Trek explores the beautiful valley of the Melamchi River, a peaceful region known for its terraced farmlands, traditional Tamang and Hyolmo villages, and stunning mountain views. This gentle trek follows the river upstream through lush forests, past waterfalls, and into the heart of Helambu's apple-growing region. With moderate altitudes and excellent teahouse accommodations, it offers a perfect introduction to the landscapes and cultures of Nepal's mid-hills.
treks.short_description: Gentle valley trek through traditional villages with river scenery and mountain views.

itinerary.description: Descend to Melamchi. Drive to Kathmandu.

treks.name: Bhairav Kunda Trek
treks.permit_details: Langtang National Park Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Sacred Hindu pilgrimage site dedicated to Lord Bhairav
treks.description: The Bhairav Kunda Trek leads to a sacred glacial lake at 4,000m, dedicated to Lord Bhairav, a fierce manifestation of Lord Shiva. This pilgrimage site attracts Hindu devotees during the full moon of August-September. The trek offers beautiful mountain scenery, with views of Langtang Lirung, Ganesh Himal, and the surrounding peaks. The trail passes through dense forests and alpine meadows, providing a sense of spiritual tranquility in a pristine mountain setting far from the main tourist routes.
treks.short_description: Pilgrimage trek to sacred Bhairav Kunda lake at 4,000m with Langtang mountain views.

itinerary.place: Thulo Syafru to Bhairav Kunda Base Camp (3200m)
Base Camp to Bhairav Kunda (4000m) and return
itinerary.description: Early morning ascent to sacred lake. Return to base camp.

treks.name: Gosainkunda Trek
treks.permit_details: Langtang National Park Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Sacred Hindu and Buddhist pilgrimage site, Lord Shiva legend
treks.description: The Gosainkunda Trek is a classic pilgrimage route leading to the sacred alpine lakes at 4,380m. According to Hindu mythology, these lakes were created by Lord Shiva and are considered one of the holiest sites in Nepal. The trek offers stunning mountain scenery, with views of Langtang Lirung, Ganesh Himal, and other peaks. The main lake, Gosainkunda, is surrounded by 108 smaller lakes and is a spectacular sight, especially during the Janai Purnima festival when thousands of pilgrims visit. This trek combines spiritual significance with beautiful Himalayan landscapes.
treks.short_description: Classic pilgrimage trek to the sacred Gosainkunda lakes with stunning mountain scenery.

itinerary.place: Sing Gompa to Gosainkunda (4380m) via Laurebina Pass (4610m)
itinerary.description: Trek 6-7 hours crossing pass to the sacred lake.
Explore the main lake and surrounding smaller lakes.
Descend to Sundarijal. Drive to Kathmandu.

treks.name: Laurebina Pass Trek
treks.permit_details: Langtang National Park Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Sacred pass on the pilgrimage route to Gosainkunda
treks.description: The Laurebina Pass Trek focuses on crossing the scenic Laurebina Pass at 4,610m, the gateway to the sacred Gosainkunda lakes. This shorter version of the Gosainkunda trek is perfect for those wanting to experience the thrill of a high-altitude pass crossing and the beauty of the alpine lakes without the longer circuit. The pass offers spectacular panoramic views of the Langtang, Ganesh, and Tibetan Himalayan ranges before descending to the turquoise waters of Gosainkunda, one of Nepal's holiest lakes.
treks.short_description: Shorter trek focusing on Laurebina Pass and the sacred Gosainkunda lakes with mountain views.

itinerary.place: Sing Gompa to Laurebina Pass (4610m) to Gosainkunda (4380m)

treks.name: Manaslu Circuit Trek
treks.permit_details: Manaslu Restricted Area Permit (USD 100/week Sept-Nov, USD 75/week Dec-Aug), Manaslu Conservation Area Permit (MCAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Tibetan Buddhist culture, Nyeshang Valley, ancient trade route to Tibet
treks.description: The Manaslu Circuit Trek is a spectacular journey around the world's eighth-highest mountain, Mount Manaslu (8,163m). This less-crowded alternative to the Annapurna Circuit offers stunning mountain scenery, crossing the challenging Larkya La Pass (5,160m), and experiencing authentic Tibetan Buddhist culture in remote villages. The trek follows the ancient salt trading route along the Budi Gandaki River, through deep gorges, subtropical forests, and high alpine landscapes. With its combination of natural beauty, cultural richness, and sense of remoteness, the Manaslu Circuit is considered one of Nepal's best treks.
treks.short_description: Classic circuit trek around Mount Manaslu with Tibetan culture and the challenging Larkya La Pass.

itinerary.description: First views of Manaslu. Tibetan influence.
Hike to Manaslu Base Camp or Birendra Tal Lake.
Trek to Tibetan trading village of Samdo.

itinerary.place: Dharmasala to Bimthang (3720m) via Larkya La Pass (5160m)
itinerary.description: Early start to cross high pass. Long descent.

treks.name: Upper Mustang Trek
treks.permit_details: Upper Mustang Restricted Area Permit USD 500 for first 10 days (plus USD 50 per additional day), Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000
treks.cultural_significance: Ancient Kingdom of Lo, preserved Tibetan Buddhist culture, forbidden kingdom opened in 1992, restricted area requiring special permit [citation:1][citation:7]
treks.description: The Upper Mustang Trek takes you to the former forbidden Kingdom of Lo, a restricted area that opened to trekkers only in 1992. This trans-Himalayan region lies in the rain shadow of the Annapurna massif, creating a dramatic desert landscape of eroded canyons, red cliffs, and whitewashed villages that feels more like Tibet than Nepal. The walled capital of Lo Manthang, with its ancient monasteries and royal palace, offers a rare glimpse into a living Tibetan Buddhist culture preserved for centuries. The trek follows ancient trade routes through villages with centuries-old monasteries, mysterious sky caves carved into cliffs, and stunning mountain views of Dhaulagiri, Annapurna, and Nilgiri. [citation:1][citation:7]
treks.short_description: Journey to the ancient forbidden Kingdom of Lo with preserved Tibetan culture, ancient monasteries, and dramatic desert landscapes.

itinerary.description: Drive or fly to Pokhara. Overnight at lakeside hotel.
Scenic 20-minute mountain flight to Jomsom between Dhaulagiri and Annapurna. Trek 3-4 hours to Kagbeni, the ancient gateway to Upper Mustang. [citation:7]
Enter Upper Mustang restricted area. Trek 5-6 hours through dramatic canyons and ridges.
Cross Taklam La Pass (3624m) and Dajori La Pass (3735m). Spectacular rock formations. [citation:7]
Cross Yamda La Pass (3850m). Visit the longest mani wall in Mustang and ancient monastery. [citation:7]
Trek 5-6 hours to Tsarang. Visit 400-year-old Tsarang Gompa and 5-story palace.
Arrive at the walled capital of the former Kingdom of Lo. Explore the walled city. [citation:1]
Explore Royal Palace, Thubchen Gompa (15th-century with stunning murals), Jampa Gompa (oldest monastery with giant Buddha), and Namgyal Gompa. [citation:7]

itinerary.place: Lo Manthang to Chhoser Caves and return
itinerary.description: Day trek to ancient sky caves of Chhoser, 2000-year-old cave dwellings carved into cliffs with ancient Buddhist paintings. [citation:7]
Take alternative route back via Ghar Gompa, one of Mustang's oldest Bon monasteries.
Trek 5-6 hours crossing high passes with stunning Mustang Valley views.
Pass through beautiful red cliffs and desert landscapes.
Final trekking day back to Jomsom. Celebrate completion of Mustang adventure.
Morning flight back to Pokhara. Free afternoon to relax by Phewa Lake. [citation:7]
Drive or fly back to Kathmandu. Free time for souvenir shopping.
Transfer to airport for international departure.

treks.name: Lower Mustang Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000 (Foreigners), NPR 1000 (SAARC), NPR 100 (Nepali); TIMS Card optional [citation:2][citation:8]
treks.cultural_significance: Sacred Hindu and Buddhist pilgrimage site, ancient trading village of Kagbeni, Thakali culture, apple orchards of Marpha [citation:2][citation:8]
treks.description: The Lower Mustang Trek is a short and accessible trek through the beautiful Mustang Valley, offering spectacular mountain scenery, rich Tibetan Buddhist culture, and visits to sacred pilgrimage sites without the expensive permits and restrictions of Upper Mustang. The trek passes through the world's deepest gorge, the Kali Gandaki Gorge, flanked by two 8,000-meter peaks - Dhaulagiri and Annapurna. Highlights include the sacred Muktinath Temple revered by both Hindus and Buddhists, the ancient village of Kagbeni at the gateway to Upper Mustang, and the charming apple orchards of Marpha village famous for its apple brandy. [citation:2][citation:8]
treks.short_description: Accessible cultural trek through the Kali Gandaki Valley with sacred temples, ancient villages, and stunning mountain views.

itinerary.description: Scenic drive or flight to Pokhara. Overnight at lakeside hotel.

itinerary.place: Pokhara to Jomsom (2720m) flight, trek to Kagbeni (2810m)
itinerary.description: Morning flight to Jomsom (20 minutes with mountain views). Trek 3-4 hours to Kagbeni, ancient village at Upper Mustang gateway.
Trek 5-6 hours to sacred Muktinath Temple. Visit the temple with 108 water taps and eternal flame.
Explore Muktinath area, visit Jharkot village with ancient monastery, and Khingar village.
Descend through Kagbeni to Marpha, famous apple capital of Nepal. Visit apple orchards and brandy factory.
Trek to traditional Thakali village of Tukuche. Explore village and local culture.
Short trek through beautiful villages with mountain views.
Trek through deepest gorge with views of Dhaulagiri and Annapurna.
Long descent through dramatic gorges to Tatopani. Relax in natural hot springs.
Morning flight to Pokhara, connect to Kathmandu.

treks.name: Lo Manthang Trek
treks.permit_details: Upper Mustang Restricted Area Permit USD 500 for first 10 days (plus USD 50 per additional day), Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000
treks.cultural_significance: Capital of ancient Kingdom of Lo, richest collection of 15th-century Buddhist Mandalas in the world, preserved Tibetan culture [citation:9]
treks.description: The Lo Manthang Trek focuses on reaching the ancient walled capital of the Kingdom of Lo, the cultural heart of Upper Mustang. Lo Manthang contains the wealthiest surviving collection of 15th-century Buddhist Mandalas in the world, housed within its centuries-old monasteries including Thubchen Gompa and Jampa Gompa. The trek follows the same route as the Upper Mustang Trek but emphasizes exploration of the capital city and its surrounding cultural treasures, including the Mustang King's Palace and the mysterious sky caves of Chhoser. The journey offers stunning views of Mt. Dhaulagiri, Mt. Annapurna, and Nilgiri while passing through traditional Tibetan-style villages. [citation:9]
treks.short_description: Cultural trek focused on the ancient walled capital of Lo Manthang with its 15th-century Buddhist monasteries and royal palace.

itinerary.description: Trek to Kagbeni, gateway to Upper Mustang.
Visit Royal Palace and Thubchen Gompa.
Visit Jampa Gompa, Namgyal Gompa, and local villages.

itinerary.place: Lo Manthang to Chhoser Caves and return
itinerary.description: Day trek to ancient sky caves.

treks.name: Muktinath Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000 [citation:2][citation:4]
treks.cultural_significance: One of the holiest pilgrimage sites for both Hindus and Buddhists, believed to grant salvation [citation:2][citation:4]
treks.description: The Muktinath Trek is a short pilgrimage journey to one of the holiest sites in the Himalayas, revered by both Hindus and Buddhists. Muktinath Temple, situated at 3,710m, is believed to grant salvation (moksha) to those who visit. The temple complex features 108 water taps shaped like cow heads, from which holy water flows, and the Jwala Mai Temple with an eternal flame that has burned for centuries. The trek passes through the world's deepest gorge, the Kali Gandaki Gorge, with spectacular views of Dhaulagiri and Annapurna, and visits the charming villages of Kagbeni and Marpha. [citation:2][citation:4]
treks.short_description: Short pilgrimage trek to the sacred Muktinath Temple with 108 water taps and eternal flame.

itinerary.description: Trek to ancient Kagbeni village at Upper Mustang gateway.
Visit temple complex, 108 water taps, eternal flame.
Descend through Kagbeni to apple capital Marpha.
Trek to Jomsom, fly to Pokhara.

treks.name: Kagbeni Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card optional [citation:4]
treks.cultural_significance: Ancient trading village at the confluence of Kali Gandaki and Jhong Rivers, gateway to Upper Mustang, Tibetan Buddhist culture [citation:4]
treks.description: The Kagbeni Trek is a very easy and short trek to one of the most atmospheric villages in the Himalayas, set in a spectacular landscape of dry eroded valleys and high snow-clad peaks. Kagbeni serves as the gateway to the Upper Mustang region and is a picturesque village of red clay Tibetan-style buildings, narrow alleys, and an ancient monastery. It would be a shame to miss this picturesque village in your rush to get back to Pokhara or Kathmandu. If you have the time, stop and take a break in Kagbeni to explore its rich culture and stunning location at the confluence of the Kali Gandaki and Jhong Rivers. [citation:4]
treks.short_description: Very easy short trek to the picturesque gateway village of Kagbeni with its red clay buildings and ancient monastery.

itinerary.description: Fly to Pokhara then to Jomsom.
Explore village, monastery, and surrounding valleys.
Return to Jomsom, fly to Pokhara.

treks.name: Jomsom Trek
treks.permit_details: No permits required for Jomsom town area, but ACAP required if trekking beyond
treks.cultural_significance: District headquarters of Mustang, Thakali culture, apple farming heritage
treks.description: The Jomsom Trek is a short exploration of the town of Jomsom, the district headquarters of Mustang and the main gateway to both Upper and Lower Mustang. Located on the banks of the Kali Gandaki River in the world's deepest gorge, Jomsom is famous for its apple orchards, apple brandy, and dramatic mountain scenery with Dhaulagiri rising to the west and Nilgiri to the east. This easy trek allows visitors to experience the unique culture of the Thakali people, visit apple brandy factories, explore the Mustang Eco Museum, and enjoy spectacular mountain views without any trekking permits or strenuous walking.
treks.short_description: Short cultural exploration of Jomsom, the apple capital of Nepal with spectacular mountain views.

itinerary.description: Fly to Pokhara, then scenic 20-minute flight to Jomsom.
Explore Jomsom bazaar, visit apple orchards, apple brandy factory, and Mustang Eco Museum.
Morning flight to Pokhara, connect to Kathmandu.

treks.name: Damodar Kunda Trek
treks.permit_details: Upper Mustang Restricted Area Permit USD 500 for first 10 days (plus USD 50 per additional day), Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000
treks.cultural_significance: Sacred Hindu pilgrimage site, believed that bathing in the lake brings salvation, restricted area opened in 2002 [citation:6]
treks.description: The Damodar Kunda Trek is an adventurous and remote pilgrimage trek to a sacred high-altitude lake in Upper Mustang, near the Tibetan border. Opened to trekkers in 2002, Damodar Kunda at 4,890m is revered by both Hindus and Buddhists, with legend holding that bathing in its waters brings salvation. This challenging trek requires full camping support from Yara village onward and a special restricted area permit. The journey follows the classic Upper Mustang trail to Lo Manthang before continuing northeast to the sacred lake through remote valleys and high passes. Trekkers are rewarded with stunning views of the Annapurna and Dhaulagiri ranges, ancient monasteries, and a profound spiritual experience in one of Nepal's most remote regions. [citation:6]
treks.short_description: Remote pilgrimage trek to sacred Damodar Kunda lake in Upper Mustang with stunning mountain views and spiritual significance.

itinerary.description: Trek to Kagbeni, gateway to Upper Mustang.

itinerary.place: Yara to Damodar Kunda Base Camp (4300m)
Base Camp to Damodar Kunda (4900m) and return
itinerary.description: Early morning trek to sacred lake. Return to base camp. [citation:6]

treks.name: Mustang Circuit Trek
treks.permit_details: Upper Mustang Restricted Area Permit USD 500 for first 10 days (plus USD 50 per additional day), Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000
treks.cultural_significance: Complete circuit of the Mustang region combining both Lower and Upper Mustang highlights with sacred lakes and ancient villages [citation:6]
treks.description: The Mustang Circuit Trek is a comprehensive journey that combines the highlights of both Lower and Upper Mustang into one complete circuit, including the remote pilgrimage site of Damodar Kunda. This challenging expedition follows the classic Upper Mustang route to Lo Manthang, then continues northeast to the sacred Damodar Kunda at 4,890m before returning through the beautiful villages of Yara, Tetang, and Muktinath. The circuit offers the most complete experience of Mustang's unique Tibetan Buddhist culture, ancient monasteries, dramatic desert landscapes, and sacred Hindu pilgrimage sites, all set against the stunning backdrop of the Annapurna and Dhaulagiri ranges.
treks.short_description: Complete circuit of the Mustang region combining all major highlights including Lo Manthang, Damodar Kunda, and Muktinath.

itinerary.place: Yara to Damodar Kunda Base Camp (4300m)
Base Camp to Damodar Kunda (4900m) and return

treks.name: Api Himal Base Camp Trek
treks.permit_details: Api Nampa Conservation Area Permit, Darchula Restricted Area Permit (USD 90/week for Byas VDC area)
treks.cultural_significance: Indigenous communities including Raute nomads, traditional mountain culture, unclimbed sacred peak
treks.description: The Api Himal Base Camp Trek is one of Nepal's most remote and challenging treks, leading to the base of Mount Api (7,132m), the highest peak in the Far Western region. This expedition-style trek takes you through pristine wilderness, lush forests of rhododendron and oak, and traditional villages where time seems to have stood still. The region is home to diverse ethnic communities including the nomadic Raute people. The trek offers spectacular views of Api, Nampa, and Bobaye peaks, and includes a visit to the sacred Kali Dhunga Lake. With full camping required and minimal infrastructure, this trek offers a true wilderness adventure for experienced trekkers seeking solitude and pristine Himalayan landscapes. [citation:1][citation:9]
treks.short_description: Extreme remote trek to the base of sacred Mount Api in far-western Nepal with pristine wilderness and traditional villages.

itinerary.description: Fly from Kathmandu to Dhangadhi (1 hour 15 minutes). Transfer to hotel. Visit Jokhar Lake and botanical garden.
Scenic 8-hour drive along Mahakali Highway through Dadeldhura, the 'Queen of Hills'. Stunning mountain views.
Drive 2 hours to Latinath, then trek 1 hour to Paribagar. Homestay in traditional village.
Trek 5-6 hours through terraced fields and subtropical forests along Chameliya River. Camping.
Challenging 8-hour trek with steep ascents through oak and rhododendron forests. Camping.
Trek 7 hours through Khayekot, the last permanent settlement. Confluence of Chameliya and Rokap rivers. Camping.
Challenging 5-hour ascent through open meadows and caves to lower base camp. Camping.

itinerary.place: Dhaulo Odar to Api Base Camp (3861m) and return
itinerary.description: 2.5-hour ascent to Api Base Camp. Spectacular views of Api, Nampa, and Bobaye. Visit Kali Dhunga holy lake. Return to Dhaulo Odar. Camping. [citation:9]
Long descent retracing route back to Siti. Camping.
Continue descent through Pato village. Camping.
4-hour trek to Latinath, then drive 2 hours to Gokuleshwor. Lodge.
Morning flight back to Kathmandu. Free afternoon.
Transfer to airport for international departure.

treks.name: Saipal Himal Base Camp Trek
treks.permit_details: Api Nampa Conservation Area Permit, Bajhang Restricted Area Permit (USD 90/week for Saipal area)
treks.cultural_significance: Remote mountain communities, Khas and Bhotia cultures, Tibetan Buddhist heritage
treks.description: The Saipal Himal Base Camp Trek is one of Nepal's most remote and challenging expeditions, leading to the base of the majestic Saipal Himal (7,031m) in the far-western region. This strenuous trek begins with a flight to Simikot and follows pristine trails through dense forests, alpine meadows, and remote villages untouched by modernization. The route crosses the challenging Sakya Lagna Pass (4,700m) and Khadikhani Pass, offering spectacular panoramic views of Saipal, Api, Nampa, and distant peaks. Trekkers visit both the East and North Base Camps, experiencing true wilderness and authentic cultural encounters with Khas and Bhotia communities. [citation:1][citation:10]
treks.short_description: Extreme expedition trek to the remote base camps of Saipal Himal with challenging high passes and pristine wilderness.

itinerary.description: Flight to Nepalgunj. Overnight at hotel.
Scenic mountain flight to Simikot. Explore the village, meet local team.
Trek through pine forests and suspension bridges. Camping.
Trek along Humla Karnali River, visit Lakiyo Gompa and hot springs. Camping.
Pass buckwheat fields and pine forests. Visit Namkh Kyung Dzong monastery. Camping.
Trek through beautiful landscapes with first views of Saipal range. Camping.
Follow Kalungba River through Karang Valley. Views of Saipal Glacier. Camping.

itinerary.place: Karang Khola to Saipal East Base Camp (4250m)
itinerary.description: Challenging trek to East Base Camp with spectacular views of Saipal, Api, and Nandadevi. Camping. [citation:1]

itinerary.place: Rest and Exploration Day at Base Camp
itinerary.description: Hike to nearby vantage points. Explore Saipal Glacier and surrounding peaks. Camping.
Return trek through open meadows. Camping.

itinerary.place: Lampato to Khadikhani Pass (4700m) to Rani Kharka (4100m)
itinerary.description: Cross Khadikhani Pass with sweeping views of Saipal range. Camping.

itinerary.place: Rani Kharka to Saipal North Base Camp (5000m) and return
itinerary.description: Trek to North Base Camp for spectacular glacier views. Return to Rani Kharka. Camping.

itinerary.place: Rani Kharka to Sakya Lagna Pass (4700m) to Chala
itinerary.description: Cross highest pass. Descend to Chala. Camping. [citation:10]
Return trek through forests. Visit Yalbang Monastery. Camping.
Final trekking day. Celebrate completion. Lodge.

treks.name: Khaptad National Park Trek
treks.permit_details: Khaptad National Park Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Spiritual site of Khaptad Baba, Hindu pilgrimage, traditional Khas and Thakuri culture
treks.description: The Khaptad National Park Trek explores a beautiful high-altitude plateau in far-western Nepal, known for its rolling green hills, sacred lakes, and the ashram of the late spiritual saint Khaptad Baba. This unique trek combines pristine nature with spiritual tranquility, passing through forests of rhododendron, oak, and pine that are home to over 260 bird species and diverse wildlife. The trek includes visits to the Khaptad Baba Ashram, the sacred Khaptad Daha Lake, and the Sahasra Linga temple. The region offers spectacular views of the Api and Saipal Himalayan ranges on clear days. [citation:2]
treks.short_description: Spiritual and nature trek through the pristine meadows of Khaptad National Park with sacred lakes and diverse wildlife.

itinerary.description: Fly to Dhangadhi. Transfer to hotel.
Drive through hills to Bajhang. Overnight in village.
Begin trek into Khaptad National Park. Camping.
Visit Khaptad Baba Ashram, museum, Khaptad Daha Lake, and tower. Sunset views. Camping. [citation:2]
Trek through rhododendron forests. Option to visit Sahasra Linga temple. Camping.
Descend through pine forests, passing waterfall. Camping.
Trek to district headquarters of Doti. Lodge.

treks.name: Rara Lake Trek
treks.permit_details: Rara National Park Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Thakuri and Malla communities, traditional villages, Rara National Park
treks.description: The Rara Lake Trek takes you to Nepal's largest and most pristine alpine lake, a shimmering sapphire set amidst pine-covered hills and snow-capped peaks in the remote northwest. Often described as Nepal's hidden gem, Rara Lake offers a serene experience far from the crowded trekking routes. The trek passes through Rara National Park, rich in flora and fauna including red pandas, musk deer, and over 200 bird species. The 5.1 km long and 2.7 km wide lake sits at 2,990m, surrounded by forests of pine, spruce, and juniper. The trek includes an optional ascent of Chuchemara Peak (4,039m) for spectacular panoramic views. [citation:3][citation:4]
treks.short_description: Trek to Nepal's largest and most pristine alpine lake, a hidden gem with stunning turquoise waters and rich wildlife.

itinerary.description: Flight to Nepalgunj. Overnight at hotel.
Morning flight to Jumla. Prepare for trek.
Trek through pine forests to Danphe Lagna. Camping/teahouse.
Arrive at stunning Rara Lake. Explore lakeshore. Lodge.
Hike to Chuchemara Peak (4039m) for panoramic views. Explore lake. Lodge. [citation:4]

treks.name: Rara Ghorasain Trek
treks.permit_details: Rara National Park Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Ancient Khas Kingdom capital at Sinja Valley, Thakuri culture, traditional villages
treks.description: The Rara Ghorasain Trek combines a visit to Nepal's largest lake with an ascent of Ghorasain Peak for spectacular panoramic views of the Himalayas. This extended trek explores the remote beauty of far-western Nepal with pristine forests, authentic village experiences, and a visit to the historic Sinja Valley, the ancient capital of the Khas Kingdom. The trek crosses the Jaljala Pass and offers stunning views of the Saipal, Api, and Kanjiroba ranges. This challenging route provides a more comprehensive exploration of the Karnali region, combining natural beauty with rich cultural heritage.
treks.short_description: Extended Rara trek with peak ascent for panoramic Himalayan views and visit to ancient Khas Kingdom capital.

itinerary.description: Descend to Bota via Sinja Khola. Visit Sinja Valley ruins.

itinerary.place: Rara Lake to Ghorasain Peak (4200m) to Talcha
itinerary.description: Ascend Ghorasain Peak for sunrise. Descend to Talcha.

treks.name: Jumla Rara Lake Trek
treks.permit_details: Rara National Park Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Khas culture, traditional villages, apple farming heritage
treks.description: The Jumla Rara Lake Trek is a classic route combining a journey through the historical Jumla Valley with a visit to Nepal's largest and most pristine alpine lake. This trek offers stunning lake and mountain views, diverse wildlife, and a glimpse into the traditional lifestyle of the Karnali region. The route passes through apple orchards, traditional villages, and crosses the Danphe Lagna Pass before descending to the turquoise waters of Rara Lake. The trek is less crowded than popular routes and offers excellent opportunities for photography and cultural immersion.
treks.short_description: Classic Karnali trek combining the historical Jumla Valley with Nepal's largest lake and traditional village life.

itinerary.description: Trek through apple orchards and villages.

treks.name: Mugu Karnali Trek
treks.permit_details: Mugu Restricted Area Permit USD 90 for first 7 days + USD 15/day, Rara National Park Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Khas and Tibetan-influenced culture, remote mountain communities
treks.description: The Mugu Karnali Trek follows the mighty Karnali River through the remote Mugu district, one of Nepal's most isolated regions. This restricted area trek offers a true wilderness experience, combining dramatic river valley scenery with high mountain passes and pristine forests. The route passes through traditional villages where time seems to have stood still, with opportunities to encounter rare wildlife including snow leopards. The trek can be combined with a visit to Rara Lake for a longer expedition. This is an adventure for experienced trekkers seeking solitude and raw Himalayan nature. [citation:6]
treks.short_description: Wilderness trek following Nepal's longest river through remote Mugu with pristine forests and rare wildlife.

treks.name: Humla Limi Valley Trek
treks.permit_details: Humla Restricted Area Permit USD 50 for first 7 days + USD 7/day, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Ancient Tibetan Buddhist enclave, preserved Bon and Buddhist culture, sacred pilgrimage route
treks.description: The Humla Limi Valley Trek is a journey into one of Nepal's most isolated and culturally rich regions, nestled in the trans-Himalayan valley of Humla near the Tibetan border. Opened to foreigners only in 2002, this restricted area offers a rare glimpse into a timeless Tibetan Buddhist culture preserved for centuries. The trek follows ancient salt trading routes through dramatic landscapes, crossing high passes including Nyalu La (5,018m), and visiting ancient monasteries in villages like Yalbang. On clear days, there are spectacular views of Mount Kailash across the border in Tibet. This expedition-style trek requires full camping support and offers profound cultural immersion for the most dedicated adventurers. [citation:7]
treks.short_description: Journey to a hidden Tibetan Buddhist valley in remote Humla with ancient monasteries and views of Mount Kailash.

itinerary.description: Scenic flight to Simikot. Explore village. Lodge.
Begin trek through pine forests. Camping.
Visit Lakiyo Gompa and hot springs. Camping.
Visit Namkh Kyung Dzong monastery. Camping.
Trek through medicinal plant fields. Camping.
Trek to village near Tibetan border. Camping.

itinerary.place: Manepeme to Nyalu La Base Camp (4500m)

itinerary.description: Early morning pass crossing. Descent. Camping. [citation:7]

treks.name: Karnali Corridor Trek
treks.permit_details: Humla Restricted Area Permit USD 50/week, Mugu Restricted Area Permit USD 90/week, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Multi-ethnic cultures, traditional mountain lifestyle, ancient trade routes
treks.description: The Karnali Corridor Trek follows Nepal's longest river through remote western districts, offering a true wilderness experience through some of the country's most isolated regions. This challenging trek connects Humla and Mugu districts, passing through traditional villages and pristine forests with minimal tourist infrastructure. The route offers spectacular views of the Himalayas, encounters with diverse ethnic communities, and opportunities to see rare wildlife including snow leopards and blue sheep. This trek is for experienced adventurers seeking authentic cultural immersion and untouched landscapes.
treks.short_description: Remote wilderness trek along Nepal's longest river through traditional villages and pristine forests.

itinerary.place: Yari to Nyalu La Base Camp (4500m)

treks.name: Badimalika Temple Trek
treks.permit_details: Khaptad National Park Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Important Hindu pilgrimage site, Janai Purnima festival, traditional Khas culture
treks.description: The Badimalika Temple Trek is a challenging pilgrimage journey to one of Nepal's most sacred Hindu temples, located at 4,200m on a hilltop in the Bajura district. The temple, dedicated to Goddess Bhagwati, attracts thousands of pilgrims during the Janai Purnima festival in August. The trek offers stunning views of Api and Saipal Himal, passes through the beautiful Khaptad National Park with its rich biodiversity, and provides authentic cultural encounters in traditional villages. The route is also home to over 250 bird species and diverse wildlife including musk deer and leopards. [citation:8]
treks.short_description: Challenging pilgrimage trek to sacred Badimalika Temple with Himalayan views and Khaptad National Park wildlife.

itinerary.description: Challenging ascent to temple area. Camping. [citation:8]
Visit temple, sacred lake, enjoy mountain views.

treks.name: Darchula Byas Trek
treks.permit_details: Darchula Restricted Area Permit USD 90 for first 7 days + USD 15/day, Api Nampa Conservation Area Permit
treks.cultural_significance: Indigenous communities, traditional mountain lifestyle, restricted area
treks.description: The Darchula Byas Trek explores the remote Byas Valley in far-western Nepal, a restricted area rich in biodiversity and traditional culture. The trek follows the Chameliya River through pristine forests and traditional villages, offering stunning views of the Api and Saipal Himal ranges. This area is known for its incredible biodiversity, with dense forests of rhododendron, oak, and pine, and opportunities to spot rare wildlife including snow leopards, red pandas, and Himalayan tahrs. The trek provides authentic cultural encounters with indigenous communities whose traditional lifestyle remains largely unchanged. [citation:6]
treks.short_description: Remote valley trek in far-western Nepal with stunning mountain views, rich biodiversity, and traditional villages.

itinerary.place: High Camp to Viewpoint (4500m) and return
itinerary.description: Hike for Api and Saipal views. Return to High Camp.

treks.name: Yari Khaptad Trek
treks.permit_details: Khaptad National Park Permit NPR 3000, Bajhang Restricted Area Permit, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Multi-ethnic communities, traditional mountain lifestyle
treks.description: The Yari Khaptad Trek explores the remote Khaptad National Park from its northern approach, passing through traditional villages and pristine forests. This challenging route offers stunning alpine meadows, sacred lakes, and a true wilderness experience far from tourist crowds. The trek provides spectacular views of the Api and Saipal Himalayan ranges and opportunities to encounter diverse wildlife including musk deer and Himalayan black bears. The Yari region is known for its rich cultural heritage and traditional mountain communities whose lifestyle has remained unchanged for generations.
treks.short_description: Remote Khaptad trek from the north through pristine forests, alpine meadows, and traditional villages.

itinerary.description: Trek to main plateau area. Camping.
Visit sacred lakes, ashram, viewpoints. Camping.

treks.name: Simikot Trek
treks.permit_details: Humla Restricted Area Permit USD 50 for first 7 days + USD 7/day, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Gateway to Humla and Limi Valley, Tibetan Buddhist culture, ancient trade route
treks.description: The Simikot Trek is a short exploration of the area around Simikot, the district headquarters of Humla and the main gateway to the remote Limi Valley and other far-western treks. This gentle trek offers beautiful views of the surrounding Himalayas, visits to Buddhist monasteries, and opportunities to experience the unique Tibetan-influenced culture of the region. Short hikes lead to nearby villages, hot springs at Kermi, and viewpoints overlooking the Humla Karnali River valley. This trek is perfect for acclimatization before longer expeditions or for those wanting a taste of remote Humla without extended trekking.
treks.short_description: Short cultural trek exploring Simikot and surrounding areas, gateway to remote Humla region.

itinerary.description: Fly to Nepalgunj then Simikot. Explore town.
Trek to Kermi, visit hot springs and monastery.

treks.name: Rolwaling Valley Trek
treks.permit_details: Gaurishankar Conservation Area Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Sacred valley known as the 'Graveyard of the Himalayas' in Sherpa mythology, ancient Tibetan Buddhist culture, homeland of legendary Sherpa climbers[citation:1][citation:2]
treks.description: The Rolwaling Valley Trek takes you into a wild, remote high valley nestled between steep ice giants on the Tibetan border. Known by Sherpas as the 'Graveyard of the Himalayas' for its challenging terrain, this mystical valley is believed to be the homeland of the Yeti and the ancestral route of the Sherpa people. The trek passes through traditional Sherpa villages including Simigaon, Beding, and Na Gaon, offering breathtaking views of Mount Gaurishankar, Rolwaling Himal, and other peaks. The journey includes day hikes to Tsho Rolpa Lake and Yalung Ri Base Camp, providing spectacular glacier views and immersion in ancient Tibetan Buddhist culture.[citation:1][citation:2][citation:8]
treks.short_description: Mystical Sherpa valley trek with stunning mountain views, glacial lakes, and ancient Tibetan Buddhist culture.

itinerary.description: Drive 7-8 hours from Kathmandu to Jagat via Charikot and Singati. Overnight at teahouse.
Trek 6-7 hours through forests and terraced fields. First views of Gaurishankar. Overnight in Sherpa village.
Trek 6-7 hours through dense mixed forests. Cross suspension bridges and enter Rolwaling Valley. Overnight at riverside teahouse.
Trek 6 hours through lush alpine vegetation with spectacular mountain scenery. Overnight in Beding, the largest Sherpa village in the valley.
Rest day. Hike to Beding Monastery (Chorkyang) perched on cliffs with stunning views. Explore village.
Trek 5 hours through rocky terrain and yak pastures to Na Gaon, the highest settlement in the valley.

itinerary.place: Na Gaon to Tsho Rolpa Lake (4580m) and return
itinerary.description: Day hike to Nepal's largest glacial lake. Spectacular turquoise waters with Trakarding Glacier backdrop. Return to Na Gaon.

itinerary.place: Na Gaon to Yalung Ri Base Camp (5067m) and return
itinerary.description: Challenging 8-hour day hike to base camp with panoramic mountain views. Return to Na Gaon.
Descend 4-5 hours back to Dokhang. Overnight at teahouse.
Trek 4 hours to Chhetchhet. Drive back to Kathmandu.

treks.name: Tso Rolpa Lake Trek
treks.permit_details: Gaurishankar Conservation Area Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
attractions: Tsho Rolpa Lake (Nepal's largest glacial lake)
treks.cultural_significance: Sherpa and Tamang villages, Buddhist monasteries, traditional mountain lifestyle
treks.description: The Tsho Rolpa Lake Trek leads to Nepal's largest glacial lake, a stunning turquoise gem nestled at 4,580m in the remote Rolwaling Valley. 'Tsho' means lake and 'Rolpa' indicates the Rolwaling valley - together creating one of the country's most spectacular hidden treasures. Created by glacial melting, the lake stretches approximately 3.5 km long with the massive Trakarding Glacier as its backdrop. This off-the-beaten-path trek offers breathtaking views of Mount Gaurishankar, pristine forests, traditional Sherpa villages, and the famous second cantilever bridge of Nepal. Perfect for those seeking solitude and raw natural beauty away from crowded trekking routes.[citation:3][citation:10]
treks.short_description: Trek to Nepal's largest glacial lake with turquoise waters, Gaurishankar views, and authentic Sherpa villages.

itinerary.description: Early morning 7-8 hour drive to Chhetchhet via Charikot and Singati. Trek 2-3 hours steep ascent to Simigaun. Overnight at teahouse.[citation:10]
Trek 6-7 hours through lush forest. Cross second cantilever bridge at Surmuche. Pass Kyalche village. Overnight at Dongang.
Trek 5-6 hours through alpine terrain. Spectacular views of Gaurishankar. Overnight at Beding, the largest Sherpa village.
Rest day. Visit Beding Monastery. Explore village. Short acclimatization hikes.
Trek 6-7 hours through rocky terrain and yak pastures. Overnight at Na Gaon.

itinerary.place: Na Gaon to Tsho Rolpa Lake (4580m) and return to Beding
itinerary.description: Day hike to Tsho Rolpa Lake. Explore frozen turquoise waters and Trakarding Glacier. Return to Beding.[citation:3]
Descend 6-7 hours back to Dongang. Overnight at teahouse.
Trek 2-3 hours to Chhetchhet. Drive 7-8 hours back to Kathmandu.
Transfer to airport for international departure.

treks.name: Gaurishankar Base Camp Trek
treks.permit_details: Gaurishankar Conservation Area Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Sacred mountain named after Hindu deities Gauri (Parvati) and Shankar (Shiva), unclimbed peak due to religious significance, Tibetan Buddhist culture
treks.description: The Gaurishankar Base Camp Trek takes you to the foot of the sacred Mount Gaurishankar (7,145m), one of Nepal's most beautiful and revered peaks. Named after the Hindu deities Gauri (Parvati) and Shankar (Shiva), this mountain has never been climbed due to its religious significance. The trek follows ancient trade routes through remote valleys, passing traditional Sherpa and Tamang villages, ancient monasteries including Bigu Gompa, and offering spectacular views of the Rolwaling Himal range. The journey combines rich cultural encounters with pristine mountain wilderness in the Gaurishankar Conservation Area.[citation:1]
treks.short_description: Trek to the base of sacred Mount Gaurishankar with ancient monasteries and spectacular Rolwaling Himal views.

itinerary.place: Na Gaon to Gaurishankar Base Camp (5200m) and return
itinerary.description: Challenging day hike to base camp.

treks.name: Dolakha Kalinchowk Trek
treks.permit_details: Gaurishankar Conservation Area Permit NPR 3000
treks.cultural_significance: Sacred Hindu temple dedicated to Goddess Bhagwati, believed to fulfill pilgrims' wishes, multi-cultural communities including Sherpa, Tamang, Brahmin, and Chhetri[citation:5]
treks.description: The Dolakha Kalinchowk Trek is a short and popular pilgrimage trek to the famous Kalinchowk Bhagwati Temple, perched at 3,842m in the Dolakha district. This sacred Hindu shrine dedicated to Goddess Bhagwati is believed to fulfill the prayers and desires of devotees. Beyond its religious significance, the trek offers spectacular panoramic views of the Himalayas including Everest, Gaurishankar, Langtang, Jugal, and Ganesh ranges. The trail passes through beautiful rhododendron forests and multi-cultural villages where Sherpa, Tamang, Brahmin, and Chhetri communities live in harmony. Winter trekkers enjoy snow-covered landscapes, making it a popular weekend escape from Kathmandu.[citation:5]
treks.short_description: Short pilgrimage trek to sacred Kalinchowk Temple with panoramic Himalayan views and winter snow.

itinerary.description: Scenic 6-hour drive from Kathmandu to Charikot, district headquarters of Dolakha. Explore local culture and lifestyle. Overnight at lodge.[citation:5]
Trek 5 hours through impressive landscapes with stunning Himalayan views, terraced fields, and traditional villages. Overnight at Kuri village.

itinerary.place: Kuri to Kalinchowk Temple (3842m) to Charikot
itinerary.description: Early morning 1.5-hour hike to Kalinchowk Temple for sunrise and panoramic mountain views. Visit temple, then descend to Kuri and drive to Charikot.
Visit Dolakha Bhimsen Temple before driving back to Kathmandu.

treks.name: Panch Pokhari Trek
treks.permit_details: Gaurishankar Conservation Area Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Sacred Hindu pilgrimage site, Janai Purnima festival, Tamang and Sherpa communities
treks.description: The Panch Pokhari Trek leads to five sacred high-altitude lakes, one of Nepal's most important Hindu pilgrimage sites. Located in the remote reaches of Sindhupalchok district, these lakes are believed to be the abode of gods and attract thousands of pilgrims during the Janai Purnima festival in August. The trek offers stunning views of the Jugal Himal, Gaurishankar, Langtang, and Rolwaling ranges, passing through beautiful rhododendron forests and traditional Tamang and Sherpa villages. This off-the-beaten-path route provides a perfect blend of spiritual significance, natural beauty, and authentic cultural encounters.[citation:7]
treks.short_description: Pilgrimage trek to five sacred lakes with stunning Himalayan views and authentic Tamang culture.

itinerary.description: Drive 5-6 hours to Chautara. Overnight at guesthouse.
Trek 5-6 hours through villages and farmlands.
Trek through rhododendron and pine forests.
Beautiful forest trail with yak herding settlements.
Trek 6-7 hours to sacred five lakes. Stunning panoramic views.
Explore lakes, visit shrines, enjoy mountain views.

treks.name: Panch Pokhari Thodung Trek
treks.permit_details: Gaurishankar Conservation Area Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Sacred Hindu pilgrimage lakes, historic Thodung cheese factory established with Swiss aid, Sherpa culture
treks.description: The Panch Pokhari Thodung Trek combines the sacred five lakes of Panch Pokhari with the historic Thodung area, once the gateway to Everest. This challenging trek crosses the Gyajo La Pass (4,880m) and offers spectacular views of Gaurishankar, Langtang, and Jugal Himal ranges. The route passes through pristine rhododendron forests, traditional Sherpa villages, and the Thodung monastery with its famous cheese factory established with Swiss aid in the 1950s. This off-the-beaten-path trek provides a unique blend of spiritual significance, mountain wilderness, and authentic cultural experiences.[citation:6]
treks.short_description: Challenging trek combining sacred Panch Pokhari lakes with historic Thodung and Gaurishankar views.

itinerary.description: Drive to Shivalaya, starting point of classic Everest route.
Challenging day crossing Panch Pokhari Pass. Arrive at sacred lakes.
Explore five sacred lakes. Enjoy mountain panoramas.

itinerary.place: Panch Pokhari to Ngeju Kharka (4100m) via Gyajo La Pass (4880m)
itinerary.description: Early start to cross Gyajo La Pass. Descent to Ngeju.
Trek to Thodung. Visit monastery and cheese factory.

treks.name: Thodung Trek
treks.permit_details: Gaurishankar Conservation Area Permit NPR 3000
treks.cultural_significance: Historic cheese factory established with Swiss aid in 1957, Thodung monastery, traditional Sherpa culture[citation:6]
treks.description: The Thodung Trek is a cultural journey to the historic Thodung area, famous for its Swiss-established cheese factory dating back to 1957 and its ancient Buddhist monastery. Located in the Gaurishankar Conservation Area, this region was once the gateway to Everest before the Lukla airstrip was built. The trek passes through beautiful rhododendron forests, traditional Sherpa villages, and offers spectacular views of Gaurishankar and the surrounding peaks. Visitors can sample authentic yak cheese, visit the monastery, and experience traditional Sherpa hospitality in this off-the-beaten-path destination.
treks.short_description: Cultural trek to historic Thodung with Swiss cheese factory, ancient monastery, and Gaurishankar views.

itinerary.description: Trek 5-6 hours through forests. Arrive at Thodung.
Visit cheese factory and monastery. Short hikes.

treks.name: Chisapani Trek
treks.permit_details: No permits required for this short trek
treks.cultural_significance: Tamang and Newar villages, traditional mountain lifestyle
treks.description: The Chisapani Trek is a short and easy weekend escape from Kathmandu, offering beautiful sunrise views over the Langtang and Gaurishankar ranges. Starting from Sundarijal at the edge of the valley, the trail passes through Shivapuri National Park with its lush forests, waterfalls, and diverse bird life. Chisapani ('Cold Water') village perched at 2,215m offers spectacular mountain panoramas and a peaceful retreat from the city. This trek is perfect for those with limited time or as a warm-up for longer treks in the region.[citation:7]
treks.short_description: Short weekend trek from Kathmandu with stunning sunrise views over the Langtang and Gaurishankar ranges.

itinerary.description: Drive 1 hour to Sundarijal. Trek 5-6 hours through Shivapuri National Park. Overnight at Chisapani.[citation:7]
Early morning sunrise views. Explore surrounding area and villages.
Trek back to Sundarijal or drive alternative route. Return to Kathmandu.

treks.name: Jugal Himal Trek
treks.permit_details: TIMS Card NPR 2000, Local Permit NPR 1000-1500
treks.cultural_significance: Tamang and Sherpa communities, traditional mountain lifestyle, sacred lakes[citation:7]
treks.description: The Jugal Himal Trek is a short yet scenic journey to the remote Jugal Himal range, located northeast of Kathmandu. This less-crowded trek offers breathtaking views of Dorje Lakpa, Gaurishankar, Langtang, and the Jugal Himal peaks, along with a spiritual visit to the sacred Panch Pokhari lakes. The trail passes through unspoiled rhododendron forests, traditional Tamang and Sherpa villages, and offers authentic homestay experiences with warm mountain hospitality. Perfect for those seeking peace, solitude, and genuine cultural encounters away from commercial trekking routes.[citation:7]
treks.short_description: Short scenic trek to Jugal Himal with sacred lakes, Himalayan views, and authentic Tamang culture.

itinerary.description: Drive 5-6 hours to Chautara. Overnight at guesthouse.[citation:7]
Trek 5-6 hours through villages and farmlands.
Trek through rhododendron and pine forests.
Beautiful forest trail with yak herding settlements.
Trek 6-7 hours to sacred five lakes. Stunning panoramic views.
Descend to Chautara. Drive back to Kathmandu.

treks.name: Dhaulagiri Circuit Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000 (restricted area permit not required as route starts from ACAP area) [citation:1]
treks.cultural_significance: Remote mountain wilderness, traditional Magar and Thakali villages, Marpha apple orchards [citation:1]
treks.description: The Dhaulagiri Circuit Trek is one of Nepal's most challenging and remote treks, circling the seventh-highest mountain in the world, Mount Dhaulagiri (8,167m). This expedition-style trek crosses two high passes, French Pass (5,360m) and Dhampus Pass (5,200m), and traverses the stunning Hidden Valley, a remote alpine plateau surrounded by snow-capped peaks. The journey begins in Beni and follows the Myagdi Khola through lush forests, remote villages, and glacial moraines before reaching Dhaulagiri Base Camp. After crossing the challenging French Pass, trekkers descend through the world's deepest gorge, the Kali Gandaki, to the apple orchards of Marpha and conclude in Jomsom. This trek requires full camping arrangements, technical skills for glacier travel, and excellent physical fitness. [citation:1][citation:3]
treks.short_description: Extreme expedition circling the 7th highest mountain with challenging high passes and the remote Hidden Valley.

itinerary.description: Scenic 8-9 hour drive from Kathmandu to Beni, the district headquarters of Myagdi. Overnight at lodge.
Trek 5-6 hours through villages and forests. Visit natural hot springs at Tatopani.
Trek 6-7 hours through valleys and villages.
Trek 5-6 hours to large Magar village. Spectacular mountain views.
Trek 6-7 hours through forests and villages.
Trek 5-6 hours. Last permanent settlement before entering wilderness.
Trek 5-6 hours through dense forests. Camping.

itinerary.place: Choriban Khola to Italian Base Camp (3660m)
itinerary.description: Challenging 7-8 hour trek to Italian Base Camp. First views of Dhaulagiri.
Rest day for acclimatization. Short hikes around camp.

itinerary.place: Italian Base Camp to Glacier Camp (4210m)
itinerary.description: Trek 6-7 hours through glacial moraines. Camping on glacier.

itinerary.place: Glacier Camp to Dhaulagiri Base Camp (4740m)
itinerary.description: Challenging 7-8 hour trek across glacier. Stunning close-up views of Dhaulagiri.
Rest day. Explore base camp area. Spectacular mountain views.

itinerary.place: Dhaulagiri Base Camp to Hidden Valley (5200m) via French Pass (5360m)
itinerary.description: Early morning start to cross French Pass. Descent to Hidden Valley. Camping.
Explore Hidden Valley, a remote alpine plateau surrounded by peaks.

itinerary.place: Hidden Valley to Yak Kharka (4200m) via Dhampus Pass (5200m)
itinerary.description: Cross Dhampus Pass. Descent to Yak Kharka. Camping.
Long descent to Marpha, famous for apple orchards and apple brandy.
Short trek to Jomsom. Celebrate completion of trek.
Morning flight to Pokhara. Free afternoon at Phewa Lake.
Drive or fly back to Kathmandu.
Transfer to airport for international departure.

treks.name: Dhaulagiri Base Camp Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Remote mountain wilderness, traditional Magar villages
treks.description: The Dhaulagiri Base Camp Trek is a challenging expedition to the base of the world's seventh-highest mountain, Mount Dhaulagiri. This trek follows the same route as the full circuit but ends at Dhaulagiri Base Camp without crossing the French Pass. The journey offers spectacular close-up views of Dhaulagiri's massive south face, the Chhonbardan Glacier, and surrounding peaks. Trekkers pass through traditional Magar villages, dense forests, and glacial moraines before reaching the awe-inspiring base camp at 4,740m. This trek requires full camping arrangements and is suitable for experienced trekkers seeking a high-altitude adventure without the additional challenges of crossing high passes. [citation:1][citation:9]
treks.short_description: Challenging expedition to the base of the 7th highest mountain with spectacular glacier views.

itinerary.place: Choriban Khola to Italian Base Camp (3660m)
Italian Base Camp to Glacier Camp (4210m)
