Glacier Camp to Dhaulagiri Base Camp (4740m)

treks.name: French Pass Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Remote mountain wilderness, named after French mountaineering expeditions
treks.description: The French Pass Trek focuses on crossing the spectacular French Pass (5,360m), one of the highest and most challenging trekking passes in Nepal. Named after French mountaineering expeditions, this pass offers breathtaking panoramic views of the Dhaulagiri massif, including Dhaulagiri I, II, III, and V, as well as Tukche Peak. The trek follows the classic Dhaulagiri Circuit route, crossing the pass to descend into the remote Hidden Valley before continuing to Jomsom. This challenging expedition requires technical skills for glacier travel and excellent acclimatization. [citation:1][citation:2]
treks.short_description: Extreme pass crossing trek focusing on the spectacular French Pass (5360m) with Dhaulagiri panoramas.

itinerary.place: Italian Base Camp to Glacier Camp (4210m)
Glacier Camp to Dhaulagiri Base Camp (4740m)
Base Camp to Hidden Valley (5200m) via French Pass (5360m)
itinerary.description: Early morning pass crossing. Spectacular views.

itinerary.place: Hidden Valley to Yak Kharka (4200m) via Dhampus Pass (5200m)

treks.name: Hidden Valley Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Isolated alpine valley, traditional trans-Himalayan grazing lands
treks.description: The Hidden Valley Trek focuses on exploring the remote and spectacular Hidden Valley, a high-altitude plateau surrounded by the peaks of the Dhaulagiri massif. This isolated valley, accessible only via the French Pass, offers a true wilderness experience with stunning 360-degree mountain views. The valley was once used as grazing land by yak herders and remains one of the most pristine and untouched areas in the Nepalese Himalayas. This challenging trek follows the Dhaulagiri Circuit route, spending extra time exploring the valley's unique alpine ecosystem. [citation:1][citation:2]
treks.short_description: Remote wilderness trek focusing on the stunning Hidden Valley plateau surrounded by Dhaulagiri peaks.

itinerary.place: Italian Base Camp to Glacier Camp (4210m)
Glacier Camp to Dhaulagiri Base Camp (4740m)
Dhaulagiri Base Camp to Hidden Valley (5200m) via French Pass (5360m)
itinerary.description: Cross French Pass. Enter Hidden Valley.

treks.name: Dhorpatan Hunting Reserve Trek
treks.permit_details: Dhorpatan Hunting Reserve Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Nepal's only hunting reserve, Magar culture, traditional mountain lifestyle, conservation area [citation:4]
treks.description: The Dhorpatan Hunting Reserve Trek takes you through Nepal's only hunting reserve, a pristine high-altitude plateau rich in wildlife including blue sheep, Himalayan tahr, musk deer, and red pandas. Covering 1,325 sq km across the districts of Baglung, Myagdi, and Rukum, this remote reserve offers spectacular views of the Dhaulagiri range including Gurja Himal (7,193m), Churen Himal (7,385m), and Putha Hiunchuli (7,246m). The trek passes through traditional Magar villages and offers a unique opportunity to observe endangered wildlife in their natural habitat while experiencing authentic mountain culture far from tourist crowds. [citation:4]
treks.short_description: Wildlife trek through Nepal's only hunting reserve with blue sheep, Himalayan views, and Magar culture.

itinerary.description: Enter hunting reserve. First mountain views.
Trek to main plateau. Wildlife sightings.

itinerary.place: Phagune Phedi to Phagune Danda (4493m) to Gurjaghat (2800m)
itinerary.description: Early morning ascent for sunrise views of Dhaulagiri. Long descent.

treks.name: Ramche Danda Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Magar culture, traditional mountain villages
treks.description: The Ramche Danda Trek offers spectacular close-up views of the Dhaulagiri range from a beautiful alpine ridge in the Myagdi district. This emerging trek passes through traditional Magar villages and offers a peaceful alternative to busier Annapurna treks. The ridge provides stunning panoramic views of Dhaulagiri, Annapurna South, and other peaks. The trail passes through beautiful rhododendron forests and alpine meadows, offering excellent opportunities for nature photography and cultural immersion.
treks.short_description: Alpine ridge trek with Dhaulagiri views and authentic Magar village culture.

itinerary.place: Base to Ramche Danda Summit (3600m) and return
itinerary.description: Early morning summit for sunrise views.

treks.name: Sano Dhaulagiri Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Remote mountain wilderness
treks.description: The Sano Dhaulagiri (Little Dhaulagiri) Trek is a challenging route to a spectacular peak in the Dhaulagiri massif. This less-known trek offers pristine mountain scenery, remote valleys, and a true wilderness experience for experienced trekkers seeking solitude and adventure away from crowded routes. The trek provides close-up views of Sano Dhaulagiri and the surrounding peaks, with opportunities to explore alpine landscapes and glacial terrain.
treks.short_description: Challenging trek to Sano Dhaulagiri with pristine mountain scenery and remote wilderness.

itinerary.place: Dobang to Sano Dhaulagiri Base Camp (3700m)
High Camp to Sano Dhaulagiri Viewpoint (4700m) and return
itinerary.description: Early morning trek for sunrise views. Return to base camp.

treks.name: Putha Hiunchuli Trek (Expedition)
treks.permit_details: Putha Hiunchuli Expedition Permit (pricing varies by team size), Dhorpatan Hunting Reserve Permit
treks.cultural_significance: Expedition mountaineering, first ascent in 1954 [citation:6]
treks.description: The Putha Hiunchuli Trek is an expedition to climb Putha Hiunchuli (7,239m), also known as Dhaulagiri VII, the westernmost peak of the Dhaulagiri massif. First climbed in 1954, this expedition offers a challenging mountaineering experience with spectacular views of the entire Dhaulagiri range. The approach crosses the remote Dhorpatan Hunting Reserve and requires multiple high-altitude camps. This expedition is suitable for experienced climbers with previous high-altitude experience. In 2008, climbers made what may have been the first alpine-style ascent of the mountain from the north. [citation:6]
treks.short_description: Mountaineering expedition to climb Putha Hiunchuli (7239m) in the Dhaulagiri range.

treks.name: Sisne Himal Trek
treks.permit_details: TIMS Card NPR 2000, local municipality permits
treks.cultural_significance: Hiunchuki Patan (high-altitude meadow), birthplace of Maoist civilian war, traditional Khas and Magar culture, historical significance [citation:2]
treks.description: The Sisne Himal Trek is a newly promoted trekking route in Western Nepal, circling Sisne Himal (also known as Hiunchuki Patan), the highest mountain in Western Nepal at 5,911m. This lesser-explored region offers pristine natural beauty, authentic cultural experiences, and significant historical interest as the birthplace of the Maoist civilian war. The trek features beautiful lakes including Kamal Lake with lotus flowers, Deurali Cave, Gupta Daha, and Shyarpu Lake, along with spectacular views of the West Dhaulagiri Range and Kanjirowa mountain range [citation:2]. The route passes through traditional villages where visitors can experience the true living style, culture, and historical aspects of Western Nepal.
treks.short_description: Newly promoted trek in Western Nepal circling Sisne Himal with beautiful lakes and cultural heritage.

itinerary.place: Deurali Cave to Sisne Base Camp (3800m)
itinerary.description: Trek to base camp. Mountain views.

itinerary.place: Base Camp to Viewpoint (4000m) and return
itinerary.description: Sunrise views of Sisne Himal and Dhaulagiri range.

treks.name: Kanjiroba Himal Trek
treks.permit_details: Upper Dolpa Restricted Area Permit USD 500 for 10 days, plus USD 50 per additional day, Shey Phoksundo National Park Permit NPR 3000 [citation:3]
treks.cultural_significance: Ancient Bon and Buddhist culture, restricted area [citation:2]
treks.description: The Kanjiroba Himal Trek is an extreme expedition through the remote Kanjiroba range in western Dolpa, one of Nepal's most isolated regions. This challenging trek requires full camping support and crosses high passes through pristine wilderness. The region is known for its stunning mountain scenery, ancient Bon and Buddhist monasteries, and opportunities to spot rare wildlife including snow leopards and blue sheep [citation:2]. The trek offers spectacular views of Kanjiroba Himal and the surrounding peaks, with opportunities to explore remote valleys untouched by modernization.
treks.short_description: Extreme expedition trek through the remote Kanjiroba range in western Dolpa with stunning mountain scenery.

itinerary.place: Base Camp to Kanjiroba Viewpoint (5000m) and return
itinerary.description: Early morning trek for mountain views.

itinerary.place: Upper Camp to High Pass (5400m) and return
itinerary.description: Cross high pass for panoramic views.

treks.name: Dolpa-Tibet Border Trek
treks.permit_details: Upper Dolpa Restricted Area Permit USD 500 for 10 days, plus USD 50 per additional day, Shey Phoksundo National Park Permit [citation:3]
treks.cultural_significance: Ancient salt trading route to Tibet, preserved Bon and Buddhist culture [citation:3]
treks.description: The Dolpa-Tibet Border Trek follows ancient salt trading routes to the Tibetan border through one of Nepal's most remote regions. This extreme expedition requires full camping support and crosses multiple high passes through landscapes that feel more like Tibet than Nepal. The route offers unparalleled cultural immersion in ancient Bon and Buddhist monasteries, dramatic desert-like terrain, and opportunities to experience a way of life that has remained unchanged for centuries. This trek is for the most dedicated adventurers seeking the ultimate Himalayan wilderness experience [citation:2][citation:3].
treks.short_description: Extreme expedition following ancient salt trading routes to the Tibetan border through remote Dolpa.

itinerary.place: Phoksundo Lake to Shey Gompa (4300m) via Kang La (5360m)
Yangze Gompa to Tibet Border Approach (5000m)
Border Approach to Viewpoint (5500m) and return

treks.name: Mugu-Tibet Border Trek
treks.permit_details: Mugu Restricted Area Permit USD 90 for first 7 days + USD 15 per day thereafter, Rara National Park Permit NPR 3000 [citation:3]
treks.cultural_significance: Remote trading routes, Khas and Tibetan culture [citation:3]
treks.description: The Mugu-Tibet Border Trek is an extreme expedition through the remote Mugu district to the Tibetan border. This challenging route follows ancient trade paths through high mountain passes, offering spectacular views of the Himalayas and opportunities to experience traditional Tibetan-influenced culture in one of Nepal's most isolated regions. The trek can be combined with a visit to Rara Lake, Nepal's largest lake, before heading north toward the border [citation:3][citation:4].
treks.short_description: Extreme expedition trek through remote Mugu to the Tibetan border with high passes and cultural encounters.

itinerary.place: Border Approach to Viewpoint (5400m) and return

treks.name: Tansen Ranimahal Heritage Hike
treks.permit_details: No permits required. Entry fee at Rani Mahal: NPR 100 for Nepalis, NPR 500 for international visitors [citation:5]
treks.cultural_significance: Rani Mahal built in 1893 by Khadga Shamsher Rana in memory of his wife, blend of Nepali and Mughal architecture, historic Newari culture in Tansen [citation:5]
treks.description: The Tansen Ranimahal Heritage Hike is a short cultural journey from the historic hill town of Tansen to the beautiful Rani Mahal, often called the 'Taj Mahal of Nepal.' Located on the banks of the Kali Gandaki River, this four-storey palace was built in 1893 by General Khadga Shamsher Rana in memory of his beloved wife Tej Kumari Devi, costing 35 crore Nepalese rupees at the time [citation:5]. The trail passes through traditional villages, terraced farmlands, and offers stunning views of the river valley. The palace features a blend of Nepali, Mughal, and European architectural styles and was restored by the Nepalese government in 2017.
treks.short_description: Heritage hike from Tansen to the 'Taj Mahal of Nepal' with stunning river views and cultural exploration.

itinerary.description: Drive 6-7 hours from Kathmandu to Tansen. Explore historic Tansen Bazaar with Newari architecture and Palpali Dhaka fabric shops. Overnight at lodge [citation:5].

itinerary.place: Tansen to Rani Mahal (800m) and return
itinerary.description: Hike 4-5 hours (13km) downhill through forests and villages to Rani Mahal on Kali Gandaki River. Explore palace, gardens, and riverside. Return hike or drive back to Tansen. Overnight at lodge [citation:5].
Drive back to Kathmandu. Free afternoon.

treks.name: Makrau Danda Trek
treks.permit_details: No permits required
treks.cultural_significance: Magar and Brahmin communities, traditional mountain lifestyle
treks.description: The Makrau Danda Trek is an easy cultural trek through the beautiful mid-hills of western Nepal. The route passes through traditional Magar villages with stunning terrace farming landscapes and offers views of the Dhaulagiri range on clear days. This off-the-beaten-path trek provides authentic cultural experiences, warm homestay hospitality, and peaceful walks through forests and farmlands away from the crowded tourist routes.
treks.short_description: Easy cultural trek through Magar villages with Dhaulagiri views and authentic homestay experiences.

itinerary.description: Drive 8-9 hours to Tamghas, district headquarters of Gulmi.
Trek 4-5 hours through villages and forests to ridge top with mountain views. Homestay.
Trek through forest to Resunga area. Homestay.

treks.name: Gorepani Danda Trek (Gulmi)
treks.permit_details: No permits required
treks.cultural_significance: Magar culture, traditional villages (not to be confused with Ghorepani in Annapurna region)
treks.description: The Gorepani Danda Trek (not to be confused with the famous Ghorepani in the Annapurna region) is a cultural trek in the Gulmi district of western Nepal, offering beautiful views of the Dhaulagiri range. This quiet off-the-beaten-path route passes through traditional Magar villages with opportunities for homestay experiences and organic farm visits. The trek provides authentic cultural immersion and peaceful walks through forests and terraced farmlands without the crowds of more popular routes.
treks.short_description: Cultural trek through Magar villages in Gulmi with Dhaulagiri views and authentic homestay experiences.

itinerary.description: Trek 4-5 hours through villages and forests. Homestay.
Explore village, enjoy mountain views, organic farm visits.

treks.name: Swargadwari Hill Trek
treks.permit_details: No permits required
treks.cultural_significance: Important Hindu temple mentioned in Mahabharata, believed to be where Pandavas worshipped before departing to heaven, sacred cow sanctuary [citation:7]
treks.description: The Swargadwari Hill Trek leads to the sacred Swargadwari Temple, literally meaning 'Door to Heaven,' located at 2,200m in Pyuthan District. Mentioned in the epic Mahabharata, this is where the Pandavas are believed to have worshipped before their journey to heaven [citation:7]. The temple complex includes a unique cow protection area and offers spectacular panoramic views of the Dhaulagiri and other Himalayan peaks. Simple lodging is available at the temple for a nominal fee (NPR 50 per bed, NPR 100 for dinner). The trail passes through beautiful rhododendron forests and traditional villages, with the temple's evening aarti ceremony providing a profound spiritual experience.
treks.short_description: Pilgrimage trek to sacred Swargadwari Temple with Himalayan views and profound spiritual significance.

itinerary.description: Drive to Bhaluwang in Dang district.
Drive to Chakchake, then to Bhingri at mouth of Rapti River valley. Overnight in Bhingri [citation:7].
Trek through small villages and fields. Camping or teahouse.
Trek through rhododendron forests to temple. Evening aarti ceremony. Temple lodging (NPR 50) [citation:7].
Explore temple complex, cow sanctuary, natural cave shrine. Sunrise views of Dhaulagiri. Temple lodging [citation:7].

treks.name: Madi Danda Trek
treks.permit_details: No permits required
treks.cultural_significance: Magar culture, traditional villages, rural lifestyle [citation:5]
treks.description: The Madi Danda Trek is an easy cultural trek from the historic town of Tansen, passing through traditional Magar villages with beautifully preserved architecture. The trek offers insights into rural Nepali life, stunning terrace farming landscapes, and panoramic views of the Himalayas on clear days. This gentle route provides authentic cultural immersion and warm hospitality through community homestays, perfect for those seeking a peaceful introduction to Nepal's mid-hill culture [citation:5].
treks.short_description: Easy cultural trek from Tansen through traditional Magar villages with beautiful terrace farming landscapes.

itinerary.description: Drive 6-7 hours to Tansen. Explore historic Tansen Bazaar. Overnight at lodge [citation:5].
Trek 4-5 hours through villages and terraced fields to Madi Danda. Homestay.
Explore village, interact with locals, enjoy mountain views. Homestay.
Return trek to Tansen. Overnight at lodge.

treks.name: Bhairavnath Mahadev Trek
treks.permit_details: No permits required
treks.cultural_significance: Hindu pilgrimage site, Magar culture [citation:5]
treks.description: The Bhairavnath Mahadev Trek is a short pilgrimage trek from Tansen to sacred Hindu sites. The trek offers beautiful views of the Dhaulagiri range and passes through traditional villages with opportunities for cultural interactions. The temple of Bhairavnath and the Mahadev cave are important pilgrimage destinations, especially during festivals [citation:5].
treks.short_description: Short pilgrimage trek from Tansen with mountain views and sacred Hindu sites.

itinerary.description: Drive to Tansen. Explore Tansen Bazaar.

itinerary.place: Tansen to Bhairavnath Temple (1600m) and Mahadev Cave
itinerary.description: Trek 4-5 hours to temple and cave. Return to Tansen.

treks.name: Tansen Trek
treks.permit_details: No permits required
treks.cultural_significance: Historic Newari trading town, Palpali Dhaka weaving tradition, traditional architecture [citation:5]
treks.description: The Tansen Trek is a gentle cultural exploration of the historic hill town of Tansen, the district headquarters of Palpa. Known for its well-preserved Newari architecture, traditional Palpali Dhaka fabric, and panoramic views of the Dhaulagiri and Annapurna ranges from nearby Shreenagar Hill, Tansen offers a perfect short getaway. The trek includes visits to the Amar Narayan Temple, local weaving centers, and the bustling Tansen Bazaar with its narrow brick-paved streets and traditional buildings [citation:5].
treks.short_description: Gentle cultural exploration of historic Tansen with Newari architecture, traditional crafts, and mountain views.

itinerary.description: Drive 6-7 hours to Tansen. Settle into hotel. Evening explore Tansen Bazaar [citation:5].
Visit Amar Narayan Temple, explore old town, visit Palpali Dhaka weaving centers. Sunset views [citation:5].

itinerary.place: Tansen to Shreenagar Hill (5km round trip)
itinerary.description: Early morning hike to Shreenagar Hill for sunrise over Dhaulagiri and Annapurna ranges. Return for breakfast [citation:5].
Morning free for last-minute shopping. Drive back to Kathmandu.

treks.name: Muktinath Temple Pilgrimage
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: One of the eight Swayam Vyakta Kshetras (self-manifested temples) of Lord Vishnu, believed to grant salvation (moksha) to devotees; sacred to both Hindus and Buddhists; known in Tibetan as 'Chumig Gyatsa' meaning 'Hundred Waters' where Guru Rinpoche meditated [citation:1][citation:10]
treks.description: Muktinath Temple, meaning 'Lord of Liberation,' is one of the holiest pilgrimage sites in the Himalayas, revered by both Hindus and Buddhists. Located at 3,710m in the Mustang district, it is believed to grant salvation (moksha) to devotees who visit and bathe in its 108 sacred water taps. The temple is one of the eight Swayam Vyakta Kshetras (self-manifested temples of Lord Vishnu) and the only one located in Nepal. The complex features a pagoda-style temple, 108 water spouts shaped like cow heads, the Jwala Mai Temple with an eternal flame that burns without fuel, and several Buddhist monasteries including Narsingh Gompa. According to Tibetan Buddhist tradition, Guru Rinpoche meditated here and achieved enlightenment [citation:1][citation:10].
treks.short_description: Sacred pilgrimage to the 'Lord of Liberation' with 108 holy water taps, eternal flame, and panoramic Himalayan views.

itinerary.description: Scenic drive or flight to Pokhara. Overnight at hotel.
20-minute mountain flight with spectacular views of Dhaulagiri and Annapurna. Acclimatize in Jomsom.
Trek 3-4 hours to ancient Kagbeni village, gateway to Upper Mustang. Explore monastery.
Trek 5-6 hours through dramatic landscapes to Muktinath. Visit temple complex in evening.
Early morning blessings. Bathe in 108 water taps, visit Jwala Mai Temple with eternal flame, explore Narsingh Gompa. Pilgrims believe bathing here cleanses past sins [citation:1][citation:10].
Descend to Marpha, famous for apple orchards and apple brandy. Explore village.

itinerary.place: Marpha to Jomsom to Pokhara to Kathmandu
itinerary.description: Trek to Jomsom, fly to Pokhara, connect to Kathmandu.

treks.name: Gosaikunda Lake Pilgrimage
treks.permit_details: Langtang National Park Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Sacred Hindu and Buddhist pilgrimage site, believed to be created by Lord Shiva when he thrust his trident into the mountain to obtain water to cool his burning throat after consuming poison; major festival during Janai Purnima [citation:2]
treks.description: Gosaikunda Lake is a sacred alpine lake at 4,380m in Langtang National Park, revered by both Hindus and Buddhists. According to Hindu mythology, the lake was created by Lord Shiva when he thrust his trident into the mountain to obtain water to cool his burning throat after consuming poison. The lake complex includes 108 smaller lakes, with the main Gosaikunda Lake being the most sacred. During the Janai Purnima festival in August, thousands of pilgrims undertake the challenging trek to bathe in the holy waters, believing it cleanses sins and brings salvation [citation:2].
treks.short_description: Sacred pilgrimage to Lord Shiva's lake at 4,380m with stunning Himalayan views and vibrant festival atmosphere.

itinerary.description: Drive 6-7 hours through scenic Trishuli River valley. Overnight at teahouse.
Trek 5-6 hours through dense forests. Visit famous Buddhist monastery.

itinerary.place: Sing Gompa to Gosaikunda (4380m) via Laurebina Pass (4610m)
itinerary.description: Challenging 6-7 hour ascent. Arrive at sacred lake. Overnight at lakeside teahouse.
Explore main lake and surrounding smaller lakes including Bhairav Kunda and Saraswati Kunda. Holy bath for purification.

treks.name: Panch Pokhari Pilgrimage
treks.permit_details: Gaurishankar Conservation Area Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000 (Langtang National Park Office currently under development - permits may be introduced soon) [citation:3]
treks.cultural_significance: Sacred Hindu pilgrimage site named after the five lakes: Jethi Pokhari, Mahili Pokhari, Sahili Pokhari, Kaili Pokhari, and Kanchi Pokhari; thousands of pilgrims visit during Janai Purnima festival (August) [citation:3]
treks.description: Panch Pokhari, meaning 'Five Lakes,' is one of Nepal's most important Hindu pilgrimage sites, located at 4,100m in the remote reaches of Sindhupalchok district. The five sacred lakes are named Jethi Pokhari, Mahili Pokhari, Sahili Pokhari, Kaili Pokhari, and Kanchi Pokhari in Nepali. During the Janai Purnima festival in August, thousands of pilgrims journey to bathe in the holy waters, believing it cleanses sins and brings blessings. The site offers stunning views of the Jugal Himal, Gaurishankar, Langtang, and Rolwaling ranges. The trek gained official promotion as a trekking destination in the early 2000s by Nepal Tourism Board and TAAN [citation:3].
treks.short_description: Sacred pilgrimage to five high-altitude lakes with spectacular Himalayan views and vibrant festival celebrations.

itinerary.description: Drive 5-6 hours to Chautara. Overnight at guesthouse.
Trek 5-6 hours through villages and farmlands.
Trek through rhododendron and pine forests.
Beautiful forest trail with yak herding settlements.
Trek 6-7 hours to sacred five lakes. Stunning panoramic views.
Explore all five lakes, visit shrines, perform holy rituals. Pilgrims believe bathing here brings salvation [citation:3].

treks.name: Badimalika Temple Pilgrimage
treks.permit_details: Khaptad National Park Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: One of the 51 Shakti Peethas where the left knee (or left part of the leg) of Goddess Sati is believed to have fallen after her self-immolation; major festivals include Malika Chaturdashi, Ganga Dussehra, and Janai Purnima [citation:4]
treks.description: Badimalika Temple is a sacred Hindu temple dedicated to Goddess Bhagwati, located at approximately 4,200m in the Bajura district. According to Hindu mythology, this is one of the 51 Shakti Peethas where the left knee (or left part of the leg) of Goddess Sati fell when Lord Shiva carried her burning body across the cosmos [citation:4]. The temple attracts thousands of pilgrims during the Malika Chaturdashi festival in August-September. The remote location requires a challenging 2-3 day trek through the beautiful Khaptad National Park, home to over 260 bird species and diverse wildlife. In 2025, the Nepali Army completed reconstruction of the temple, preserving its traditional Nepali architectural style under the guidance of the Department of Archaeology [citation:4].
treks.short_description: Sacred Shakti Peetha pilgrimage at 4,200m with rich mythology, remote wilderness, and stunning Himalayan views.

itinerary.description: Challenging ascent to temple area. Camping. According to legend, this is where the left knee of Sati Devi fell [citation:4].
Visit temple, sacred lake, enjoy mountain views of Api and Saipal. Perform rituals.

treks.name: Halesi Mahadev Pilgrimage
treks.permit_details: No permits required
treks.cultural_significance: Sacred pilgrimage site revered by Hindus, Buddhists, and Kirats; believed to be where Lord Shiva meditated; Manjushree Cave is significant to Buddhists; represents unique religious harmony [citation:5]
treks.description: Halesi Mahadev is a sacred cave temple complex in the Khotang district, revered by Hindus, Buddhists, and Kirats as a unique center of interfaith harmony. Hindus believe Lord Shiva meditated here, Buddhists associate the site with Manjushree, and Kirats consider it an ancient spiritual center. In late 2024, a new 360-meter trekking route with attractive stone paving was constructed to improve access to the Manjushree Cave, making pilgrimage easier for devotees. The Tuwachung Trekking Route has also been developed in the area to boost religious tourism [citation:5].
treks.short_description: Sacred cave temple complex where three religions converge - Hindus, Buddhists, and Kirats worship in harmony.

itinerary.description: Flight to Lamidanda (via Biratnagar). Drive to Halesi area.
Visit main Halesi Mahadev Cave Temple. Experience the unique spiritual atmosphere.
Explore new Manjushree Trekking Route (360m) completed in 2024. Visit Tuwachung area [citation:5].
Return to Lamidanda. Fly back to Kathmandu.

treks.name: Manakamana Temple Pilgrimage
treks.permit_details: No permits required. Cable car ticket: NPR 950 round trip (approx. USD 7)
treks.cultural_significance: Temple dedicated to Hindu goddess Bhagwati, incarnation of Parvati; believed to fulfill wishes of devotees; name 'Manakamana' means 'wish-fulfilling'; historical connection to King Ram Shah [citation:7]
treks.description: Manakamana Temple, dedicated to the Hindu goddess Bhagwati (incarnation of Parvati), is one of Nepal's most popular pilgrimage sites, located at 1,302m in the Gorkha district. The temple's name means 'wish-fulfilling' in Nepali, and devotees believe that the goddess grants the sincere wishes of those who pray here. The most popular way to reach the temple is via the Manakamana Cable Car, the first cable car in Nepal, which opened in 1998. The 2.8km ride takes about 10 minutes and offers spectacular views of the Trishuli River and surrounding hills. For the more adventurous, a 3-4 hour hiking trail from Kurintar leads to the temple through forests and villages [citation:7].
treks.short_description: Wish-fulfilling goddess temple accessible by Nepal's first cable car with spectacular river and mountain views.

itinerary.description: Drive 3-4 hours from Kathmandu to Kurintar on Prithvi Highway. Overnight at hotel or resort.

Morning cable car ride (10 minutes, 2.8km) or 3-4 hour trek. Visit temple, offer prayers, make wishes. Return by cable car to Kurintar. Drive back to Kathmandu. Temple is open 4 AM to 7 PM daily [citation:7].

treks.name: Palanchok Bhagwati Pilgrimage
treks.permit_details: No permits required
treks.cultural_significance: Ancient temple dedicated to Goddess Bhagwati, important Shakti Peetha in the Kathmandu Valley rim; Newari architectural heritage
treks.description: Palanchok Bhagwati Temple is an ancient Shakti Peetha dedicated to Goddess Bhagwati, located in the Kavrepalanchok district east of Kathmandu. The temple features traditional Newari architecture and offers stunning views of the Himalayas including Gaurishankar, Langtang, and Ganesh Himal ranges. The site is an important pilgrimage destination for Hindus, particularly during the Dashain festival. The trek from Dhulikhel passes through traditional Newari villages and terraced farmlands, providing a peaceful spiritual journey close to Kathmandu.
treks.short_description: Ancient goddess temple with Newari architecture and stunning Himalayan views, accessible from Kathmandu.

itinerary.description: Drive 1.5 hours to Dhulikhel, historic Newari town. Explore town and sunset views.

itinerary.place: Dhulikhel to Palanchok Bhagwati Temple (1650m) and return
itinerary.description: Trek 2-3 hours through villages and forests to temple. Offer prayers, enjoy Himalayan panorama. Return to Dhulikhel. Drive back to Kathmandu.

treks.name: Dakshinkali Temple Pilgrimage
treks.permit_details: No permits required
treks.cultural_significance: One of Nepal's most important temples dedicated to Goddess Kali (the fierce form of Goddess Parvati); animal sacrifice is performed on Tuesdays and Saturdays; located at confluence of rivers; important for tantric traditions [citation:9]
treks.description: Dakshinkali Temple, dedicated to Goddess Kali (the fierce form of Goddess Parvati), is one of the most important Hindu temples in the Kathmandu Valley, located about 22km south of Kathmandu. The temple sits at the confluence of two rivers, creating a powerful spiritual setting. Tuesdays and Saturdays are the main worship days when animal sacrifices are performed as offerings to the goddess. The temple is particularly crowded during the Dashain festival. Nearby, the historic town of Pharping is an important Buddhist site where Guru Rinpoche (Padmasambhava) is believed to have meditated in Yangleshö Cave, making the area significant for both Hindus and Buddhists [citation:9].
treks.short_description: Powerful Kali temple south of Kathmandu with traditional rituals, tantric significance, and nearby Buddhist caves.

itinerary.description: Early morning departure (7 AM) to avoid traffic. 1-hour drive. Visit temple, offer prayers. Observe rituals (animal sacrifices on Tuesdays and Saturdays). Visit holy stream for purification. Then proceed to Pharping (10 minutes) to visit Yangleshö Cave where Guru Rinpoche meditated and Rigon Tashi Choeling Monastery. Optional 2-hour hike to Champadevi Hill for panoramic views. Return to Kathmandu by afternoon [citation:9].

treks.name: Ama Dablam Base Camp Trek
treks.permit_details: Sagarmatha National Park Permit NPR 3000, Khumbu Pasang Lhamu Rural Municipality Permit NPR 2000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Sherpa culture, sacred mountain known as 'Mother's Necklace' for its hanging glaciers, Buddhist monasteries
treks.description: The Ama Dablam Base Camp Trek offers close-up views of one of the world's most beautiful mountains, known as the 'Matterhorn of the Himalayas' for its stunning pyramidal shape. Ama Dablam (6,812m) is named for its hanging glaciers that resemble a mother's necklace. This trek branches off from the classic Everest trail at Pangboche and provides a less crowded alternative with spectacular mountain scenery. The route passes through traditional Sherpa villages, rhododendron forests, and offers incredible views of Ama Dablam, Everest, Lhotse, and Makalu. The base camp at 4,600m sits directly beneath the mountain's southwest ridge, providing an unforgettable wilderness experience.
treks.short_description: Trek to the base of the stunning Ama Dablam, the 'Matterhorn of the Himalayas,' with close-up views of its iconic hanging glaciers.

itinerary.description: 30-minute scenic flight to Lukla. Trek 3-4 hours to Phakding along Dudh Koshi River.
Trek 5-6 hours through pine forests, crossing several high suspension bridges including the famous Hillary Bridge. First views of Everest.
Rest day with optional hike to Everest View Hotel (3880m) for panoramic mountain views. Explore local market and museum.
Trek 5-6 hours through rhododendron forests with stunning views of Ama Dablam. Visit Tengboche Monastery for evening prayers.
Trek 3-4 hours to Pangboche, one of the oldest Sherpa villages. Visit ancient monastery said to contain a yeti scalp.

itinerary.place: Pangboche to Ama Dablam Base Camp (4600m)
itinerary.description: Challenging 5-6 hour trek to base camp with spectacular close-up views of Ama Dablam's southwest ridge. Camping.

itinerary.place: Ama Dablam Base Camp Exploration Day
itinerary.description: Explore the base camp area, photography of Ama Dablam, Everest, Lhotse, and Makalu. Optional hikes to nearby viewpoints.

itinerary.place: Ama Dablam Base Camp to Pangboche
itinerary.description: Descend 4-5 hours back to Pangboche. Teahouse accommodation.
Trek 6-7 hours descending through Tengboche to Namche Bazaar.
Final trekking day descending 6-7 hours to Lukla. Celebration dinner.
Morning flight back to Kathmandu. Free afternoon.
Transfer to airport for international departure.

treks.name: Everest Panorama Trek (Tengboche Route)
treks.permit_details: Sagarmatha National Park Permit NPR 3000, Khumbu Pasang Lhamu Rural Municipality Permit NPR 2000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Tengboche Monastery - largest monastery in Khumbu, Sherpa culture, Sir Edmund Hillary's legacy in Khumjung
treks.description: The Everest Panorama Trek, also known as the Tengboche route, is a short and scenic trek that offers spectacular views of the world's highest peaks without the extreme altitude and distance of the full Everest Base Camp trek. This 10-day adventure takes you to Tengboche Monastery at 3,867m, home to the largest monastery in the Khumbu region and offering breathtaking panoramic views of Mount Everest, Lhotse, Nuptse, and the iconic Ama Dablam. The trek passes through the vibrant Sherpa town of Namche Bazaar, the traditional village of Khumjung with its Hillary School and hospital, and provides a perfect introduction to Sherpa culture and Himalayan scenery for those with limited time.
treks.short_description: Short and scenic trek to Tengboche Monastery with stunning Everest panoramas and rich Sherpa cultural experiences.

itinerary.description: 40-minute scenic flight to Lukla. Trek 3 hours to Phakding through rhododendron forests and along Dudh Koshi River. Overnight teahouse.
Trek 6 hours through Benkar, Monjo (enter Sagarmatha National Park), and Jorsale. Cross Hillary suspension bridge and steep climb to Namche. Stunning mountain views. Overnight teahouse.
Hike to Everest View Hotel (3,880m) for spectacular Everest panorama. Visit Sherpa Culture Museum and local markets. Overnight teahouse.
Trek 3-4 hours to Khumjung, traditional Sherpa village. Visit Hillary School and hospital, Khumjung Monastery with alleged yeti scalp. Overnight teahouse.
Trek 5 hours through Phunki Thanga and up to Tengboche. Spectacular views of Everest, Nuptse, Lhotse, and Ama Dablam. Visit Tengboche Monastery for evening prayer ceremony. Overnight teahouse.

itinerary.place: Tengboche Sunrise and return to Namche
itinerary.description: Early morning sunrise over Everest and surrounding peaks. Trek 5-6 hours descending to Namche Bazaar. Overnight teahouse.
Trek 5 hours descending through lush forests to Phakding. Overnight teahouse.
Trek 3-4 hours to Lukla. Celebrate completion of trek. Overnight teahouse.
Morning flight back to Kathmandu. Free afternoon for rest or souvenir shopping.
Transfer to airport for international departure.

treks.name: Mera Peak Trek
treks.permit_details: Mera Peak Climbing Permit (NMA): Spring USD 250, Autumn USD 125; Makalu Barun National Park Permit NPR 3000; Khumbu Pasang Lhamu Municipality Permit NPR 2000; TIMS Card NPR 2000
attractions: Mera Peak Summit (6476m - Nepal's highest trekking peak)
treks.cultural_significance: Sherpa and Rai villages, traditional mountain culture, Makalu Barun National Park biodiversity
treks.description: Mera Peak, at 6,476m, is the highest trekking peak in Nepal and a perfect introduction to Himalayan mountaineering. While the mountain poses little technical difficulty, it requires good physical fitness and proper acclimatization. The climbing is straightforward, with short steeper sections of 35° snow requiring ice axe and crampons use. The journey through the remote Hinku Valley offers spectacular views of five of the world's highest peaks: Everest, Lhotse, Makalu, Cho Oyu, and Kanchenjunga. The ascent involves crossing the Mera La pass (5,400m) and establishing high camp before the summit push. This expedition is ideal for those with moderate mountaineering experience, a good level of fitness, and a keen sense of adventure.
treks.short_description: Nepal's highest trekking peak (6,476m) offering stunning views of five 8,000m peaks and a perfect introduction to Himalayan mountaineering.

itinerary.description: Scenic 30-minute flight to Lukla. Meet team, prepare for trek.
Trek 4-5 hours through forests and villages, leaving main Everest trail to enter Hinku Valley.
Trek 5-6 hours crossing Pangkongma La (2,846m). Descend to Panggom.
Trek 5-6 hours through rhododendron forest, crossing Surke La. Camping.
Trek 5-6 hours descending into Hinku Valley. Camping.
Trek 5-6 hours through beautiful valley with mountain views. Camping.
Rest day. Short hike to 5,000m for acclimatization. Camping.
Trek 3-4 hours to Khare, base camp area. Camping.
Rope skills training, crampon and ice axe practice. Camping.

itinerary.place: Khare to Mera Base Camp (5300m) via Mera La (5400m)
itinerary.description: Trek 5-6 hours, rope up for glacier crossing over Mera La pass. Spectacular views. Camping.
Rest and final preparation for summit push. Camping.
Trek 3-4 hours up easy-angled snow slopes to high camp. Spectacular views of Everest, Makalu. Camping.

itinerary.place: High Camp to Mera Peak Summit (6476m) and return to Base Camp
itinerary.description: Early morning start for summit ascent via open snowfields and final 50m snow slope using jumar. Panoramic views of five 8,000m peaks. Descent to base camp.
Buffer day in case of bad weather. Camping.
Retrace route back to Thangnak. Camping.
Long descent via alternative route to Lukla.

treks.name: Island Peak Trek
treks.permit_details: Island Peak Climbing Permit (NMA): Spring USD 250, Autumn USD 125, Winter/Summer USD 70; Sagarmatha National Park Permit NPR 3000; Khumbu Pasang Lhamu Municipality Permit NPR 2000; TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Named 'Island Peak' because it appears as an island in a sea of ice when viewed from Dingboche; first ascent in 1953 by British team as training for Everest
treks.description: Island Peak (Imja Tse), at 6,189m, is one of Nepal's most popular trekking peaks, offering an ideal introduction to Himalayan mountaineering. The peak is named for its appearance as an 'island' in a sea of ice when viewed from Dingboche. The climb involves glacier travel, a steep headwall of 45-60° snow and ice using fixed ropes, and an exposed summit ridge. First climbed in 1953 by a British team as training for Everest, it remains a classic objective for climbers preparing for higher peaks like Ama Dablam or Everest. The trek follows the classic Everest Base Camp route through Namche Bazaar and Tengboche before branching off to Chhukung. The summit offers spectacular views of Everest, Lhotse, Makalu, and Ama Dablam.
treks.short_description: Classic 6,189m trekking peak offering technical climbing on 45-60° snow/ice with spectacular Everest views - perfect introduction to mountaineering.

itinerary.description: 40-minute flight to Lukla. Trek 3-4 hours to Phakding.
Hike to Everest View Hotel (3,880m) for acclimatization and views.
Hike to Nangkartshang Peak (5,083m) for acclimatization.

itinerary.place: Chhukung to Island Peak Base Camp (5100m)
itinerary.description: Trek 4-5 hours to base camp. Camping.
Crampon, ice axe, and fixed rope practice. Camping.
Trek 3-4 hours to high camp on glacier. Camping.

itinerary.place: Summit Island Peak (6189m) and return to Base Camp
itinerary.description: Early morning start. Climb glacier, ascend 45-60° headwall with fixed ropes, traverse exposed summit ridge. Spectacular views. Descend to base camp.
Buffer day in case of bad weather.

treks.name: Gokyo–EBC via Cho La Trek
treks.permit_details: Sagarmatha National Park Permit NPR 3000, Khumbu Pasang Lhamu Rural Municipality Permit NPR 2000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Sherpa culture, sacred lakes, high-altitude mountaineering heritage
treks.description: The Gokyo–EBC via Cho La Trek is a classic adventure combining the best of the Everest region, taking you to both the turquoise Gokyo Lakes and the iconic Everest Base Camp via the challenging Cho La Pass (5,420m). This grand circuit crosses the Ngozumpa Glacier, the largest glacier in Nepal, and offers breathtaking views from Gokyo Ri (5,483m) and Kala Patthar (5,545m) including four of the world's highest 8,000-meter peaks: Everest, Lhotse, Makalu, and Cho Oyu. The trek requires glacier crossing experience and excellent physical condition but rewards with unparalleled Himalayan scenery and a true sense of adventure.
treks.short_description: Grand Everest circuit combining turquoise Gokyo Lakes, challenging Cho La Pass, and Everest Base Camp with spectacular mountain panoramas.

itinerary.description: 40-minute flight to Lukla. Trek 3-4 hours to Phakding.
Hike to Everest View Hotel (3,880m) for panoramic views.
Trek 6 hours via less crowded route to Phortse Thanga.
Trek 5 hours to first views of turquoise Gokyo Lakes.
Early morning hike to Gokyo Ri for sunrise over Everest, Lhotse, Makalu, and Cho Oyu. Spectacular views of four 8,000m peaks.
Trek 4 hours preparing for Cho La crossing.

itinerary.place: Thagnag to Dzonglha (4830m) via Cho La Pass (5420m)
itinerary.description: Challenging 7-hour day crossing Cho La Pass. Glacier travel with crampons. Spectacular views.

itinerary.place: Lobuche to Gorakshep (5170m) to Everest Base Camp (5364m)
itinerary.description: Trek 7-8 hours round trip to Everest Base Camp. Return to Gorakshep.

itinerary.place: Gorakshep to Kala Patthar (5545m) to Pangboche (3990m)
itinerary.description: Early morning hike to Kala Patthar for sunrise over Everest. Long descent to Pangboche.

treks.name: Lobuche Peak Trek
treks.permit_details: Lobuche Peak Climbing Permit (NMA): Spring USD 125, Autumn USD 125, Winter USD 70; Sagarmatha National Park Permit NPR 3000; Khumbu Pasang Lhamu Municipality Permit NPR 2000; TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Sherpa mountaineering heritage, named after Lobuche village at base of peak
treks.description: Lobuche Peak Climbing is an exciting and technically demanding adventure in the Everest region, combining trekking through famous Sherpa villages with challenging mountaineering on Lobuche East (6,145m). The journey follows the classic Everest Base Camp trail through Namche Bazaar and Tengboche with spectacular views of Everest, Lhotse, and Ama Dablam. The climb involves navigating steep rock, snow, and ice sections with fixed ropes, requiring previous high-altitude experience and excellent physical condition. The summit offers 360-degree panoramic views over the surrounding peaks and valleys, making it a rewarding challenge for experienced climbers seeking a technical Himalayan peak.
treks.short_description: Technical mountaineering on Lobuche East (6,145m) with stunning Everest views and challenging steep terrain.

itinerary.description: 40-minute flight to Lukla. Trek 3-4 hours to Phakding.
Hike to Everest View Hotel for acclimatization.
Short trek to base camp. Camping.
Trek 3-4 hours to high camp. Camping.

itinerary.place: Summit Lobuche East (6145m) and return to Base Camp
itinerary.description: Early morning start for summit. Technical climbing on steep rock, snow, and ice with fixed ropes. Spectacular views. Descent to base camp.
Buffer day in case of bad weather.

treks.name: Pikey Peak Trek
treks.permit_details: TIMS Card NPR 2000, Local Municipality Permits NPR 1000-1500
treks.cultural_significance: Lower Sherpa region, Sir Edmund Hillary reportedly called this the finest Everest view, Buddhist monasteries
treks.description: Pikey Peak Trek offers one of the best panoramic views of the Himalayan range, including Mount Everest, Lhotse, Makalu, Kanchenjunga, and Ama Dablam. Sir Edmund Hillary himself reportedly described the Everest view from Pikey Peak as the finest he had ever seen [citation:7]. This relatively easy trek in the lower Solu region passes through beautiful rhododendron forests, traditional Sherpa villages, and ancient Buddhist monasteries. The summit at 4,065m provides a spectacular 360-degree panorama, making it perfect for those wanting incredible mountain views without the extreme altitude and crowds of the main Everest trails. The trail is especially stunning in April and May when rhododendrons are in full bloom, creating natural 'tunnels' of pink, white, and light purple flowers.
treks.short_description: Easy trek to Sir Edmund Hillary's favorite Everest viewpoint with 360° panoramas, rhododendron forests, and authentic Sherpa villages.

itinerary.description: Scenic 6-7 hour drive from Kathmandu to Dhap through beautiful countryside. Overnight at teahouse.
Trek 5-6 hours through pristine rhododendron forests. First mountain views. Overnight at teahouse.

itinerary.place: Jhapre to Pikey Peak Base Camp (3640m)
itinerary.description: Trek 5-6 hours through beautiful forests and meadows with increasingly dramatic mountain views. Overnight at teahouse.

itinerary.place: Pikey Peak Base Camp to Pikey Peak Summit (4065m) to Loting (3170m)
itinerary.description: Early morning start for summit to witness spectacular sunrise over Everest, Makalu, Kanchenjunga, and the entire Himalayan arc. Descend to Loting. Overnight at teahouse.
Trek 5-6 hours through traditional Sherpa villages and monasteries. Overnight at teahouse.
Explore Phaplu village, visit local monasteries, or relax. Optional flight back to Kathmandu available.
Scenic 30-minute flight from Phaplu airstrip to Kathmandu (or drive). Free afternoon.
Transfer to airport for international departure.

treks.name: Nar Phu Valley Trek
treks.permit_details: Nar Phu Restricted Area Permit USD 100/week (Sept-Nov) or USD 75/week (Dec-Aug), Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000 [citation:1]
treks.cultural_significance: Isolated Tibetan Buddhist villages with medieval culture, opened to trekkers in 2003, ancient monasteries, restricted area [citation:1]
treks.description: The Nar Phu Valley Trek is a journey into one of Nepal's most remote and culturally preserved regions, opened to trekkers only in 2003. Located north of the Annapurna Circuit, these two secluded valleys are rich in Tibetan culture and surrounded by towering peaks including Kangaru Himal and Himlung Himal [citation:1]. The trek passes through narrow gorges, remote villages, yak pastures, and crosses the challenging Kang La Pass (5,320m) that connects Nar Valley to the Annapurna Circuit trail. The ancient villages of Nar and Phu offer a rare glimpse into a way of life unchanged for centuries, with their ancient monasteries, chortens, and mani walls. This restricted area trek offers a true wilderness experience combined with profound cultural immersion.
treks.short_description: Remote restricted area trek to medieval Tibetan villages with ancient monasteries, dramatic canyons, and the challenging Kang La Pass.

itinerary.description: Scenic 8-9 hour drive from Kathmandu to Koto via Besisahar and Dharapani. Overnight at teahouse.
Trek 5-6 hours along Nar Khola, crossing pine forests and waterfalls. Enter Nar Phu restricted area.
Trek 6-7 hours through desert-like terrain and dramatic canyons to reach ancient Phu village [citation:1].
Explore Tashi Lhakhang Monastery, hike to nearby viewpoints, experience medieval Tibetan Buddhist culture.
Descend to the valley junction and overnight at Nar Phedi monastery area.
Hike uphill to reach Nar village, rich in Tibetan Buddhist culture with ancient monasteries [citation:1].
Explore Nar village and its monasteries. Acclimatization and cultural immersion.

itinerary.place: Nar to Ngawal (3660m) via Kang La Pass (5320m)
itinerary.description: Early morning start to cross challenging Kang La Pass with majestic mountain views. Descent to Ngawal on Annapurna Circuit [citation:1].
Trek down to Pisang along Annapurna Circuit trail.

treks.name: Tilicho Lake Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000 [citation:2]
treks.cultural_significance: Hindu pilgrimage site mentioned in Ramayana, sacred to both Hindus and Buddhists, ancient trade routes [citation:2]
treks.description: Tilicho Lake, situated at 4,919 meters (16,138 ft) in the Manang District, is recognized as one of the highest lakes in the world [citation:2]. This stunning turquoise body of water is of glacial origin, fed by meltwater from surrounding peaks including Tilicho Peak (7,134m) and the northern slopes of the Annapurna range. Mentioned in the ancient Hindu epic Ramayana, the lake holds deep spiritual significance for both Hindus and Buddhists. The trek can be done as a side trip from the Annapurna Circuit or as a standalone journey, offering spectacular mountain scenery and a true high-altitude adventure. The final approach involves navigating a challenging landslide zone with exposed cliffs, adding to its reputation as a serious trekking destination [citation:2].
treks.short_description: Challenging trek to one of the world's highest lakes at 4,919m, sacred in Hindu mythology with stunning turquoise waters.

itinerary.description: Trek to Manang, the district headquarters.
Rest and acclimatization. Hike to Gangapurna Lake.
Trek to Khangsar, the last permanent settlement before the lake [citation:2].
Trek to base camp area with basic lodges.

itinerary.place: Tilicho Base Camp to Tilicho Lake (4919m) and return
itinerary.description: Early morning start for the challenging final ascent through landslide zone [citation:2]. The trail climbs steeply with nearly 800m gain over 4-5km. Stunning turquoise lake views. Return to base camp.

itinerary.place: Manang to Jomsom via Thorong La (optional)
itinerary.description: Option to cross Thorong La Pass (5416m) to Jomsom.

treks.name: Khopra Ridge Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000 [citation:3]
treks.cultural_significance: Community-based tourism initiative, Magar villages, sustainable tourism supporting local schools and cottage industries [citation:3]
treks.description: The Khopra Ridge Trek is one of the least-visited off-the-beaten-path treks in the Annapurna region, offering spectacular views of Dhaulagiri, Annapurna, Nilgiri, and Machhapuchhre peaks [citation:3]. This community-managed route features three different trekking options depending on where you start. From Khopra Ridge (3,660m), trekkers can take an optional challenging day hike to Khayer Lake (4,620m), a sacred lake ensconced deep into a hill just below the summit of Annapurna South, covering approximately 2 sq.km [citation:3]. The trek directly supports community development programs including yak rearing, cheese production, Nepali Lokta paper, plum jam, and handmade wool items through Corporate Social Responsibility initiatives [citation:3]. The route passes through ethnic Magar villages of Paudwar, Swanta, Nagi, and Narchyang.
treks.short_description: Community-based ridge trek with spectacular Dhaulagiri-Annapurna views, optional Khayer Lake hike, and authentic Magar village culture.

itinerary.description: Drive or fly to Pokhara. Overnight at hotel.
1.5 hour drive to Nayapul. Trek to Ulleri via Tikhedhunga, a small Magar village [citation:3].
Trek through rhododendron and oak forest to Ghorepani.

itinerary.place: Ghorepani to Poon Hill (3210m) to Swanta (2270m)
itinerary.description: Early morning hike to Poon Hill for sunrise. Trek to Swanta village at the lap of Annapurna South and Nilgiri [citation:3].
Trek to Chistibang (Dhankharka) with locally operated community lodge [citation:3].
Steady ascent through alpine meadows and pastures to Khopra Ridge with breathtaking panoramas [citation:3].

itinerary.place: Khopra Ridge to Khayer Lake (4620m) and return
itinerary.description: Challenging 10-11 hour round trip hike to sacred Khayer Lake. Packed lunch required [citation:3].
Entirely downhill trek to Paudwar, a Magar village with community cheese factory and local honey facility [citation:3].
Downhill walk to Tatopani with famous natural hot springs [citation:3].

treks.name: Mohare Danda Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000 [citation:4]
treks.cultural_significance: Community-based sustainable tourism initiative established in 2010 by Mahabir Pun, eco-friendly trekking, all lodges community-owned with solar energy and locally-sourced food [citation:4]
treks.description: The Mohare Danda Trek, also known as the Community Eco Lodge Trek, was established in 2010 by Mahabir Pun as part of an initiative to create economic opportunities in remote areas while preserving local culture and nature [citation:4]. This eco-friendly trek uses only community-owned lodges that utilize solar energy and serve locally-sourced food, ensuring direct benefits reach local Magar and Gurung villages. From the summit at 3,300m, trekkers enjoy spectacular 360-degree views of Dhaulagiri, Annapurna South, Nilgiri, Hiunchuli, Gangapurna, and Machhapuchhre. The trek is less crowded than nearby Ghorepani Poon Hill and offers a peaceful alternative with authentic cultural experiences. Many trekkers combine this with daily yoga and meditation practices, making it a holistic wellness journey [citation:4].
treks.short_description: Eco-friendly community trek with panoramic mountain views, sustainable lodges, and authentic Magar village culture.

itinerary.description: Drive or fly to Pokhara. Overnight at hotel.
Scenic drive to Galeshwor. Cross Kali Gandaki River suspension bridge.
Trek through orange-growing villages and Magar settlements.
Visit community-focused village of Nangi with eco-lodge.
Climb through rhododendron forests to the ridge top with spectacular sunrise/sunset views [citation:4].
Descend to Ghorepani through beautiful forests.

itinerary.place: Ghorepani to Poon Hill to Nayapul to Pokhara
itinerary.description: Early morning hike to Poon Hill for sunrise, then descent to Nayapul and drive to Pokhara.

treks.name: Royal Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000 (approx. USD 10) [citation:5]
treks.cultural_significance: Named after Prince Charles who trekked this route in 1980 with 90 guests; footsteps of British royalty and Rolling Stones vocalist Mich Zaggar [citation:5]
treks.description: The Royal Trek earned its name from Prince Charles who first trekked this route in 1980 with 90 guests, and has also been trekked by Rolling Stones vocalist Mich Zaggar [citation:5]. This easy and short trek offers beautiful views of the Annapurna and Dhaulagiri ranges without requiring mountaineering experience, making it accessible for all ages. The trail passes through picturesque Gurung villages, terraced farmlands, and rhododendron forests, offering authentic cultural experiences and warm hospitality. The trek begins from Kalikasthan after a short drive from Pokhara and ends at Begnas Lake, the eighth largest lake of Pokhara, where trekkers can enjoy boating in freshwater [citation:5]. This trek is ideal for those with limited time or seeking a peaceful, less-crowded alternative in the Annapurna region.
treks.short_description: Easy, scenic trek through Gurung villages named after Prince Charles' 1980 visit, with stunning mountain views and lakeside finale.

itinerary.description: Drive 6-7 hours to Pokhara. Overnight at 3-star hotel.
1.5 hour drive to Bijayakhola. Trek through paddy fields and cross small river to Kalikasthan. Overnight at teahouse [citation:5].
Trek via Thulokot observing Gurung community. Walk through rhododendron forests with massif Annapurna range views [citation:5].
Trek to Chisapani with excellent views of Dhaulagiri and Annapurna ranges [citation:5].

itinerary.place: Chisapani to Begnas Lake to Pokhara
itinerary.description: Trek to Begnas Lake, boating in freshwater, then drive back to Pokhara.

treks.name: Panchase Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000 [citation:6]
treks.cultural_significance: Sacred Hindu pilgrimage site, Gurung, Magar, and Brahmin communities, traditional villages, destination where youngest generation shows respect to ancestor's souls [citation:6]
treks.description: The Panchase Trek is a short and easy trek from Pokhara, known for its rich biodiversity and stunning mountain views. The Panchase valley is an enclosed place surrounded by Parbat on the west, Kaski on the east, and Syangjha on the south, offering spectacular views of the Himalayan peaks including Annapurna South, Hiunchuli, Machhapuchhre, Dhaulagiri, Lamjung Himal, and Manaslu [citation:6]. The area is a designated Important Bird Area with abundant bird life and beautiful rhododendron forests that bloom spectacularly in spring. The trek passes through traditional villages with diverse ethnic mixes including Brahmin, Magar, and Gurung communities, offering authentic cultural experiences. Panchase is also a destination for Hindu pilgrims, where you'll observe the youngest generation showing respect to ancestors' souls [citation:6]. This off-the-beaten-path trek is suitable for all ages and fitness levels.
treks.short_description: Short biodiversity trek from Pokhara with spectacular mountain panoramas, excellent bird watching, and authentic village culture.

itinerary.description: Drive or fly to Pokhara. Overnight at hotel.
Drive to Bhadaure. Trek to Panchase Bhanjyang.

itinerary.place: Panchase Bhanjyang to Panchase Hill (2500m) to Panchase Village
itinerary.description: Early morning sunrise hike to Panchase Hill for panoramic mountain views. Descend to Panchase village.

treks.name: Sikles Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000 [citation:7]
treks.cultural_significance: One of the largest Gurung villages in Nepal, traditional stone and wood architecture, community homestay program, Gurung Cultural Museum with artifacts and traditional tools [citation:7]
treks.description: Sikles Village, nestled in the Annapurna Conservation Area at 1,980m, is one of the largest and most beautiful Gurung villages in Nepal. The village features traditional Gurung houses made of stone and wood with slate roofs and intricately carved wooden doors and windows [citation:7]. The Sikles Eco Trail is a well-marked trekking route through terraced fields, rhododendron forests, and charming villages offering spectacular views of Annapurna II, Annapurna IV, and Lamjung Himal [citation:7]. Visitors can experience the Gurung Cultural Museum showcasing artifacts, clothing, and traditional tools, and explore Buddhist monasteries including the Kali Gandaki Gorge Monastery. The community homestay program allows trekkers to immerse themselves in daily village life, sharing meals and stories with local families. Spring transforms the hillsides with rhododendron blooms, while autumn offers clear mountain views [citation:7].
treks.short_description: Cultural trek to one of Nepal's largest Gurung villages with traditional architecture, community homestays, and stunning mountain views.

itinerary.description: Drive to Kalikasthan, then trek through terraced fields and forests to Sikles.
Visit Gurung Cultural Museum, explore traditional architecture, interact with locals, visit Buddhist monasteries.
Trek to roadhead, drive to Pokhara.

treks.name: Annapurna North Base Camp Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000 (individual) or NPR 1000 (group through agency) [citation:8]
treks.cultural_significance: Less-visited northern approach to Annapurna Base Camp, authentic Gurung villages without tourist influence, camping-based experience [citation:8]
treks.description: The Annapurna North Base Camp Trek is a quieter, less-visited alternative to the popular southern route, offering a more wilderness-oriented experience with camping as the principal accommodation option [citation:8]. The trek begins in Humkhola or Narchyang villages after a 5-7 hour journey from Pokhara, passing through virgin forests, rhododendron blooms, and authentic Gurung villages untouched by mass tourism. The route includes spectacular highlights like Panchakunda Lake, a revered spot with five crystal-clear lakes, and Gurase Danda with panoramic mountain views [citation:8]. The maximum altitude of 4,190m is suitable for trekkers with moderate fitness levels. The northern route is far less touristic, offering a more genuine cultural experience and the opportunity to experience Busket Mela if trekking during festival times. This trek retains its remote nature with encampments and fewer tourists compared to the increasingly crowded southern teahouse route [citation:8].
treks.short_description: Less-crowded northern approach to Annapurna Base Camp with pristine forests, Panchakunda Lake, and authentic Gurung village culture.

itinerary.description: 5-7 hour drive to trailhead villages. Overnight in Gurung village [citation:8].
Trek through pristine forests with rhododendron blooms.
Trek to the revered spot with five crystal-clear lakes [citation:8].

itinerary.place: Panchakunda Lake to Annapurna North Base Camp (4190m)
itinerary.description: Challenging ascent to base camp with magnificent mountain views.

itinerary.place: Annapurna North Base Camp Exploration Day
itinerary.description: Rest day, explore surrounding areas, photography.
Visit Gurase Danda viewpoint with 360-degree mountain panoramas [citation:8].
Drive to Pokhara, return to Kathmandu.

treks.name: Ghale Gaun Trek
treks.permit_details: Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000
treks.cultural_significance: Model Gurung village with award-winning homestay program, cultural museum showcasing traditional tools, attire, and lifestyle, authentic Gurung hospitality
treks.description: Ghale Gaun is a model Gurung village in the Lamjung district, renowned for its award-winning community homestay program and cultural preservation efforts. The village is named after the Ghale clan, one of the prominent Gurung sub-clans, and has become a showcase of traditional Gurung culture and hospitality. The trek offers spectacular views of the Annapurna II, Manaslu, and Lamjung Himal ranges while providing deep immersion into authentic village life. The Gurung Cultural Museum displays traditional tools, clothing, and household items that offer insights into the rich heritage of the Gurung people. Visitors stay with local families in traditional stone and wood houses, participate in daily activities, and enjoy home-cooked Gurung cuisine including local specialties. The village is known for its warm hospitality and has become a model for community-based tourism in Nepal.
treks.short_description: Cultural homestay trek to a model Gurung village with award-winning hospitality, cultural museum, and stunning mountain views.

itinerary.description: Drive 6-7 hours to Besisahar. Overnight at lodge.
Trek 4-5 hours through terraced fields and forests to Ghale Gaun. Welcome by host family.
Visit Gurung Cultural Museum, participate in daily village activities, cultural programs, short hikes.
Descend back to Besisahar. Overnight at lodge.

treks.name: Manaslu + Tsum Valley Combined Trek
treks.permit_details: Manaslu Restricted Area Permit (RAP): USD 100/week (Sept-Nov) or USD 75/week (Dec-Aug); Tsum Valley Permit: USD 40/week (Sept-Nov) or USD 30/week (Dec-Aug); Manaslu Conservation Area Permit (MCAP) NPR 3000; Annapurna Conservation Area Permit (ACAP) NPR 3000 [citation:1][citation:10]
attractions: Tsum Valley - Hidden Valley of Happiness
treks.cultural_significance: Sacred Tsum Valley opened to trekkers in 2008, ancient Tibetan Buddhist culture, 'Shyagya' tradition of non-violence (no killing of animals or insects) since 1920, recently codified as Tsum Shyagya Act, Buddhist Margi Tsum people, Milarepa meditation caves [citation:10]
treks.description: The Manaslu + Tsum Valley Combined Trek merges the high-altitude circuit around Mount Manaslu (8,163m), the world's eighth-highest peak, with the spiritual serenity of the Tsum Valley, a sacred Tibetan Buddhist enclave known as the 'Hidden Valley of Happiness' [citation:1]. Tsum Valley remained restricted to outsiders until 2008 and offers a glimpse into ancient Himalayan life with its serene gompas, mani walls, and untouched lifestyle [citation:1]. The valley's inhabitants follow the unique 'Shyagya' tradition of non-violence, established in 1920, which prohibits killing any animals or insects and has recently been codified as the Tsum Shyagya Act [citation:10]. The trek features ancient monasteries including Mu Gompa (3,700m) near the Tibetan border, Rachen Gompa (one of the region's largest nunneries), and the Piren Phu Cave with Milarepa's footprint [citation:1][citation:10]. After exploring Tsum, the route rejoins the Manaslu Circuit to cross the challenging Larkya La Pass (5,106m) with spectacular views of Manaslu, Ganesh Himal, and Himalchuli [citation:10]. This remote wilderness experience offers far fewer crowds than the Everest or Annapurna regions [citation:1].

itinerary.description: Scenic 7-9 hour drive from Kathmandu to Soti Khola [citation:1].
Trek through subtropical forests and villages along Budhi Gandaki River [citation:1].
Trek through terraced fields and forests [citation:1].
Enter the Tsum Valley trail junction [citation:1].
Enter Tsum Valley. Visit traditional stone houses and chortens [citation:1].
Trek through beautiful landscapes to main village [citation:1].

itinerary.place: Chhokangparo to Nile (3361m) or Mu Gompa (3700m)
itinerary.description: Trek to the largest monastery in Tsum Valley [citation:1][citation:10].
Explore Mu Gompa, Rachen Gompa, and Milarepa's Cave. Experience unique non-violent Shyagya culture [citation:10].
Exit Tsum Valley and rejoin Manaslu Circuit [citation:1].
Trek through forests with first views of Manaslu [citation:1].
Trek to the main Sherpa village below Manaslu [citation:1][citation:10].
Hike to Manaslu Base Camp (4800m) or Birendra Tal Lake. Visit Pungyen Gompa [citation:10].
Trek to Tibetan trading village of Samdo [citation:1][citation:10].
Trek towards Larkya La Pass. Prepare for the crossing [citation:1][citation:10].

itinerary.place: Dharamsala to Bimthang (3720m) via Larkya La Pass (5106m)
itinerary.description: Early morning start to cross Larkya La Pass. Spectacular views of Himlung Himal, Cheo Himal, and Annapurna range. Long descent to Bimthang [citation:1][citation:10].
Descend through rhododendron forests and Gurung villages [citation:1].
Final trekking day. Connect to Annapurna Circuit trail [citation:1].

treks.name: Rupina La Pass Trek
treks.permit_details: Tsum Nubri Rural Municipality Entrance Permit NPR 1000; Restricted Area Permit: USD 100/week (Sept-Nov) or USD 75/week (Dec-Aug) [citation:2]
treks.cultural_significance: Barpak is the ancient resident of Gurung Communities and epicenter of the catastrophic 2015 earthquake; remote Gurung villages with heartwarming hospitality [citation:2]
treks.description: The Rupina La Pass Trek is a less-crowded, challenging adventure in the Manaslu region, promising mind-blowing Himalayan views and pristine wilderness [citation:2]. This remote trek begins at Barpak village, the epicenter of the 2015 earthquake and an ancient Gurung settlement. Trekkers will ascend through Mamche Danda (3042m), Omche, Dalin Kharka, and Mircha (3368m) before reaching Rupina La Phedi (3800m). The highlight is crossing Rupina La Pass (4720m) followed by a descent to Boudha Himal Base Camp (3420m) with stunning views of massive glaciers from Rani and Gorkha Peaks [citation:2]. The trek includes 6-7 days of continuous tented camping with no human settlements, offering complete wilderness immersion. The route connects to the Manaslu-Tsum-Nubri Circuit, with an intersection point at Nyak (2340m) where trekkers can decide to proceed to Manaslu Circuit, Ganesh Himal Trek, Tsum Valley Trek, or return to Kathmandu [citation:2].

itinerary.description: Arrive in Kathmandu. Transfer to hotel.
Full day sightseeing tour in Kathmandu. Preparation for trek.
Drive 7-8 hours from Kathmandu to Barpak. 205km. Overnight at homestay [citation:2].
Trek 6-7 hours through Gurung villages. 15km. Camping.
Trek 5-6 hours to base of Rupina La Pass. 5km. Camping [citation:2].

itinerary.place: Rupina La Phedi to Boudha Himal Base Camp (3600m) via Rupina La Pass (4720m)
itinerary.description: Early morning start. Trek 8-9 hours crossing Rupina La Pass. Spectacular glacier and mountain views. Descent to Boudha Himal Base Camp. Camping [citation:2].

itinerary.place: Boudha Himal Base Camp to Chongchet (3210m)
itinerary.description: Trek 6 hours descending through alpine landscapes. 11km. Camping [citation:2].
Trek 5 hours descending to Nyak. 10km. Camping [citation:2].
Long 8-hour trek connecting to Manaslu Circuit trail. 20km. Teahouse [citation:2].
Drive 8-9 hours back to Kathmandu. 200km. Hotel [citation:2].
Free day for rest and souvenir shopping.
Transfer to airport for international departure.

treks.name: Ganesh Himal Trek
treks.permit_details: TIMS Card NPR 2000, Local Municipality Permit NPR 1000-1500, Manaslu Conservation Area Permit (if entering MCAP area) NPR 3000 [citation:4]
attractions: Sacred Lakes (Kalo Kunda, Seto Kunda, Ganesh Kunda)
treks.cultural_significance: Tamang, Gurung, Newar, Ghale, and Dalit communities; traditional mountain lifestyle; Lhosar festival celebrations
treks.description: The Ganesh Himal Trek explores the remote region surrounding Mount Ganesh Himal (7,422m), located between the Langtang and Manaslu regions. This off-the-beaten-path trek offers spectacular views of the Ganesh Himal range, including Ganesh I, II, III, IV, and VII peaks [citation:4]. The route passes through pristine forests of rhododendron, pine, oak, and bamboo rich in wildlife including musk deer, leopards, black bears, and red pandas. Trekkers experience authentic cultural immersion in traditional Tamang, Gurung, and Newar villages, with opportunities for homestays and participation in local festivals like Lhosar [citation:4]. Highlights include crossing Pangsang La Pass (3,842m) and Singla La Pass (4,250m) with panoramic views stretching from Manaslu to Annapurna, and visiting sacred lakes including Kalo Kunda, Seto Kunda, and Ganesh Kunda near glacier-fed sources [citation:4]. Natural hot springs at Tatopani/Baluwa provide perfect relaxation after days of trekking.

itinerary.description: Drive 7-8 hours from Kathmandu to Syabrubesi. Overnight at teahouse.
Trek 5-6 hours to beautiful Tamang village of Gatlang.
Trek through forests towards Ganesh Himal base area.

itinerary.place: Somdang to Pangsang Pass (3842m) to Tipling (1900m)
itinerary.description: Early morning pass crossing with spectacular mountain views. Descend to Tipling. Hot springs [citation:4].
Continue through valley with mountain views.

itinerary.place: Borang to Sing La Pass (4250m) to Ganesh Kunda area
itinerary.description: Cross highest pass. Visit sacred Ganesh Kunda lakes [citation:4].

treks.name: Ruby Valley Trek
treks.permit_details: TIMS Card NPR 2000 (USD 20), Langtang National Park Permit NPR 3000 (foreigners) or NPR 1000 (SAARC) [citation:4]
treks.cultural_significance: Named after the region's ruby deposits, traditional Tamang, Gurung, Newar, Ghale, and Dalit communities, Lhosar festival celebrations, authentic homestay experiences [citation:4]
treks.description: The Ruby Valley Trek traverses Nepal's lesser-known Ganesh Himal region, named after the region's ruby deposits and offering lush landscapes, traditional villages, and spectacular mountain views [citation:4]. Located approximately 89km north of Kathmandu, this hidden gem provides panoramic vistas of Ganesh Himal (7,422m), Langtang Lirung, Manaslu, Annapurna, Dorje Lakpa, and Machhapuchhre from passes like Pangsang La (3,842m) and Singla La (4,250m) [citation:4]. The trail winds through rhododendron, pine, oak, and bamboo forests rich in wildlife including musk deer, leopards, black bears, and red pandas. Trekkers experience authentic cultural immersion in untouched Tamang, Gurung, Newar, Ghale, and Dalit communities through homestays, shared meals, local crafts, and festivals such as Lhosar [citation:4]. Natural highlights include sacred lakes (Kalo Kunda, Seto Kunda, Ganesh Kunda), hot springs at Tatopani/Baluwa, and waterfalls like Ganga Jamuna and Parvati Kunda [citation:4]. The trek offers minimal tourism infrastructure with pristine nature and authentic community engagement, ideal for those seeking tranquility close to Kathmandu [citation:4].

itinerary.description: 7-9 hour scenic drive from Kathmandu to Syabrubesi. Overnight at teahouse [citation:4].
Trek through Tamang villages to Gatlang. Homestay experience [citation:4].
Trek through forests to Somdang, known for ruby deposits.

itinerary.place: Somdang to Pangsang La Pass (3842m) to Tipling (1900m)
itinerary.description: Early morning pass crossing with spectacular mountain views. Visit hot springs at Tatopani [citation:4].
Trek through traditional villages with rich cultural heritage.
Continue through valley with views of Ganesh Himal.

itinerary.place: Borang to Singla La Pass (4250m) to Ganesh Kunda area
itinerary.description: Cross highest pass. Visit sacred Ganesh Kunda, Kalo Kunda, and Seto Kunda lakes [citation:4].
Long descent to Arughat. Overnight at teahouse.

treks.name: Rara Lake Circuit
treks.permit_details: Rara National Park Permit NPR 3000 (foreigners), TIMS Card NPR 2000 [citation:5]
attractions: Rara Lake (Nepal's largest freshwater lake - 10.8 sq km)
treks.cultural_significance: Thakuri and Malla communities, traditional villages, one of Nepal's least crowded trekking routes offering serene wilderness experience [citation:5]
treks.description: The Rara Lake Circuit takes you to Nepal's largest and most pristine freshwater lake, a shimmering sapphire set amidst pine-covered hills and snow-capped peaks in the remote northwest of Nepal. Often described as Nepal's hidden gem, Rara Lake covers 10.8 square kilometers at 2,990m and is the centerpiece of Rara National Park [citation:5]. Far from the popular trekking routes, this trek is ideal for those seeking solitude, silence, and raw Himalayan nature. The circuit includes a visit to Murma Top (3,710m) for panoramic views of the lake and surrounding peaks including Mt. Api and Saipal ranges [citation:5]. Trekkers walk through peaceful pine and rhododendron forests, encounter Thakuri and Malla communities in traditional villages, and enjoy birdwatching at the lake, a haven for migratory species. The trek is one of Nepal's least crowded routes, offering a serene experience surrounded by nature far from mainstream tourism [citation:5].

itinerary.description: Flight to Nepalgunj. Overnight at hotel [citation:5].
Morning flight to Jumla. Prepare for trek [citation:5].
Trek through pine forests and traditional villages. Camping/teahouse [citation:5].
Trek through traditional Thakuri villages. Teahouse [citation:5].
Arrive at stunning Rara Lake. Explore lakeshore. Lodge [citation:5].

treks.name: Khaptad Trek
treks.permit_details: Khaptad National Park Permit NPR 3000, TIMS Card NPR 2000 [citation:6]
treks.cultural_significance: Spiritual site of Khaptad Baba (renowned spiritual saint), Hindu pilgrimage site, traditional Tharu and Baitadi communities, one of Nepal's least crowded trekking routes [citation:6]
treks.description: The Khaptad Trek explores the pristine Khaptad National Park, a beautiful high-altitude plateau in far-western Nepal known for its rolling green hills, sacred lakes, and the ashram of the late spiritual saint Khaptad Baba [citation:6]. This unique trek combines pristine nature with spiritual tranquility, passing through forests of rhododendron, oak, and pine that are home to diverse wildlife. The maximum altitude is 3,276m, with low risk of altitude sickness making it accessible for trekkers of all levels [citation:6]. Trekkers experience a remote trekking route with panoramic views of snow-capped Himalayan peaks from Khaptad Danda, interact with local Tharu and Baitadi communities and experience their unique traditions, and walk through vibrant rhododendron forests, especially during the spring blooming season [citation:6]. The trek offers a chance to escape the hustle and bustle of city life and immerse yourself in nature's tranquility in one of Nepal's least crowded regions. The best seasons are spring (March-May) and autumn (September-November) with bearable temperatures of 10-20°C, clear skies, and trails in best condition [citation:6].

treks.name: Api Himal Trek
treks.permit_details: Api Nampa Conservation Area Permit, Darchula Restricted Area Permit (USD 90/week for Byas VDC area) [citation:7]
treks.cultural_significance: Indigenous communities, first ascent of Api in 1960 by Doshisha Alpine Society of Japan, first winter ascent December 24, 1983 by Polish climbers [citation:7]
treks.description: The Api Himal Trek is one of Nepal's most remote and challenging expeditions, leading to the base of Mount Api (7,132m), the highest peak in the Yoka Pahar Section of Gurans Himal in the extreme northwest corner of Nepal near the borders of India and Tibet [citation:7]. Although low in elevation among major mountains of Nepal, Api is exceptional in its rise above local terrain, dramatically towering over significantly lower valleys. The region was visited by Westerners in 1899, 1905, and 1936, but the peak was not attempted until 1953. The first ascent occurred in 1960 by the Doshisha Alpine Society of Japan via the Northwest Face route, and the first winter ascent was achieved on December 24, 1983 by Polish climbers Tadeusz Piotrowski and Andrzej Bielu [citation:7]. The trek takes about 20 days to reach Api Himal Base Camp (4,000m), with rest days for acclimatization and construction of different camps. Highlights include a 2-hour side trek to the holy Kali Dhunga Lake from base camp [citation:7]. This expedition is gaining popularity with increasing numbers of trekkers each year, offering true wilderness experience in one of Nepal's least-visited regions.

treks.name: Saipal Trek
treks.permit_details: Restricted Area Permit (RAP) for Bajhang District, Far-Western Region Trekking Permit (issued through registered agency), TIMS Card NPR 2000 [citation:8]
treks.cultural_significance: Indigenous Byansi and Darchula communities, Khas and Bhote ethnic groups, sacred mountain, extremely remote region with very few trekkers [citation:8]
treks.description: The Saipal Trek ventures into Nepal's isolated far-western region near the Tibetan border, home to the sacred Saipal Himal (7,031m). This off-the-beaten-path journey combines dense forests, glacial rivers, and interactions with indigenous Byansi and Darchula communities [citation:8]. The trek is fully camping-based with no teahouse facilities, requiring complete logistical support including tents, kitchen crew, food supplies, and dining/toilet tents [citation:8]. Porters and mules transport gear through remote terrain with no access to hospitals, making safety gear and first-aid support critical. Trekkers experience authentic cultural encounters with Khas, Bhote, and Byansi communities, observing their unique traditions and mountain lifestyle. The route offers majestic views of Mt. Saipal and surrounding peaks, untouched forests, alpine meadows, and glacial rivers. This trek requires prior multi-day trekking experience and excellent physical fitness due to high passes, remote terrain, and logistical complexities [citation:8]. It is ideal for adventurous trekkers seeking solitude and raw Himalayan beauty far from any tourist crowds.
